Oku_Text: Ghine lu ɛbaa ə Jesus Christ wɛ wán ə David.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah the son of David.

Oku_Text: David nè lu wán ə Ablaham.
English_NIV_Equivalent: David was the son of Abraham.

Oku_Text: Ablaham nè lu ba ə Ayzek.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Abraham was the father of Isaac.

Oku_Text: Ayzek nè luun ba ə Jakob.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Isaac the father of Jacob.

Oku_Text: Jakob nè lu ba ə Juda ghene ghone-no ə wen ɛluumene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.

Oku_Text: Juda nè lu ba ə Bɛlɛz ghene Zela ghɛ ɛybii Tamal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Judah the father of Perez and Zerah whose mother was Tamar.

Oku_Text: Bɛlɛz nè lu ba ə Hezlon wɛ nè luun ba ə Lam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Perez the father of Hezron and Hezron the father of Ram.

Oku_Text: Lam nè lu ba ə Amminadab wɛ ba ə Nahshon wɛ nè luun ba ə Salmon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Ram the father of Amminadab and Amminadab the father of Nahshon and Nahshon the father of Salmon.

Oku_Text: Salmon nè lu ba ə Boaz wɛ ɛybii Lahab.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Salmon the father of Boaz whose mother was Rahab.

Oku_Text: Boaz nè lu ba Obet wɛ ɛybii Lut.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Boaz the father of Obed whose mother was Ruth.

Oku_Text: Nɛ Obet lu ba ə Jesse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Obed the father of Jesse.

Oku_Text: Jesse nè lu ba əbfon wɛ David.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Jesse the father of King David.

Oku_Text: David nè lu ba ə Solomon wɛ no ə wen nè luun wiy Uliah se mbiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: David was the father of Solomon whose mother had been Uriah’s wife.

Oku_Text: Solomon nè lu ba ə Lehoboam wɛ ba Abija wɛ nè luun ba Asa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Solomon the father of Rehoboam and Rehoboam the father of Abijah and Abijah the father of Asa.

Oku_Text: Asa nè lu ba ə Jehoshafat wɛ ba ə Jeholam wɛ nè luun ba Uzzia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Asa the father of Jehoshaphat and Jehoshaphat the father of Jehoram and Jehoram the father of Uzzia.

Oku_Text: Uzzia nè lu ba ə Jotham wɛ ba Aghaz wɛ nè luun ba ə Hezekia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Uzzia the father of Jotham and Jotham the father of Ahaz and Ahaz the father of Hezekia.

Oku_Text: Hezekia nè lu ba ə Manasse wɛ ba Amos wɛ nè luun ba Josia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Hezekia the father of Manasse and Manasse the father of Amon and Amon the father of Josiah.

Oku_Text: Josia nè lu ba ə Jekonia ghene ghone-no ə wen ɛluumene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers.

Oku_Text: É nè bii ghene əbliy wɛ é nè koon ghele Islael ghene ndu è se chii ɛ Babilon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.

Oku_Text: Jekonia bii Shealtiel wɛ ba ə Zelubbabel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel.

Oku_Text: Zelubbabel nè lu ba Abiud wɛ ba Eliakim wɛ nè luun ba Azol.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Zerubbabel the father of Abihud and Abihud the father of Eliakim and Eliakim the father of Azor.

Oku_Text: Azol nè lu ba ə Zadok wɛ ba Achim wɛ nè luun ba Eliud.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Azor the father of Zadok and Zadok the father of Akim and Akim the father of Eliud.

Oku_Text: Eliud nè lu ba Eleaza wɛ ba ə Mattan wɛ nè luun ba ə Jakob.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Eliud the father of Eleazar and Eleazar the father of Matthan and Matthan the father of Jacob.

Oku_Text: Jakob nè lu ba ə Josɛf wɛ lum ə Mɛɛle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary.

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle wɛ əb nè biin Jesus wɛ é toŋten ge: Vi wɛ Feyine nè Yiosen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Mary was the mother of Jesus who is called the Messiah.

Oku_Text: Ghan jii yɛ ke nè ganen nɛ Mɛɛle geese bii Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is how the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about.

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle nêy yɛ lu wiy ə Josɛf.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph.

Oku_Text: É me keele ge Mɛɛle shi nè əblɛɛ əbtaawe nə Keyioy Kelanene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: She was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit.

Oku_Text: Josɛf nè lu wel əbjuŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Joseph her husband was faithful to the law.

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa nə Báàba Feyin nêy gwiy se wen ɛ jɛm è suuy ge: Josɛf ghàà yɛ fan se liɛ Mɛɛle se wɛ əbvii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: An angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said "Joseph do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife."

Oku_Text: Bek ge əb chia nè əblɛɛ kɛn əbtaawe nə Keyioy Kelanene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

Oku_Text: Əb nêy bii wán əbluumen é wè chio ɛyghel ə wen ge Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: She will give birth to a son and you are to give him the name Jesus.

Oku_Text: Bek ge əb nêy boyse ghel ə wen se ghene mbeese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Because he will save his people from their sins.

Oku_Text: Wándɛ ngon əblee nêy chia nè əblɛɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son.

Oku_Text: Feyine lu ghesen nè ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: God with us.

Oku_Text: Josɛf me yɛmen è nɛɛy kɛn ka nchiy-ndaa nə Báàba Feyin kè suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Joseph woke up he did what the angel of the Lord had commanded him.

Oku_Text: É nè bii Jesus ɛ ntɛke Betlehem ɛ ɛylak ə Judea.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea.

Oku_Text: Nè lu əbliy fô nɛ əbfon wɛ Helod sake ɛylake ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This was during the time of King Herod.

Oku_Text: Ghes kè yɛn fenjes ə wen ka fe yɛnsen ɛ ɛyyio è me lòò è se kɛŋe gwiye se kokse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: We saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him.

Oku_Text: Əbfon wɛ Helod yio kenəə ten ke jefse ɛytof ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When King Herod heard this he was disturbed.

Oku_Text: Wè wɛ Betlehem ɛ ntɛke Judea wè dì báà se lu kɛn fentɛktese ɛ məme ntɛke sé Juda.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But you Bethlehem in the land of Judah are by no means least among the rulers of Judah.

Oku_Text: Bek ge əbfon wɛ əb náà yɛ sake ghel ə ghome ghɛ Islael é nêy fəy ɛ ghɛne məm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For out of you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn ndu ɛ Betlehem è loote kɛŋ ghaa wan ten luun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Go and search carefully for the child.

Oku_Text: Fe me se bane nduu ɛ ghene mbiy dine jii se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The star they had seen when it rose went ahead of them.

Oku_Text: È ndu ləysen bene fɛ wan vi nè luun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It stopped over the place where the child was.

Oku_Text: Ghene yɛn wán vi ghene no ə wene Mɛɛle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They saw the child with his mother Mary.

Oku_Text: È me təm ɛləy ɛ wen ɛyshiɛ è fo ɛykokse se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They bowed down and worshiped him.

Oku_Text: Ghene fô ɛykokse di è me dise əbbuam ə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They opened their treasures and presented him with gifts.

Oku_Text: Tefo tine nè lu: Ɛytian nè kɛlɛye gese nè keyióséne.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Gifts of gold frankincense and myrrh.

Oku_Text: Feyine suuy se ghene ɛ jɛm ge ghàà ghene kase ndu ghaa Helod luun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Having been warned in a dream not to go back to Herod.

Oku_Text: Loole è liɛ wán ghene no ə ten è nyiŋ è ndu nè ghene se Ejibt.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Get up take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt.

Oku_Text: Josɛf me loole kɛn nɛ ntəke è liɛ wán ghene no ə ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he got up took the child and his mother during the night.

Oku_Text: Mɛ nè jaŋ wán ə wom əb fəy se Ejibt.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Out of Egypt I called my son.

Oku_Text: Helod yuyte ghon ɛluumene ɛjim ghɛ é nè bɛɛy ghàà chin nguk sebaa ɛ Betlehem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Herod gave orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem who were two years old and under.

Oku_Text: É lu nɛ é yio ka fezəə gwiyen ɛ Lama.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A voice is heard in Ramah.

Oku_Text: Loole è liɛ wán ghene no ə wen è fiile se ɛylake Islael.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Get up take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel.

Oku_Text: Əb ndu dì è se chii ɛ ntɛke Nazalet.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he went and lived in a town called Nazareth.

Oku_Text: É nêy yɛ toŋte vi wɛ Feyine nè yiosen ge wele Nazalet.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He will be called a Nazarene.

Oku_Text: Joon Nfoo Moo ndu əbkwak ɛ Judea è se fekte se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn kubsen tetem ə tɛn è fiile se ɛybame mbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.

Oku_Text: Siyse jii se Báàba nɛyte kevəə jii se wen é ke yɛ nduu ɛ titiy!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.

Oku_Text: Ɛyyiɛ ə wene nè lu nguumese ghene fedioŋe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His food was locusts and wild honey.

Oku_Text: Ghene nè lô se taŋle ɛychio ə ghen ge ghene lu nɛ é nɛɛy mbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Confessing their sins they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ nɛɛy əbnəə əbjuŋe se din ge ghɛn kubsenmen tetem ə tɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance.

Oku_Text: Mɛ foo nɛ lu moo se ghen se din ge ghɛn kubsenmen tetem ə tɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I baptize you with water for repentance.

Oku_Text: Dî nɛ əblee nêy gwiy è yɛ foo nɛ lu Keyioy Kelanene gese nè əbvəse se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But after me comes one who will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.

Oku_Text: Jesus me lòò ɛ Galilee è gwiy ɛ jio Joldan è yɛn Joon ge əb fó moo se yi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.

Oku_Text: Jesus fii moo ɛ leghɛ è loo fəy se jio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As soon as Jesus was baptized he went up out of the water.

Oku_Text: Əb yɛn Keyioy ə Feyin nɛ ke səəke ka kenlal è guo ɛ wen əbghun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him.

Oku_Text: Vin lu wán ə wom wɛ mɛ koŋen nɛ mɛ gese saŋle naa se ŋaŋ kum wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.

Oku_Text: Keyioy ə Feyine me liɛ Jesus è ndu nè wen se əbkwak ge Satan momse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

Oku_Text: Satan gwiy è suuy se wen ge: Lòó è yɛ lu ge wɛ lu wán ə Feyin wè bense ɛtiy ghin se keban.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The tempter said "If you are the Son of God tell these stones to become bread."

Oku_Text: Wele báà se chii kɛn se əbfua-əbyiéne wele chii se giɛ sejim sɛ se fəyen ɛ Feyine əbchio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.

Oku_Text: Wè báà se lu se momse Báàba wɛ Feyin ə fiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not put the Lord your God to the test.

Oku_Text: Wè lu se yɛ kokse kɛn Báàba wɛ Feyin ə fiɛ gese fɛle kɛn se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.

Oku_Text: Satan me lòò è vəse Jesus se fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the devil left him.

Oku_Text: Nchiyse-ndaa nə Feyine gwiy è giamten wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Angels came and attended to him.

Oku_Text: Jesus nɛy yio ge é viimen Joon se nchak è me lòò è ndu ɛ Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison he withdrew to Galilee.

Oku_Text: Ghel ghɛ é chiin ɛ nfiine nêy yɛn nɛ ɛybane gwiye se ghene naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people living in darkness have seen a great light.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn kubsen tetem ə tɛn bek ge ɛysak əbfon ɛyyio shi ɛ keghoo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Ghɛn gwiy è loo é mɛ nɛɛy é ghɛn yɛ koo nɛ lu ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Come, follow me, Jesus said

Oku_Text: Ghene me vəse əmlam mə ghen kɛn ɛymok è se jumte nduu nè wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At once they left their nets and followed him.

Oku_Text: Jesus me lòò è se ghane Galilee əbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus went throughout Galilee.

Oku_Text: Dinte əbnəə se ghele ɛ ghene ngayse ɛyjem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Teaching in their synagogues.

Oku_Text: Fekte sakghɛ əbjuŋ wɛ kum ɛysak ə Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Proclaiming the good news of the kingdom.

Oku_Text: Gese chuke naa tenkii teyaf tejim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And healing every disease and sickness.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ é tanen se keyioy bek ge ɛysak əbfon ɛyyio lu yé ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are the poor in spirit for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ é diyen bek ge Feyine náà chəmsen ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are those who mourn for they will be comforted.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ é liɛn ngeŋse ghen ɛ ntiɛ bek ge ghene náà yiɛ ketuu ntiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are the meek for they will inherit the earth.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ yafen jeŋ se yɛ nɛɛye kenəə kɛ Feyine kɛŋen bek ge Feyine náà nɛɛy é ghene yɛ lu ɛkulen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness for they will be filled.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ é koosen ɛysen bek ge Feyine náà koosen ɛysen se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are the merciful for they will be shown mercy.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ tetem ə ghene laanen bek ge ghene náà yɛn Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are the pure in heart for they will see God.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se ghel ghɛ é nɛɛyen ge ghele yɛ chii nè əmnyiaŋe bek ge é náà yɛ toŋte ghene ge ghon ə Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed are the peacemakers for they will be called children of God.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn lu ɛ mbi fɛn ka fengwaŋe lu ɛ mbas.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You are the salt of the earth.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn lu ɛybane yé mbii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You are the light of the world.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ nɛɛy əbnəə əbjuŋe kɛn ɛ jii nê ghan ka ghele yɛ yɛn me kokse Ba ə wɛn wɛ əb luun ɛ ɛyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the same way let your light shine before others that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

Oku_Text: Mɛ báà gwiy se buuse əbghene mɛ gwiy se nɛɛy ge əb kwɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have not come to abolish the law or the prophets but to fulfill them.

Oku_Text: Wel yafse ɛytem se wan-no ə wen fene é sak wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Anyone who is angry with a brother or sister will be subject to judgment.

Oku_Text: Loo lu ɛyshiɛ ə yiɛ yɛ əbkoy əbtaa yɛ lobte wè ge wè nɛɛy mbee wè chok è maay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If your right eye causes you to stumble gouge it out and throw it away.

Oku_Text: Wele yɛ beeme kenəə yɛ lu nɛ əb bééme beemen è tun yɛ lu nɛ əb tun tunen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: All you need to say is simply ‘Yes’ or ‘No’.

Oku_Text: Mɛ me suuye se ghɛn ge: Wel nɛɛy mbee se wè ghàà wè seese nè mbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I tell you do not resist an evil person.

Oku_Text: Wele tɛl wè ɛ kegiɛke kɛ əbkoy əbtaa wè bense kelee əb məyse è tɛl.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If anyone slaps you on the right cheek turn to them the other cheek also.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ koŋe ghele əbbaanese nə ghɛn jeme se ghel ghɛ é nəksɛn ghɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ lu se yɛ nɛɛy əbnəə əbjim ɛ titiy kɛn ka Ba ə wɛn wɛ əb luun ɛ ɛyyio nɛɛyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Be perfect therefore as your heavenly Father is perfect.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ toknen se yɛ loo nɛɛy kenəə kejuŋe ghɛn yɛ din ge ghele yɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them.

Oku_Text: Ba ə wɛs wɛ əb luun ɛ ɛyyio nɛɛy é ghele yɛ ngvəmle ɛyghel ə yiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Our Father in heaven hallowed be your name.

Oku_Text: Nɛɛy é ɛysak ə yiɛ gwiy se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your kingdom come.

Oku_Text: É ghele yɛ nɛɛy kenəə kɛ wè kɛŋen ge é yɛ nɛɛy ɛ ntiɛ kɛn ka ke luun ɛ ɛyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

Oku_Text: Fó əbfua-əbyiéne se ghɛs lan vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Give us today our daily bread.

Oku_Text: È lɛyse mbee sɛs ka ghɛs lɛyse se ghel ghɛ é jasen se ghɛs.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And forgive us our debts as we also have forgiven our debtors.

Oku_Text: Nɛɛy é ghɛs jia lii se ɛymomse tɛɛke ghɛs se ɛyfime.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And lead us not into temptation but deliver us from the evil one.

Oku_Text: Wè yɛ loo bam əbfua-əbyiéne wè ko suu ɛykek ə yiɛ è sol nè keyióséne.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when you fast put oil on your head and wash your face.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ baate ɛykɛŋ ə yɛn ləme nɛ lu ɛyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven.

Oku_Text: Ke lu dî bek ge ɛykɛŋ ə wele lòó lu ghááy ɛytem ə wene yɛ lu fô.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For where your treasure is there your heart will be also.

Oku_Text: Lòó ghɛn jia yɛ fɛl se Feyine gese fɛle se əbkaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You cannot serve both God and money.

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghɛn yɛ fof kum kefaa kɛ yiɛ gese nè kɛ nəə se geese yɛ chii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not worry about your life what you will eat or drink.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn liɛ nfek se əmnənmə əm sèè jia lem əbfua-əbyiéne kaa wiy ki wiy techia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Look at the birds of the air they do not sow or reap or store away in barns.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛn əbgii ka əb lòó əb yɛ buse jof.
English_NIV_Equivalent: See how the flowers of the field grow.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ luŋ nɛ lu se kiile kelen ɛ məme ɛysak nə Feyine gese nɛɛye ka əb kɛŋen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness.

Oku_Text: É əb náà fo əbfua nê vin se ghɛn əbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And all these things will be given to you as well.

Oku_Text: Ke me se lu ge ghàà ghɛn yɛ gel kum likebvəchi bek ge likebvəchi lu nè ngekse wene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow for tomorrow will worry about itself.

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghɛn yɛ ghame ghel fɛ Feyine náà gham ghɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not judge, or you too will be judged.

Oku_Text: Əbfek wɛ wè feken kefaa so se geese fo se wel lu əbghene wɛ Feyine náà fek kiɛ so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.

Oku_Text: Lu bek ghɛ wɛ wè kiyen ɛykfəke fekak yɛ ey luun ɛ wan-no ə viɛ ɛyshiɛ dî yene wiy kefuŋ kɛ ke luun ɛ yiɛ?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?

Oku_Text: Wel əbbuam chia mbiy è fəyse kefuŋ kɛ ke luun ɛ wè ɛyshiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You hypocrite

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ bif kɛn bifen é Feyine fó se ghɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.

Oku_Text: Bek ge naa ndɛ bife é Feyine fó se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks

Oku_Text: É Ba ə wɛn wɛ əb luun ɛ ɛyyio jia fo əbfua əbjuŋe se ghel ghɛ é bifen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: How much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him!

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ nɛɛye kenəə se ghele nɛ lu kɛ ghɛne koŋen ge ghene yɛ nɛɛy se ghɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ lii jii əbchio ndaa əblaytene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Enter through the narrow gate.

Oku_Text: Bek ge jii yɛ ey nduun ɛ kemboo əbvəse bonene nɛ əbchio ndaa ghake.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction.

Oku_Text: Dî nɛ əbchio ndaa wɛ əb liin nɛ lu ɛychii yɛ ey jia yî mɛye laytee nɛ jii ten taa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn toknen kum nfektese əbbuam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Watch out for false prophets.

Oku_Text: Fekak fejuŋe sɛɛ koom əmtam əmjuŋ nɛ fekak febéé koom əmtam əmbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Likewise every good tree bears good fruit but a bad tree bears bad fruit.

Oku_Text: Bek ndee ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: By their fruit you will recognize them.

Oku_Text: Lù kɛn wel vi wɛ əb nɛɛyen kenəə kɛ Ba ə wom wɛ əb luun ɛ ɛyyio kɛŋen ge əb nɛɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter the kingdom of heaven.

Oku_Text: Naa ndɛ wɛ əb yion giɛ ə som shin è yɛ nɛɛye ka mɛ suuyen nêy yɛ lu ka wel əbtofen wɛ əb nè baklen ndaa ɛ wen ɛ ketiɛ kɛ luun ɛyghok.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.

Oku_Text: Wel wɛ əb yion giɛ som shin nɛɛye wiy ka mɛ suuyen nêy yɛ lu ka kengoo kɛ ke nè baklen ndaa ə wen ɛ kegvəmte əmntie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.

Oku_Text: Əb nè dinte əbnəə se ghene ka wel wɛ əb kiin əbtaawe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He taught as one who had authority.

Oku_Text: Báàba loo lu nɛ wè koŋe wè nɛɛy é mɛ bonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.

Oku_Text: Jesus me nase keghoo è kum wen so è suuy ge: Mɛ koŋe; bonene!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing

Oku_Text: Báàba wel ɛyfɛl nə mɛ nyiŋ ɛ ndaa nè ɛybee əbghun ɛylee kuomen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Lord, he said

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Mɛ nêy gwiy è chuk wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to him

Oku_Text: Suŋnen kɛn suŋnen é wel ɛyfɛl nə mɛ me bonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But just say the word, and my servant will be healed.

Oku_Text: Yɛɛ kfəəye ke gan kɛn ka wè beemen se mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Go! Let it be done just as you believed it would.

Oku_Text: Jesus kum keghoo ə wen kenchɛme ké tene me takle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He touched her hand and the fever left her.

Oku_Text: Əb nè fii gon ə sɛs è gese buuse teyaf ə sɛs.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He took up our infirmities and bore our diseases.

Oku_Text: Wanwel ki wiy kelik kɛ əb nyiŋse ketuu ɛ ten è zəəle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Son of Man has no place to lay his head.

Oku_Text: Gwiy é lòò é ghii dii ghɛ é lɛyenmen yɛ gvəəme ghel ghɛ kuon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.

Oku_Text: Jesus ghene ghon-ɛyfɛl nə wene me lii se kenək è loo ndu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he got into the boat and his disciples followed him.

Oku_Text: Əb kɛy mfəme yi gese nè jio yi əblike se lu nyiaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He got up and rebuked the winds and the waves

Oku_Text: Vin lu ɛynkii wel əbkɛ wɛ mfəme gese nè jio yionen se wen?
English_NIV_Equivalent: What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the waves obey him!

Oku_Text: Wan ə Feyin wè kɛŋe se nɛɛy ghɛ se ghɛs?
English_NIV_Equivalent: What do you want with us, Son of God? they shouted.

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghɛn ndu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Go! he said to them.

Oku_Text: Jesus nɛy lii se kenək è se kase diaŋe se wen ə ntɛk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus stepped into a boat crossed over and came to his own town.

Oku_Text: Wan ə wom ghal ɛyteme! Mɛ lɛysemen mbee ə shiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn ki tekwaate tebee ɛ ghɛne tetem bek ghɛ?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Why do you entertain evil thoughts in your hearts?

Oku_Text: Wanwel ki əbtaawe ɛ ketuu ntiɛ se lɛyse mbeese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.

Oku_Text: Loole è liɛ əbkun ə viɛ è yɛ kfəəy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Get up, take your mat and go home.

Oku_Text: Jesus nɛy lòò se fô è loo ndu è yɛn wel ɛyfii shile... Matio è suuy se wen ge: Gwiy é loo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As Jesus went on from there he saw a man named Matthew... "Follow me" he told him.

Oku_Text: Mɛ báà gwiy se tiyse ghel ɛjuŋe mɛ gwiy se tiyse nɛ lu ghel ɛbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.

Oku_Text: Lòó wel jia bal ndəy sɛməə nè kensase ndəy sefɛɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment.

Oku_Text: Ghɛɛ ghɛɛ ka lòó é jia kuune əmnduk əmbol ɛ kefaa əmnduk keməə.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Neither do people pour new wine into old wineskins.

Oku_Text: Jesus me loole ghene wel vi se nduu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus got up and went with him.

Oku_Text: Wan ə wom ghal ɛyteme. Ɛytem yɛ wɛ foon se Feyine nɛɛymen wè bónen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Take heart, daughter, he said

Oku_Text: Jesus me kum ɛshiɛ ə ghen è suuy ge: Ke gan yi kɛn ka ghɛn foon ɛytem se mɛ!
English_NIV_Equivalent: He touched their eyes and said "According to your faith let it be done to you."

Oku_Text: Ngose wel se lu nè əbchìô əbyúméné suuye ge: Ɛynkii kenəə ghan nè bɛɛy ghàà yi gan ɛ Islael.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The crowd was amazed and said "Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel."

Oku_Text: Ɛsaŋe biymen se ɛydio ghel ɛdənene se laaye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn lon se Bay wɛ ba ɛylem ge əb tinse ghel ɛydən ɛsaŋ nə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.

Oku_Text: Jesus nɛy tooy ghon-ɛyfɛl nə wen ghɛ njio-sebaa ɛyvəm è fo əbtaawe se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus called his twelve disciples to him and gave them authority.

Oku_Text: Ghɛne loo ndu ghɛn yɛ fekte se ghele ge: Ɛysak ə Feyine shi ɛ nchiɛ ɛ nchiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As you go, proclaim this message: ‘The kingdom of heaven has come near.’

Oku_Text: Mɛ fó əbtaawe vin se ghɛn se lólò ghɛn yɛ chuke ghel so se lólò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Freely you have received; freely give.

Oku_Text: Mɛ tume ghɛn ka bvəyse njiɛ ɛ məme baase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I am sending you out like sheep among wolves.

Oku_Text: Ke me se lu ge ghɛn yɛ feesen ka yiose dì gese loole ge wèè ka əbnlal dì.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore be as shrewd as snakes and as innocent as doves.

Oku_Text: Ghàà wel yuy wel... Ghàà ghɛn yɛ fane ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul.

Oku_Text: Ghɛn yɛ fan nɛ lu Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Rather, be afraid of the One who can destroy both soul and body in hell.

Oku_Text: Naa ndɛ wɛ əb beemen ɛ ɛyshiɛ nə ghele ge yi lu wel ə wom lu nɛ Wanwel yî beeme wen sɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever acknowledges me before others, I will also acknowledge before my Father in heaven.

Oku_Text: Dî nɛ naa ndɛ wɛ əb maan mɛ ɛyshiɛ nə ghele lu nɛ mɛ yî maa sɛy wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But whoever disowns me before others, I will disown before my Father in heaven.

Oku_Text: Mɛ báà gwiy se nɛɛy é ghele yɛ chii nè əmnyiaŋe mɛ gwiy nè ɛyvəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I did not come to bring peace, but a sword.

Oku_Text: Naa ndɛ wɛ əb jia liɛ ɛyvəm ə wen è jumte mɛ wel vi báà se lu kekwɛnten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever does not take up their cross and follow me is not worthy of me.

Oku_Text: Naa ndɛ wɛ əb nɛɛyen ge é boyse ɛychii ə wen nêy lɛyse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever finds their life will lose it.

Oku_Text: Dî nɛ naa ndɛ wɛ əb lɛysen ɛychii ə wen bek mɛ nêy yɛn ɛychii ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And whoever loses their life for my sake will find it.

Oku_Text: Naa ndɛ wɛ əb fiisen ghɛn lu nɛ əb fiisen mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me.

Oku_Text: Jesus nεy mεyse se dinte əbnəə wε əb nè kεηen ge ghon-εyfεl nə wen ghε njio-sebaa εyvəm yε nεεy è me lòò se fo è se ghane ε ntεkse dinte gese fekte kenəə ə Feyin se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in the towns of Galilee.

Oku_Text: Nè lu əbliy fô nε Joon Nfoo Moo lu ε nchak. Əb yio əbnəə wε Jesus wε mboyse nè nεεyen è me tum ghon-εyfεl nə wen
English_NIV_Equivalent: When John, who was in prison, heard about the deeds of the Messiah, he sent his disciples

Oku_Text: é bif se wen ge: Lu wè mboyse wε əb nêy se gwiy moo ghes yε chiyte εychin-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: to ask him, 'Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghεn fiile è ndu è suuy se Joon əbnəə wε ghεne yεnen gese yio;
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, 'Go back and report to John what you hear and see:'

Oku_Text: əmnfεfe se yεne əblik, nè εkεblene jεle, nè ghε é yafen nè kensaasee bonene, nè əbchichii yio əbnəə,
English_NIV_Equivalent: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those who have leprosy are cleansed, the deaf hear,

Oku_Text: nè ghel ghε é kuonmen kase loole se εykuo nè ghel ghε nchεkε nchεkε kiile sakghε əbjuη ka é fekten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the dead are raised, and the good news is proclaimed to the poor.

Oku_Text: Nε ke lu kejuη se vi di wε əb báà se maanen mε.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed is anyone who does not stumble on account of me.

Oku_Text: Ghεn nè ndu ε əbkwak əbyume se yεne ghε?
English_NIV_Equivalent: What did you go out into the wilderness to see?

Oku_Text: Mε suuy əbluuy ge ghεn nè yεn wel nε əb chia wele ntum nə Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, yes, and more than a prophet.

Oku_Text: Lu Joon nê vin wε é nyaken ε ηwaale-Feyin kum wen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the one about whom it is written:

Oku_Text: Feyine nè suuy ge mε nêy tum wele ntum nə mε ε wè mbiy é əb siyse jii ə yiε.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.

Oku_Text: Mε suuy əbluuy se ghεn ge se ghel ghε é nè shiin ε ketuu ntiε fεn lu nε wele báà se lu wε əb chian Joon Nfoo Moo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has not risen anyone greater than John the Baptist;

Oku_Text: Dî nε wel wε əb layten è ləysen ε məme εysak nə əbfon εyyio chia Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.

Oku_Text: Naa ndε wε əb kiin əbtuule se yio əb yio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever has ears, let them hear.

Oku_Text: Dî nε lòò é keele ge wel ki εytof ə Feyin se kenəə kε əb nεεyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But wisdom is proved right by her deeds.

Oku_Text: Ghεn gwiy se mε ghεn εjim ghε é bεεymen kediile ke se chia ghεn é mε giamten ghεn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.

Oku_Text: Ghεn tuη kembuη ə kom è yε yio se mε bek ge mε lu ge wèè, giεke nè ghele giεken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart,

Oku_Text: Ghεn gwiy se mε è kiile εyzəəte ε ghεne tetem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and you will find rest for your souls.

Oku_Text: Kembuη ə kome bonene nε kebεεy ə kome yaηsee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.

Oku_Text: Ko se lu ε keleη əbliy fo Jesus se chia εylem εsaη εylee ε kechii εybam
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: nè jeη yafe ghon-εyfεl nə wen, ghene me se kole əbtuu εsaη, fiksee, kfəle εshiε ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His disciples were hungry and began to pick some heads of grain and eat them.

Oku_Text: Ghele kenone Falisee yεn kenəə ten, è suuy se Jesus ge: Yεn-a!
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, 'Look!'

Oku_Text: Ghon-εyfεl nə wè nεεy kenəə kε εysake báà beeme ge é nεεy ε kechii εybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your disciples are doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: ηwaale-Feyine suuye ge: “Mε koηe ge wel yε chame wel əblee, chia nyam se yε taηle mε so.”
English_NIV_Equivalent: For I tell you: 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice.'

Oku_Text: Ke lu dî bek ge Wanwel lu Báàba ə kechii εybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: Jesus nεy lòò se fo è ndu se ngay εyjem yε ntεk fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Going on from that place, he went into their synagogue,

Oku_Text: Nè lu dî nε wel əblee lu fo nε keghoo ə wene kuomen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and a man with a shriveled hand was there.

Oku_Text: Ke lε lu ε titiy ge wel chuk wel ε kechii εybam-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?

Oku_Text: Wele mesoη lε báà se chia bvəy njiε-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep!

Oku_Text: Εysake dì dine ge ke jofe se nεεyε njuη ε kechii εybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: Əb nate ke me kase bonen è me se lu kεn ka kelee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He stretched it out and it was completely restored, just as sound as the other.

Oku_Text: Vin lu wel εyfεl nə mε wε mε nè choken, vi wε mε koηen, nε gese lu wen wε mε saηlen bek wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Here is my servant whom I have chosen, the one I love, in whom I delight;

Oku_Text: Mε nêy tinse Keyioy ə kom se wen é əb ye fekte se əbtume ka mε nêy sak ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I will put my Spirit on him, and he will proclaim justice to the nations.

Oku_Text: Satan lε lòò è yε jume Satan ke fene ke dine ge əb giabsenmen ngeη ə wen giabsen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?

Oku_Text: dì foo Keyioy ə Feyin əbtaawe ge mε yε jum εyfim so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I drive out demons,

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine lu se din ge εysak ə Feyine shi ε ghεne məm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

Oku_Text: Naa ndε wε əb báà se lu ghese wen lu nε əb noo mε noon
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.

Oku_Text: Dî nε wel tεy Keyioy Kelanené é Feyine jia yî lεyse se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.

Oku_Text: Kenəə kε ke fəyen ε wel əbchio lu kε ke lunen ε wen εytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.

Oku_Text: Fekak fejuηe fejime koom əmtam əmjuη, nε fekak febee fejime koom əmtam əmbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad.

Oku_Text: Wanwel náà chii ε məme ntiε se əbchii əbtal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

Oku_Text: Naa ndε wε əb nεεyen kenəə kε Ba ə wom wε əb luun ε εyyio kεηen lu lumse ə wom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.

Oku_Text: Jesus fəy se ndaa è ndu è diom kechem keghuye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus went out of the house and sat by the lake.

Oku_Text: Ngose wel kaame ε wen εybee bεme əb me lii se kenək è diom se ten nε ghel εjime teem se əbghune jio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat in it while all the people stood on the shore.

Oku_Text: Əb me se dinte əbnəə se ghene se εydio ε nganse suuye ge: Wel εylem əblee nè lò shik se make ngos.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he told them many things in parables saying: 'A farmer went out to sow his seed.'

Oku_Text: Əb loo mak εylee chemke ε beelese əmndu nə gwiy è biηte è yiε.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he was scattering the seed some fell along the path and the birds came and ate it up.

Oku_Text: Εylee chemke əbkuk εtiy nε ntiε wiy fo se εydio è me fəyke kan εymok bek ka ntiε nè dion wiy fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some fell on rocky places where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.

Oku_Text: Chuy nεy loo ban è nyok əbfua ten əb yoble bek ka εghaηe nè báà toke lii se ntiε.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when the sun rose the plants were scorched and they withered because they had no root.

Oku_Text: Εylee chemke ε məme kenjoonjoη ke me tooke è kuy è chite.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Other seed fell among thorns which grew up and choked the plants.

Oku_Text: Εylee chemke nε lu ε ntiε εyjuη è fəy è kuy è loote koom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Still other seed fell on good soil where it produced a crop—a hundred sixty or thirty times what was sown.

Oku_Text: Naa ndε wε əb kiin əbtuule se yio, əb yio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever has ears, let them hear.

Oku_Text: Ghon-εyfεl nə Jesus kokte gwiy è bif se wen ge: Lu bek ghε wè nè suηnen se ghel ghine ε nganse?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The disciples came to him and asked 'Why do you speak to the people in parables?'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Feyine nεεymen ghεne keele əbnəə wε kum εysak ə wen wε əb luun ε kenlete.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied 'Because the knowledge of the secrets of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you but not to them.'

Oku_Text: Naa ndε wε əb kiin lu nε Feyine nêy gise é ke dio nε naa ndε wε əb báà se ki lu nε Feyine náà fii kε εylaaye kε əb kiin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have even what they have will be taken from them.

Oku_Text: Mε suηnen se ghεne ε nganse bek ge ghene kiye yεne wiy yiote əbnəə yio wiy gese keele wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is why I speak to them in parables: Though seeing they do not see; though hearing they do not hear or understand.

Oku_Text: Bek ge tetem ə ghel ghine boolemen ghene chite əbtuule ə ghen è gese bvəte εshiε ə ghen
English_NIV_Equivalent: For this people’s heart has become calloused; they hardly hear with their ears and they have closed their eyes.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuη se ghεn bek ge εshiε ə ghεne yεne nε əbtuule ə wene yio əbnəə.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But blessed are your eyes because they see and your ears because they hear.

Oku_Text: Ghεn yiote yi kenəə kε kum ngane ngos εymaken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Listen then to what the parable of the sower means:

Oku_Text: Ngos yε εy nè chemken ε beelese lu ghel ghε é yion kenəə kε kum εysak ə Feyin è jia keele Satan me gwiy è fəyse kenəə ten
English_NIV_Equivalent: When anyone hears the message about the kingdom and does not understand it the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown.

Oku_Text: Ngos yε εy nè chemken ε əbkuk εtiy lu ghel ghε é yion kenəə ə Feyin é me beeme kaη εymok nè chεkle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The seed falling on rocky ground refers to someone who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.

English_NIV_Equivalent: But since they have no root they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes they quickly fall away.

Oku_Text: Ngos yε εy nè chemken ε məme kenjoonjoη lu ghel ghε é yion kenəə ə Feyin é vii εytem nε lu əbnəə wε ketuu ntiε fεne
English_NIV_Equivalent: The seed falling among the thorns refers to someone who hears the word but the worries of this life choke the word.

Oku_Text: Ngos yε εy nè chemken ε ntiε εyjuη lu ghel ghε é yion kenəə ə Feyin é lεm se εytem è me yε nεεy əbnəə əbjuηe

Oku_Text: Jesus gese maa ngan εylee se ghene ge: Εysak ə Feyine lu ka wel wε əb nè maken ngos ε wen εylem.

Oku_Text: Ngos yi fəy è kuy è kεεte se koom əbgii vi me yεnsen sεy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the wheat sprouted and formed heads then the weeds also appeared.

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Nεεy wel əbbaanese nə mε. Ghon-εyfεl ghii bif ge: Wè koηe ge ghes ndu è dəəne əbgii ten-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'An enemy did this,' he replied. The servants asked him, 'Do you want us to go and pull them up?'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Εηgaη! Tame ghεn loo dəəne è chok ngos ε məme ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'No,' he answered, 'because while you are pulling the weeds you may uproot the wheat with them.'

Oku_Text: Ghεn vəse é əb yε kuy fo əbjim se chin se kechii kε é náà kaa əbfua.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Let both grow together until the harvest.

Oku_Text: Ghεn chia è dəəne əbgii vi è kul ε giεse ε giεse è nyok è me dən εsaη è vii se mε əbηwak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat and bring it into my barn.

English_NIV_Equivalent: Though it is the smallest of all seeds when it grows it is the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree.

Oku_Text: Jesus gese maa ngan εylee ge: Εysak ə Feyine lu ka εyshiε feshik yε əbvii nè liεn è too kεlse əmnkfεη setal so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He told them another parable: 'The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into about sixty pounds of flour.'

Oku_Text: Jesus nè suuy əbnəə vin əbjim se ngose wel nε ε nganse. Əb nè báà suuy kenəə se ghene nε wiy ε nganse.

Oku_Text: Jesus nεy lòò è vəse feliε wel fi è lii se ndaa. Ghon-εyfεl nə wene gwiy è suuy se wen ge: Fekte se ghεs kenəə kε əbgii
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he left the crowd and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said 'Explain to us the parable of the weeds.'

English_NIV_Equivalent: The good seed stands for the people of the kingdom. The weeds are the people of the evil one.

Oku_Text: Wel wε əb nè maken əbgii lu Satan wε wel əbbaanese nə Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age.

Oku_Text: Kεn ka é nè baaten əbgii è nyok lu ghεε-ghεε ka ke naa gan əbliy wε mbi yî mεy ε ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As the weeds are pulled up and burned in the fire so it will be at the end of the age.

English_NIV_Equivalent: The Son of Man will send out his angels and they will weed out of his kingdom everything that causes sin.

Oku_Text: è mak se kemboo əbvəs bene fε ghene náà toηnen gese kfəle εsoη
English_NIV_Equivalent: They will throw them into the blazing furnace where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

Oku_Text: é ghel ə Feyine me yε ban ka chuy ε məm εysak nə Ba ə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father.

Oku_Text: Εysak ə Feyine lu ka ngan yε kum kefaa əbkaa kε ke nè luun ε əbkwak yεnsen wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field.

Oku_Text: Wel nεy yεn è nεyte chite è se saηle naa se ηaη è ndu è feene εykεη ə wen naa εyjim è yun əbkwake
English_NIV_Equivalent: When a man found it he hid it again and then in his joy went and sold all he had and bought that field.

Oku_Text: Εysak ə Feyine gese lu sεy ka ngan yε kum wel əbwεy əblee wε əb nè kεηe εytiy εjuηe se yun
English_NIV_Equivalent: Again the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant looking for fine pearls.

Oku_Text: é nεy yεn εylee nε εy jofe naa se ηaη è ndu è feene εykεη ə wen εyjim è yun so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he found one of great value he went away and sold everything he had and bought it.

Oku_Text: Εysak ə Feyine gese lu ka ngan yε kum felame səəse fε é nè maken ε jio εyghaken
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once again the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was let down into the lake and caught all kinds of fish.

Oku_Text: Səəse lun se ten ghel εykoo səəse guf è fəyse se bεy è diom è tayte sejuηe è vii se nkaa è fəyse sebee è maay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When it was full the fishermen pulled it up on the shore. They sat down and collected the good fish in baskets.

Oku_Text: Lu ka ke náà yε lu əbliy wε mbi mεyen. Feyine náà tum nchiyse-ndaa nə wen é se gwiy è fəyse ghel εbee se məm nə ghel εjuηe
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is how it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous.

Oku_Text: Jesus bif se ghon-εyfεl nə wene ge ghene sε lu nε é keele əbnəə vin-a? Ghene beeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Have you understood all these things? Jesus asked. Yes they replied.

Oku_Text: Naa ndinte tesak εykε yε ey yiekemen se yε lu wel əblee ε məme εysak nə Feyine lu ka ba ə ndaa

Oku_Text: Nεy se lu ka Jesus dinten əbnəə se ghele ε nganse ε mεyse ε leghε è me lòò se fo
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus had finished these parables he moved on from there.

Oku_Text: è ndu se ghene ntεk è se dinte əbnəə se ghele ε ghene ngay εyjem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Coming to his hometown he began teaching the people in their synagogue.

Oku_Text: Ghele yio è se lu nè əbchiò əbyúméné ghεynen ge: Əb liε ghàáy əbfee vin?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They were amazed. 'Where did this man get this wisdom and these miraculous powers?' they asked.

English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Isn't this the carpenter’s son? Isn't his mother’s name Mary?'

Oku_Text: Ghene ghεynen dì è me tunse wen.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: É lòó é yε ngvəmle wele ntum nə Feyine əblik əbjim buu kεn ε ghene ntεk.

English_NIV_Equivalent: For Herod had arrested John and bound him and put him in prison because of Herodias.

Oku_Text: Helod nè kεηe se yuye Joon dî è se fane ghel bek ge ghene nè liε wen ge wele ntum nə Feyine
English_NIV_Equivalent: Herod wanted to kill John but he was afraid of the people because they considered John a prophet.

Oku_Text: wán ə Helodias əbkiy bin ε ghel εjime εyshiε. Helod se saηle naa se ηaη
English_NIV_Equivalent: On Herod’s birthday the daughter of Herodias danced for the guests and pleased Herod so much.

Oku_Text: è me kak wán vi è laa kelek ge yi nêy fo naa ghε se wen kε əb bifen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.

Oku_Text: No ə wen bεyse wen əb suuy ge: Fó ketuu Joon Nfoo Moo se mε fεn ε εyshεn
English_NIV_Equivalent: Prompted by her mother she said 'Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.'

Oku_Text: É me ndu ε ndaa nchak è ten ketuu Joon è gwiy so εyshεn è fo se wan əbvii vi
English_NIV_Equivalent: The king was distressed... he sent and had John beheaded in the prison. His head was brought in on a platter.

Oku_Text: Jesus yio kenəə kε ke ganen è lii se kenək è lòò se fo è ndu əbkwak ka əb yε lu fo wen nyiη.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus heard what had happened he withdrew by boat privately to a solitary place.

Oku_Text: Jesus gwiy è se fəye se kenək è yεn feliε wel ketuu lum wen əb me se chuke ghε é nè yafken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus landed and saw a large crowd he had compassion on them and healed their sick.

Oku_Text: Lam é ghele giabsen è lii è ndu ε ntεkse è yun əbfua-əbyíéne nə ghen.

Oku_Text: Ghene fise ge: Ghεs ki kεn ntukse keban fεn setan nè səə sebaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish,' they answered.

Oku_Text: Əb fii ntukse keban shi setan nè səə shi sebaa è kiy εyyio è bεèlè Feyin è bεkte kebane ten
English_NIV_Equivalent: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven he gave thanks and broke the loaves.

Oku_Text: Wel əbjim yiε è kul. Ghon-εyfεlε biηte əbmvεkele wε əb nè bεεsen əb lun nkaase njio-sebaa εyvəm.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghon-εyfεl nə wen ge: Ghεn lii se kenək è diaη ε njine jio se mbiy.

Oku_Text: Əb tinse ghel dì è lòò è kok ε kfən se jem wen nyiη. Kebeye nεy se lu əb bεεy fo wen nyiη.

Oku_Text: nε əbfiεfe gwiye jii mbiy shikse jio nε εy loole sheηse kenək.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the boat was already a considerable distance from land buffeted by the waves because the wind was against it.

Oku_Text: Jesus se jεle gwiye ε ketuu moo se baksene ghon-εyfεl nə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Shortly before dawn Jesus went out to them walking on the lake.

Oku_Text: Ghone yεn ka əb jεlen gwiyen ε ketuu moo è me se fane toηe suuye ge: Lu nkfəsay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake they were terrified. 'It’s a ghost,' they said.

Oku_Text: Bita me suuy ge: Sayte ngan vin se ghεs.

Oku_Text: Bek ge εytem ə wel lu kε ke fəye əbnəə əbbee εy yuye wel εymaay εychoηe εysuuy əbsuuse nè εytεy.

Oku_Text: Jesus nεy lòò se fo è ndu ε jio-sebaa ntεk ε Taya ghene Sidon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Leaving that place, Jesus withdrew to the region of Tyre and Sidon.

Oku_Text: Εεne əbvii wè fo εytem ε wen εybee! Ke yε lu ka wè nè koηen.

Oku_Text: Ngose wel gwiy nè ghε é nè kfəfe nè εkεblene nè ghε é nè yafen nè əbchichii

Oku_Text: è gwiy nè ghene è me fəyse se Jesus əbghun è chuk ghene əbjim.

Oku_Text: Ghel εjim ghii yεn ka əbchichii suηnen εkεblene jεle ghε é nè kfəfe yεne əblik nè ghε é nè yafen bonen è me kante dì è kokse Feyin ə ghele Islayεl.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people were amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled made well, the lame walking and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.

Oku_Text: Ketuu lum mε bek ghel ghine bek ge ghene nè chiin nè mε è me se mεy əbchii əbtal
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I have compassion for these people; they have already been with me three days and have nothing to eat.'

Oku_Text: Mε báà se koηe se lam é ghene ndu nè jεηe bek ka ghene yε fəy jio.

English_NIV_Equivalent: 000) nε é báà taη əbkiy ghene ghone.

English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor,'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghon-εyfεl nə wen ge: Mε suuy se ghεn ge: Ke lu kεghoo se wele nkaa lii ε məme εysak nə Feyin.

Oku_Text: Jesus kokte baksen se ghene è suuy se ghene ge: Ba ǝ wom lu ne ǝb foomen ǝbtaawe eysake wé eyyio gese nè wé ntie se me ǝbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus came to them and said, ‘All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.

Oku_Text: Ghen ndu yi se ǝbtum ə bjim è nɛɛy ghele ye lu ghon-eyfel nə me é ghen ye foo moo se ghene se eyghel nə Bay nề se eyghel nə Wan gese nè se eyghel nə Keyioy Kelanene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,

Oku_Text: Ghen dinte əbnəə se ghene é ghene ye lem əbnəə ǝbjim we me nè dinten se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you.

Oku_Text: Ghen keele ge me ko nêy ye lu ken ghesen se chin ka mbi náà mεy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age.’

Oku_Text: Lee sakghe ǝbjuŋ kum Jesus Christ we wán a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, the Son of God,

Oku_Text: Əb nè kɛɛte ken ka wele ntum nə Feyin we Ezaya nè nyakenmen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: as it is written in Isaiah the prophet:

Oku_Text: Με náà tum wele ntum nə me e wè mbiy é əb siyse jii ə yiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way’—

Oku_Text: Yio ka gie wele jarŋen e ǝbkwak ge: Siyse jii se Báàba, neyte kevǝǝ jii se wen é ke ye nduu e titiy!
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’a voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.’’

Oku_Text: Ken di Joon me gwiy & ǝbkwak è se foo moo fekte se ghele ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: And so John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preaching

Oku_Text: Ghen kubsen tetem a ten è fiile se cybame mbee é fó moo se ghen ka Feyine leyse mbee a sen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

Oku_Text: Ghele nè lô fǝy teghane Judea tejim ghele Jolesaleme gese fǝy naa ɛjim è ndu se yene Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem went out to him.

Oku_Text: Ghene nè lô se taŋle sychio a ghen ge ghene lu ne é neɛy mbee ne Joon me foo moo se ghene e jio Joldan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.

Oku_Text: Joon nè make ndǝyse ne é báà nè cyghuye nyam keseseye gese teme kekuye guo se wen əmkenken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: John wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist,

Oku_Text: Eyyie a wene nè lu nguumese ghene fedione.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and he ate locusts and wild honey.

Oku_Text: Sakghɛ we Joon nè fekten nè lu ge: Ka me gwiyen wel bo gwiye e me eybam taa chia me
English_NIV_Equivalent: And this was his message: ‘After me comes the one more powerful than I,

Oku_Text: ne chèmè lu ken guose evəə nə wen fene me báà se lu kekwenten ka lòó me ngvǝkte è yayte.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.

Oku_Text: Με fóó ne lu moo se ghen ne ǝb nêy gwiy è yɛ fóó ne lu Keyioy Kelanene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.’

Oku_Text: Se lu & keleŋ əbliy fo Jesus lòò e nteke Nazalet & Galilee è gwiy Joon fo moo se wen & jio Joldan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

Oku_Text: Jesus se fǝye gwiye se jio e leghe è me yen ka eyyio disen
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just as Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw heaven being torn open

Oku_Text: ne Keyioy a Feyine sǝǝke gwiye ɛ wen ə əbghun & ablame kenlal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.

Oku_Text: Gie fǝy è gwiy eyyio ge: Wè lu wán a wom we me korgen. Me saŋle naa se ņaŋ kum wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And a voice came from heaven: ‘You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.’

Oku_Text: Keyioy a Feyine tinse wen ɛ ǝbkwak ken se ǝbliy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At once the Spirit sent him out into the wilderness,

Oku_Text: Əb nè chii ǝbkwak se ǝbchii əmmkeek πε Satan momse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and he was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan.

Oku_Text: Əb nè lu ken ghene nyamse ǝbkwake nchiyse-ndaa nə Feyine gwiy è giamten wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He was with the wild animals, and angels attended him.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka é nè viinmen Joon se nchak Jesus me lii è ndu e Galilee è se fekte nduu nè sakghe ǝbjun na Feyine
English_NIV_Equivalent: After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.

Oku_Text: Jesus nè suuy ge: Əbliye kwenmen ne eysake Feyine baksenmen. Ghen kubsen è beeme sakghe abjug
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’The time has come,’ he said. ‘The kingdom of God has come near. Repent and believe the good news!’

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ka Jesus nè chian & abghune kechsam keghuye ké Galilee ab yen Simon ghene wan no a wene Andole ne ghene make felam e jio bek ka ghene nè hun ghel eykoo saase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As Jesus walked beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Ghen gwiy é loo é me nery é ghen ye koo ne lu ghel
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Come, follow me,’ Jesus said, ‘and I will send you out to fish for people.’

Oku_Text: Ghene me vase əmlam mo ghen kan rymok è se jumte nduu nè wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At once they left their nets and followed him.

Oku_Text: Jesus se kente nduu è yen Jems ghene wan-no a wene Joon ghon a Zebedee e kenak nr ghene neyte amlam ma ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he had gone a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat, preparing their nets.

Oku_Text: Jesus me jag ghene kan symok ghene vase ba a ghene Zebedee ghene ghel efflene e mame kenak è me se jumte nduu nè Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Without delay he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.

Oku_Text: Ghene me lòò è ndu z Kabanaum se lu & kechii rybam Jesus me lii è ndu e ngay eyjem è se dinte əbnəə se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They went to Capernaum, and when the Sabbath came, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.

Oku_Text: Əbchio yum ghel kum jii ye ǝb nè dinten e ten bek ka ǝb nè dinten əbnəə se ghene ka wel we ǝb kiin əbtaawe dinte wiy ka ndintese tesak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as the teachers of the law.

Oku_Text: Se lu ken ǝbliy në fry wel ǝblee & ghene ngay eyjem ne eyfime lu & wen ǝbler me ton & ghene məm ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just then a man in their synagogue who was possessed by an impure spirit cried out,

Oku_Text: Jesuse wê Nazalet wè kene se jel legy se ghes? Wè gwiymen se meyse ghes-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us?

Oku_Text: Me ke wel we wè luun abghen. We lu wel ǝblanen nə Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I know who you are—the Holy One of God!’

Oku_Text: Jesus me wam ge: Yer lu s chichi è fǝy se wen ǝblee!
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Be quiet!’ said Jesus sternly. ‘Come out of him!’

Oku_Text: Eyfime yé tene jikten wele ten naa se rjan è ton e gie cytaa è me fay se wen ables.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The impure spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a shriek.

Oku_Text: Obchio yum ghel ejim e ju rybee ghene se kane ge: Kine lu rynkii kenaa keke?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people were all so amazed that they asked each other, ‘What is this?

Oku_Text: Eyyirke eyfre ye Ma é yieken nè abtaawel Əb kwanten tefim ne te yionen se went as Sakghe a wen me ghan abghoge Galilee naa abjim kan se abliy
English_NIV_Equivalent: A new teaching—and with authority! He even gives orders to impure spirits and they obey him.’ News about him spread quickly over the whole region of Galilee.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus në fǝyenmen se ngay ryjem & leghe ghene ghe Jems nè Joon me ndu e ndaa na Simon ghene Andole.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.

Oku_Text: No ǝbchii na Simon nè nyin əbkun yafe nè kencheme ghene me fekte eyyafe yê tene se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they immediately told Jesus about her.

Oku_Text: Jesus me chia è ghal keghoo wel abyafen è giamten wen ab loole.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he went to her, took her hand and helped her up.

Oku_Text: Ka ab loolen gwiyen e leghe kencheme ké tene me takle ab me se fele abfua-abyíčne ge ghene yie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The fever left her and she began to wait on them.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu e likfǝǝy fo ken ka chuy sooyen ghele lie ghel eyafkene nè ghe tefime liin se amler è gwiy nè ghene se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: That evening after sunset the people brought to Jesus all the sick and demon-possessed.

Oku_Text: Ntrke ten me lòò naa eyjim è me ndu è chintèn e ǝbchio ndaa yr Jesus nè luun e ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The whole town gathered at the door,

Oku_Text: Jesus me chuk ghel ghe é ně yafken se eydio è gese jumte tefim se sydio se ghele əmlɛɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and Jesus healed many who had various diseases. He also drove out many demons,

Oku_Text: Əb nè báà se foo jii ge tefime té tene ye sunnen bek ka te nè keen wel we luun wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but he would not let the demons speak because they knew who he was.

Oku_Text: Se lu e bvase bvase Jesus loole naa ne kebeye bo fine è fay se ndaa è ndu z kelik ken wen nyin è jem fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed.

Oku_Text: Simon ghene ghel ryjel na wene me lòò è se kɛŋe Jesus
English_NIV_Equivalent: Simon and his companions went to look for him,

Oku_Text: se lu ka ghene nè yenenmen wen e leghe ghene suuy ge: Wel ǝbjime kerne wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and when they found him, they exclaimed: ‘Everyone is looking for you!’

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghesen ndu e nteke sẽ kelen fene è me ye lu se fekte gho sey. Ghey kenǝǝ ke me nè gwiyen bek keghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, ‘Let us go somewhere else—to the nearby villages—so I can preach there also. That is why I have come.’

Oku_Text: Əb me se ghane abghorje jume tefim se ghele əmlɛɛ. Galilee ǝbjim fekte & ngayse eyjem
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.

Oku_Text: Wel ablee yafe në kensaase nè lô gwiy se Jesus è təm eləy & wene eyshie è bernte Jesus ge: Loo lu ne wè kone we nery é me bonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A man with leprosy came to him and begged him on his knees, ‘If you are willing, you can make me clean.’

Oku_Text: Jesus koosen eysen se wen naa se narŋ è nase keghoo è kum wen so è suuy ge: Me korje bonen!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus was indignant. He reached out his hand and touched the man. ‘I am willing,’ he said. ‘Be clean!’

Oku_Text: Kensaase ké tene me mey se wele ten abghun ken se ǝbliy ab bonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy ge əb ye ndu è chiise se wen ge: Yee ke se suuy kenǝǝ ke ke kooyen kin ntaŋle è taŋle ntaŋle ka eysake se wel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus sent him away at once with a strong warning: ‘See that you don’t tell this to anyone.

Oku_Text: Ndu è din ngeŋ ə yie se wele Mosese dinen ge wè bonen wè tanle ka ǝbchǝyte se din se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.’

Oku_Text: Wele ten sey me lòò è se fekte nduu nè kenǝǝ ké tene ǝblik əbjim ǝb nè lô bee kenǝǝ ké tene e jii ye Jesus nè lòó ǝb jia bo lii è yɛ ghane ntek bene lòó wel yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Instead he went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news. As a result, Jesus could no longer enter a town openly

Oku_Text: Jesus me se ghane ne lu 2 teghane ǝbkwak ne ghele lòò naa əblik əbjim gwiye yene wen fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but stayed outside in lonely places. Yet the people still came to him from everywhere.

Oku_Text: Se lu ken e eybam ǝbchii Jesus kase ndu se Kabanaum é fekte è ndu ge əb shi ɛ əbchio ndaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home.

Oku_Text: Ghele se eydio kaame e wen a ndaa fensane jia gese se lu naa nè se kebaa ndaa ǝb se fekte kenaa a Feyin se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them.

Oku_Text: Ghel elee me gwiy fo në wel ǝbkeblen se wen ne ghe ɛkerke bun
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.

Oku_Text: è jia se ki jii se chia bek ngose wel ye ey nè luun fo è me kok e ketuu ndaa fo è beyte kelik ken ghaa Jesus nè tiyen è sakse wen e Jesus eyshie ne ab ko brey & fegham.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on.

Oku_Text: Jesus yan jii ye ghene foon eytem se Feyine ten è suuy se wel ǝbkeblen vi ge: Wan a wom me leysemen mbee a shie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, ‘Son, your sins are forgiven.’

Oku_Text: Ghé eydinte tesak clee ghe é nè diom fo me se kante & ghene tetem ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves,

Oku_Text: Wel vine surŋnen lexy le! Əb teyle Feyin teylen!
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming!

Oku_Text: Lu nde we lòó ǝb leyse mbeese ghel buu ken Feyin?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Who can forgive sins but God alone?’

Oku_Text: Jesus ko me keele & wen ǝ eytem ken se ǝbliy əbnəə we ghene nè kwaaten è bif se ghene ge: Ghen ki tekwaate tin & ghene tetem bek ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, ‘Why are you thinking these things?

Oku_Text: Kɛ ke bonen lu kine se suuy se wel ǝbkeblen ge: É leysemen mbee a shie moo se suuy ge: Loole è lie fegham a fie è ye jele-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’?

Oku_Text: Dî ne me din se ghen ge Wanwel ki ǝbtaawe ɛ ketuu ntie se leyse mbeese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.’

Oku_Text: Əb suuy dî è me suuy se wel ǝbkɛblen vi ge: Με suuy se wè ge: Loole è liɛ fegham ə fie è ye kfǝǝy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he said to the man, ‘I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.’

Oku_Text: Wel vi me loole ken se ǝbliy è lie fegham ə wen è se kfǝǝye nε ghene ɛjime kiye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all.

Oku_Text: Kii në kine neεy ghel ɛjime se lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyúméné kokse Feyin suuye ge: Ghes në brey ghàà yi yen kenǝǝ ka kine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, ‘We have never seen anything like this!’

Oku_Text: Jesus ney gese ndu se ǝbghune kechem keghuye ké Galilee felic wele gwiy se wen ǝb me kerte se ye dinte əbnəə se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once again Jesus went out beside the lake. A large crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.

Oku_Text: ǝb ney loo ghanle ndu è yen wel we ǝb nè fiin shile ne eyghel a wene lu Levi we wán ǝ Alfaeus ne ǝb diom & wene kelik eyfel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax collector’s booth.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Gwiy é loo. Əb me loole è se jumte eybam ǝ Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Follow me,’ Jesus told him, and Levi got up and followed him.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney se yie e Matio a ndaa ne ghel eyfii shile se eydio gese nè ghele mbeese diom fo ghene ghe Jesus nè ghon-eyfel na wene è se yie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While Jesus was having dinner at Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with him and his disciples,

Oku_Text: Nè lu di nɛ ghele jele & wen rybam ɛ kechii nê fo se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for there were many who followed him.

Oku_Text: Ndintese tesak selee se nè luun ghele kenone Falisee yen ka əb yien ghene ghele mbeese gese nè ghel eyfii shile è bif se ghon-eyfel nə wene ge: Lu bek ghe we ǝb yien ghene ghé ɛyfii shile gese nè ghele mbeese?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the teachers of the law who were Pharisees saw him eating with the sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples: ‘Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?’

Oku_Text: Jesus yio ka ghene bifen dî è fise se ghene ge: Ghel ghe é luun ǝbghun əbtaa shii wiy wel ǝbchuken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On hearing this, Jesus said to them, ‘It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.

Oku_Text: Shii ken ghel eyafkene wel ǝbchuken. Me báà gwiy se tiyse ghel ejuje me gwiy se tiyse ne lu ghel ebee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.’

Oku_Text: Nè lu & kelerŋ əbliy kelee ghon-eyfel nə Joon gese nè ghele kenone Falisee se bame əbfua-ǝbyíéne ghel elee gwiy è bif se Jesus ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came and asked Jesus,

Oku_Text: Lu bek ghe we ghon-eyfel na Joon gese nề ghé kenone Falisee bamen ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne ne ghie bame wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’How is it that John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees are fasting, but yours are not?’

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Le lòó ghele ndu ǝbjen è yɛ lu se jeŋ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, ‘How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them?

Oku_Text: Lòó ke jia gan dì nɛ lum jen lu ghene ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They cannot, so long as they have him with them.

Oku_Text: Dî ne əbliye gwiye we é nêy fii lum jen se ghene é ghene me ye lu se jen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and on that day they will fast.

Oku_Text: Wele see jia bale ndəy seməə nè kensase ndǝy sefer.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment.

Oku_Text: Əb neey dì é kensase sefɛɛ shii sayte ndəy seməə sayten é kebuke tene ghak è chia ka ke kè luun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Otherwise, the new piece will pull away from the old, making the tear worse.

Oku_Text: Lu eyghel ka lòó wel jia kuune əmnduk əmbol ɛ kefaa əmnduk nɛ ke dənmen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins.

Oku_Text: Əb nɛɛy dì é əmnduke mé ten nɛɛy é kefaa tene bvəs é ǝmndukme ley se lólò é kefaa tene lɛy sêy se lólò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined.

Oku_Text: Engan é lu se ye vii əmnduk əmbol ne lu ɛ kefaa əmnduk kefɛɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No, he pours new wine into new wineskins.’

Oku_Text: Ney se lu e kechii ɛybam kelee ghe Jesus se chia eylem esan ghon-eyfel na wene me se kote ǝbtuu esan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and as his disciples walked along, they began to pick some heads of grain.

Oku_Text: Ghele kenone Falisee clee me bif se wen ge: Yen-a! Lu bek ghe we ghon-eyfel nə wè nɛɛyen kenǝǝ kɛ ɛysake báà beeme ge é nɛɛy & kechii cybam?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Pharisees said to him, ‘Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?’

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Ghen nè bɛɛy ghàà jaŋ kenǝǝ ke David nè nɛɛyen e əbliy we jeŋ nè yafen ghene ghel eyjel nə wen ne ghene shii ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne se yie-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: He answered, ‘Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?

Oku_Text: Nè lu əbliy we Abiatal nè luun ketuu se ghé ntarŋlee David le nè báà lii e ndaa-eykokse Feyin è liɛ keban ke é nè tarŋlen Feyin so è yie-a;
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the days of Abiathar the high priest, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread,

Oku_Text: keban nɛ eysake nè báà beeme ge lòó wel yiɛ buu ken ghé ntarŋlee əb lie è yiɛ è fo sey ghel eyjel nə wene yie-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: which is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions.’

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Feyine nè lem kechii ɛybam bek wel ne ǝb nè báà bom wel bek kechii eybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to them, ‘The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: Ke me se lu ge Wanwel lu Báàba a kechii sybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.’

Oku_Text: Oku_Text
English_NIV_Equivalent: English_NIV_Equivalent

Oku_Text: Ney se lu əbliy ǝblee Jesus ndu e ngay eyjem, ne wel ǝblee lu fo ne keghoo a wene kuomen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Another time Jesus went into the synagogue, and a man with a shriveled hand was there.

Oku_Text: Ndinte, Moses nè nyak e ghesene nchil ge: Wel kuo è vase ǝbvii ghàà ghon, fene wan-no a wel ǝbkuon lu se lem ǝbvii ten é ghene wen ye bii ghon ge ghé wel ǝbkuone.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Teacher," they said, "Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.

Oku_Text: Ghone-nyii elee nè lô se lu saamba, we ngesewel kiile ǝbvii è naa kuo ne ghene wen beɛy ghàà yɛ ki ghon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without leaving any children.

Oku_Text: Kenkumtene lem ǝbvii vi, è kuo ghàà wan, eybam ǝ wene lem, ke gan kɛn dì.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The second one married the widow, but he also died, leaving no child. It was the same with the third.

Oku_Text: Ghene ghe ghel saamba kuo ɛjim ne é báà lem wan. Se cybam, əbvii vi kuo sey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In fact, none of the seven left any children. Last of all, the woman died too.

Oku_Text: Feyine náà lose ghel se eykuo, é ǝb ye lu wiy a nde nε ghene nè liemen wen se ǝbvii ghel ejim?
English_NIV_Equivalent: At the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?"

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghene nduu nè eybame bek ge ghɛne báà se ke kenǝǝ ke ke luun e nwaale-Feyin gese ke wiy abtaawe Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, "Are you not in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God?

Oku_Text: Feyine náà lose ghel se cykuo, é ye lu ka nchiyse-ndaa na Feyine luun e eyyio, è jia yɛ chii ka ǝbvii ghene lum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.

Oku_Text: Ka ghene maanen ge Feyine náà lose ghel se ɛykuo, ke dine ge ghene beɛy ghàà jaŋ kenǝǝ ke Moses nè nyaken kum fekak fe ǝbvase nè báálen e ten?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now about the dead rising—have you not read in the Book of Moses, in the account of the burning bush, how God said to him,

Oku_Text: Feyine nè suuy se wen ge: Me lu Feyin a Mak Ablaham nè Ayzek gese nè Jakob.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?

Oku_Text: Feyine dì báà se lu Feyin ə ghel ekuone, Əb lu Feyin ə ghel echiinee. Ghene jase naa se naŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent:
He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken!""
1552,Wel ǝblee & ngoo na ghé eydinte tesake yio ka Jesus ghene ghel ghii se beken mbek yi di

Oku_Text: Wè lu se ye korje Bay Feyin nə wề nè eytem ə yie eyjime, nè keyioy a kis kejime, nè eykwaate a yie eyjime gese nè kediak ə kiɛ kejime.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.’

Oku_Text: Kenkumte nchile lu ge: Wè lu se ye koje wel we ǝb báksen se wè ken ghee-ghee ka wè korjen ngeŋ ə yiɛ. Nchile báà gese se lu ye ɛy chian nchil se sebaa shin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The second is this: ’Love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no commandment greater than these."

Oku_Text: Ndinte tesak yi me suuy se Jesus ge: Ndinte, wè suuy e titiy ge Feyine lu ken Báàba wen nyin nè Feyin felee wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Well said, teacher," the man replied. "You are right in saying that God is one and there is no other but him.

Oku_Text: Ke lu sey e titiy ka wè suuyen ge: Wel lu se ye kone Feyin nè eytem a wen eyjime, nè eykwaate a wen eyjime gese nè kediak a wen kejime, gese lu se ye koje wel we ǝb báksen se wen ken ghee-ghee ka ǝb konen ngeŋ ə wen. Nchil shine chia cyfô eyjim ye nyok ka ntarŋle se Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: To love him with all your heart, with all your understanding and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices."

Oku_Text: Jesus keele ge ǝb fise nè rytofe, è suuy se wen ge: Wê lu e nchie e nchie se fiisen eysak a Feyin. Se eybame ten, wel jia gese mom ge yi bif eybif se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw that he had answered wisely, he said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And from then on no one dared ask him any more questions.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney loo dinte əbnəə se ghele e ndaa-eykokse è bif ge: Ke ghan leey ne ndintese tesake ye suuy ge Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen lu wan a David?
English_NIV_Equivalent: While Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, he asked, "Why do the teachers of the law say that the Messiah is the son of David?

Oku_Text: Keyioy Kelanene nè lô nɛɛy David se wen a ngeŋ suuy ge: Bay nè suuy se Báàba a wom ge: Diom & me eybee ǝbkoy ǝbtaa Se chin ka me nêy nyinse mbanen a shie é wè tom evəə ə ghie e ghene ǝbtuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: David himself, speaking by the Holy Spirit, declared: ’The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet."’"

Oku_Text: David se wene ngerŋ nè torŋte Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen ge Báàba, ke ghan leey ge ǝb ye lu wán ə David. Ngose wel yióté əbnəə we Jesus nè suuyen, è se koje.
English_NIV_Equivalent: David himself calls him ’Lord.’ How then can he be his son?" The large crowd listened to him with delight.

Oku_Text: Jesus se ndinte əbnəə se ghele suuye ge: Ghen yɛ bààme ghé eydinte tesake. Ghene koje se ye kaale ne é mak dale seghakene, kone ge é ye chakte ghene nè eyvəme əblik əbwey,
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he taught, Jesus said, "Watch out for the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and most of all to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces,

Oku_Text: Ghene lóó é ye koŋ se diom e ǝblik əbtaa e ngayse cyjem. Loo lu əblik eyyie ǝbjen ghene ko ye koŋ se diom ken əblik əbtaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.

Oku_Text: Ghene lóó é leese əbkiy-nkfə, è fii əbfua ǝ ghen, fefse jeme tejem tedia. Feyine náà mak ngɛk yɛ ɛy taan è chia se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They devour widows’ houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will be punished most severely."

Oku_Text: Jesus ney diom ɛ ǝbghune kekaŋ ə Feyin, è se kiye ka ghele gwiyen, viin eyfoo a ghen se kekarje Feyin. Ghé ǝmkaa se eydio se gwiye yine ǝbkaa yinen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus sat down opposite the place where the offerings were put and watched the crowd putting their money into the temple treasury. Many rich people threw in large amounts.

Oku_Text: Wiy-nkfə əblee ne lu wele ketan gwiy ké wene, è chemse eshie ǝbkaa ebaa se kekarŋ fo. Ne lu ken nini eyful.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But a poor widow came and put in two very small copper coins, worth only a few cents.

Oku_Text: Jesus jarnte ghon-eyfel nə wen, è suuy se ghene ge: Me suuye ǝbluuy se ghen ge: Wiy-nkfə we wele ketan vin lu ne ǝb fó eyfô è chia wel ǝbjim we ǝb viin eyfô ə wen e kekaŋ nə Feyine fen lan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Calling his disciples to him, Jesus said, "Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put more into the treasury than all the others.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝbjim kè bese fefafa e məme əbdione foo, dî ne ǝbvii vin ka chèmè luun wele ketan le, kfəte kebam a wen è fo, ne shi mey-mey ǝ wen di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They all gave out of their wealth; but she, out of her poverty, put in everything—all she had to live on."
