Oku_Text: è fo eynkii əmnduk əmlee se Jesus ge əb nəə, ne é fis nè kegii kelee ne é tonte ge Mee di ǝb jia nǝǝ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.

Oku_Text: ǝb bante wen se kentam, è lie ndəyse ǝ wen, è mak kan se yen ge é se giate lexy-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they crucified him. Dividing his clothes, they cast lots to see what each would get.

Oku_Text: É nè bante wen se kentam & chuy buumok e bvǝse bvǝse,
English_NIV_Equivalent: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.

Oku_Text: è me nyak kenǝǝ è base se wene ketuu kentam ge njio ə wen. Kenəə nê kin nè lu ge: Vin lu abfon ə ghele Juus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The written notice of the charge against him read: THE KING OF THE JEWS.

Oku_Text: É gese bante echon ebaa se əmntam e Jesus eyghan, ǝblee eybee əbkoy əbtaa nè ǝblee se eybee ǝbkoy ǝbkiye,
English_NIV_Equivalent: They crucified two rebels with him, one on his right and one on his left.

Oku_Text: [É bante di, kenǝǝ kɛ é nè nyaken & ŋwaale-Feyin ge: "É nè tan wen ghene ghele mbeese" me kwen].
English_NIV_Equivalent: [And the scripture was fulfilled which says, "He was counted with the lawless."]

Oku_Text: Ghel ghe é nè chian jii fo me se mekte ǝbtuu a ghen tɛyle wen suuye ge: Aa! Wè ne bee ge wè sakte ndaa-eykokse, è kase baklé se ǝbchii əbtal,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those who passed by hurled insults at him, shaking their heads and saying, "So! You who are going to destroy the temple and build it in three days,

Oku_Text: Giamten yi ngeŋ ə yiɛ è sǝǝke se kentam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: save yourself and come down from the cross!"

Oku_Text: Ghele mbiy se ghé ntarŋlee nè ndintese tesake ko se buu sey wen suuye ge: Əb nè giamten ghel echine di ne lòó ǝb jia giamten ngen a wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the same way the chief priests and the teachers of the law mocked him among themselves. "He saved others," they said, "but he can't save himself!

Oku_Text: Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen lu abfon a ghele Islael, sǝake yi se kentam e leenen é ghes yen è fo cytem se wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Let this Messiah, this king of Israel, come down now from the cross, that we may see and believe."

Oku_Text: Ghel ghe ɛbaa ghe é nè banten ghene ghe Jesus ko se teyle wen sey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those crucified with him also heaped insults on him.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ka chuy tiysen, mbi sasen è fin naa eyjim, è fin dî se chuy setal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At noon, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.

Oku_Text: Se lu & chuy setal Jesus ton e gie eytaa ge: Eloi, Eloi, Lama Sabatani, ne lu ge: Feyin ə Fome, Feyin ə Fome, Wè maay me le bek ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: And at three in the afternoon Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?" (which means "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?").

Oku_Text: Ghel elee e mǝme ghel ghe é nè tiyen fo yio, è me se suuye ge: Yen-a, ab se jarje Elija.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When some of those standing near heard this, they said, "Listen, he’s calling Elijah."

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee e ghene mom nyiŋ è ndu, è lie kuncha, è chǝǝ se kengwey əmnduk, è sooy se ketuu mbàn, è lose se Jesus ge ab nǝǝ,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Someone ran, filled a sponge with wine vinegar, put it on a staff, and offered it to Jesus to drink.

Oku_Text: è se suuye se ghele ge: Ghesen chiyte ge Elija se gwiy è səkse wen se kentam-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Now leave him alone. Let’s see if Elijah comes to take him down," he said.

Oku_Text: Jesus nêy ton e gie eytaa, è me mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: With a loud cry, Jesus breathed his last.

Oku_Text: Ndǝy se é nè kerjen eybuu eylanene so e ndaa-eykokse me basen ǝbwaŋ əbbua se lòò e eyyio se sǝǝke se chin e ntie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.

Oku_Text: Ketuu ghel ɛyghon ke ke nè tiyen & Jesus ǝbghune kentam yen jii yɛ əb kuon e ten è suuy ge: Wel vin nè lu ken naa wán ǝ Feyin se ǝbluuy!
English_NIV_Equivalent: And when the centurion, who stood there in front of Jesus, saw how he died, he said, "Surely this man was the Son of God!"

Oku_Text: Nè lu di ne ǝbkiy clee teem ɛ əbtəm è se kiye. Əbkiy elee ɛ mǝme ǝbkiy nê ghan nè lu Merle Magdalene, në Salome gese nè Meele we no a Joses ghene Jems ǝbtele,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some women were watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joseph, and Salome.

Oku_Text: Əbkiy në ghin nè jele e Jesus rybam e ǝbliy we ǝb nè luun e Galilee giamten wen. Əbkiy elee ghe ghene nè koken e Jesus e rybam e Jolesalem nè lu sey fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In Galilee these women had followed him and cared for his needs. Many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem were also there.

Oku_Text: É gvəəme Jesus 42-43 Əbtəke ney loo jie, Josef wê Alimatea gwiy. Əb nè lu wel ǝblee & mǝm nə cheese, ne ghele ngvəmle wen, ne ǝb gese chiyte cysak a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It was Preparation Day (that is, the day before the Sabbath). So as evening approached, Joseph of Arimathea, a prominent member of the Council, who was himself waiting for the kingdom of God, went boldly to Pilate and asked for Jesus’ body.

Oku_Text: Kechii nê kin nè lu kechii eybaate, ne lu kechii ke ke nè fóon se kechii cybame. Josef me kul sytem è lii e ntok, è suuy ge Bailet fő eygvəne Jesus se yi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Pilate was surprised to hear that he was already dead. Summoning the centurion, he asked him if Jesus had already died.

Oku_Text: Bailet se lu nè ǝbchíò abyúméné se yio ge Jesus kè kuomen, è me jarŋ ketuu se ghé eyghorje è bif se wen ge se lu ǝbluuy ge Jesus kè kuomen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he learned from the centurion that it was so, he gave the body to Joseph.

Oku_Text: Bailet yio dì, è me suuy se Josef ge ǝb ndu è səkse rygvəne Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So Joseph bought some linen cloth, took down the body, wrapped it in the linen, and placed it in a tomb cut out of rock.

Oku_Text: Josef me yun əbkoye ndǝyse, è səkse eygvəne Jesus, è lim se ten è me vii se eysey ye é nè chufen & eyghok, è me biŋse eytiy è chite abchio eysey so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.

Oku_Text: Nè lu dî ne Merle Magdalene ghene Mɛɛle we no a Joses lu fo kiye ghaa é gvəəmen Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph saw where he was laid.

Oku_Text: Kechii eybame chia, Meele no a Jems nè Salome yun əbyióséne keləm kejuje se ndu se yiose eygvǝne Jesus so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might go to anoint Jesus’ body.

Oku_Text: Se lu & bvase bvǝse ken ka chuye tóòn ɛ kechii ké mbiye se ǝbwey, ghene ndu abchio eysey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they were on their way to the tomb

Oku_Text: Ghene loo ndu, è se kante ge sẽ nêy se birgse nde eytiy se chakse se ǝbchio eysey se ghen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent:
and they asked each other, "Who will roll the stone away from the entrance of the tomb?""
1747,Ghene ndu

Oku_Text: Ghene me lii se ten, è yen wánde nsum ne ab diom & eybee ǝbkoy ǝbtaa ne ǝb mak ndəy sefəfene, ǝbghune beɛme ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were alarmed.

Oku_Text: Wánde nsum vi suuy se ghene ge: Ghàà ghen yɛ fan. Me ke ge ghene kerje Jesus wê Nazalet we é nè banten se kentam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Don't be alarmed," he said. "You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified.

Oku_Text: Əb báà se lu fen, ǝb kè loolemen se rykuo. Ghen yen kelik ke é nè nyirŋsen wen e ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has risen! He is not here. See the place where they laid him.

Oku_Text: Ghen ndu è suuy se ghon-eyfel nə wene gese nè Bita ge: Əb se nduu & Galilee & ghene mbiy ghaa ghene nêy yen wen, ken ka ab nè suuyen se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ’He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.’"

Oku_Text: Əbkiy ghi fǝy se eysey fey, è se nyine, chine gese lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyűméné, è jia suuy kenǝǝ se wel bek ka ghene nè fanen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Trembling and bewildered, the women went out and fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid.

Oku_Text: [Əbkiy ghi me ndu se Bita ghene ghel eyjel nə wene, è fekte əbnəə əbjim əbtəm əbtəm we wande nsum vi kè suuyen se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: [The women went to Peter and his companions and gave a brief account of all they had been told.]

Oku_Text: 10 Se cybame ten Jesus se wene ngen tum ghon-eyfel nə wen ge é ndu əblik əbjim ɛ mbi nè sakghe ǝ Feyin we ǝb jia yî mey, sakghe we ǝb fóon eyboy yɛ εγ jia yî mey.]
English_NIV_Equivalent: [After this, Jesus himself sent out through them from east to west the sacred and imperishable message of eternal salvation.]

Oku_Text: [9Se lu ɛ bvǝse bvəse e kechii ké mbiye se ǝbwey, Jesus loole se cykuo, è keete din ngen a wen se Meɛle Magdalene we ǝb nè jumen tefim saamba ɛ wen ǝbghun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: [When Jesus rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.

Oku_Text: 10 Meele ndu è fekte se ghon-eyfel nə Jesus bene fe ghene nè luun, soo ketuu, diye,
English_NIV_Equivalent: She went and told those who had been with him and who were mourning and weeping.

Oku_Text: 11 Ghene yio ge Jesus shi ǝbtaa, gese nè ge ǝb kè lòó yen wen, dî è jia beeme kenǝǝ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it.

Oku_Text: 12 Jesus ney gese din ngeŋ ə wen & jii eylee ndun se ghon-eyfel nə wen clee ɛbaa ne ghene lii nduu əbkwak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Afterward Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking in the country.

Oku_Text: 13 Ghene me fiile è ndu è fekte se kembɛke ghele ngoo, dî ghene jia beeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: These returned and reported it to the rest; but they did not believe them either.

Oku_Text: 14 Jesus ney me din ngeŋ ə wen se ghon-eyfel na wen ghe njio eymok eyvəm, ne ghene yie, è yol ghene bek, ka ghene nè báà fo sytem se wen, gese taa ketuu, ka ghene nè báà beeme ge ghel ghɛ é nè yɛnen wen suuye ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Later Jesus appeared to the Eleven as they were eating; he rebuked them for their lack of faith and their stubborn refusal to believe those who had seen him after he had risen.

Oku_Text: 15ǝb me suuy se ghene ge: Ghen jel mbi eyjim è fekte sakghe ǝbjun se wel ǝbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.

Oku_Text: 16 Naa nde we ǝb yion, è fo eytem, è fii moo náà boy, dî ne wel yio è jia fo eytem, é ǝb náà lɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.

Oku_Text: 17Ghel ghe é foon eytem nêy yɛ ki ǝbchǝyte vin ge; ghene nêy ye jume tefim e ǝbtaawe syghel nə me, surjnen telam tefee
English_NIV_Equivalent: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues;

Oku_Text: 18 Wele ghal yio, kene əb nəə nlik, é kenǝǝ jia kooy se wen. Ghene nêy ye lem eghoo ε ghel syafkene ǝbtuu, ne ghene bonene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: they will pick up snakes with their hands; and when they drink deadly poison, it will not hurt them at all; they will place their hands on sick people, and they will get well."

Oku_Text: 19 Báàba Jesus sunnen ghene ghon-eyfel nə wen è meyse, Feyine me lie wen e eyyio, ǝb diom e wen ǝbkoy ǝbtaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and he sat at the right hand of God.

Oku_Text: 20 Ghon-cyfel nə wene me se jele ǝblik ǝbjim fekte sakghe ǝbjuŋ. Báàba se giamten ghene se din ge kenəə kɛ ghene suuyen lu ǝbluuy, ne ghene gese nɛɛye əbnəə əbchio ǝbyúméné.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed his word by the signs that accompanied it.)

Oku_Text: Sakghɛ əbjuŋ ka LUK nè Nyaken Sakghɛ əbjuŋ ka Luke nè Nyakene dine ge Jesus lu mboyse ye Feyine nè kaken ge ey yí boyse ghele Islael è gese boyse ghel ejim ɛ mbi fɛn.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Many have undertaken to draw up an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us,

Oku_Text: Luk dine ge Keyioy Kelanene nè tum Jesus ge ab gwiy è ye fekte sakghɛ əbjuŋ se ghele mbee. Luk se dine tenki ngek se ghele yénen ne Jesus chame eysen ə ghen. Kenaa kɛ ke nduun ɛ mbiy nŋwaale fɛn lu eysanjle ye Feyine foon se ghele nɛ ke lu ɛ nchaay se mbiye se suŋnen kum eykwiy ə Jesus gese lu ɛ nchaay se kenlaysene se suŋnen kum eykok ye Jesus nè koken se eyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of the word.

Oku_Text: Lu ɛ sakghɛ əbjuŋ fɛn nɛ Luke loote kunte kenəə ke kum eyjem, nɛ eyfɛl ə Keyioy Kelanene, nè eygiämten ye əbkiye nè foon se Jesus ɛ wen ə eyfcl gese nè jii ye Feyine leysen mbeese ghel ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: With this in mind, since I myself have carefully investigated everything from the beginning, I too decided to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,

Oku_Text: əbnəə we əb luun ɛ ŋwaale fɛn Eychakte 1.1-4 É fekte eybii Joon Nfoo Moo ghene ye Jesus 1.5–2.52 Eyfcl ə Joon Nfoo Moo 3.1-20 Jesus fii moo, Satan Momse Wen 3.21–4.13 Eyfcl ə Jesus ɛ Galilee 4.14–9.50 Jesus lòò se Galilee è ndu ɛ Jolesalem 9.51–19.27 Əbweye kenlaysen na Jesus ɛ Jolesalem 19.28–23.56 Bààba loole se eykuo è din ngen ə wen, è kok se eyyio 24.1-53
English_NIV_Equivalent: so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.

Oku_Text: Nè lu abliy wɛ abfon wɛ Helod nè saken eylake Judea, ne wele ntanle ablee lu nɛ ɛ́ tonte ge Zakalia. Əb lu ɛ kenon ə ghele ntanle kɛ Abija. Əb nè ɛ̀ abvii nɛ ɛ́ tonte ge Elizabet, ne lu wán ə Aalon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah; his wife Elizabeth was also a descendant of Aaron.

Oku_Text: Zakalia ghene Elizabet nè lu ghel ejunje ɛyshic na a Feyin naa tejim, gese ki wiy jas.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Both of them were righteous in the sight of God, observing all the Lord’s commands and decrees blamelessly.

Oku_Text: Dî nè ghene nè báá se bii, nɛ ghene ka Elizabet nè báá se bii, nɛ ghene gesemen ngesen naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they were childless because Elizabeth was not able to conceive, and they were both very old.

Oku_Text: Naa se lu ɛ kechii kelee abwey we kenon ə Zakalia nè fɛlɛn ɛ ndaa-eykokse Feyin, Zakalia se siyse ntanle,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once when Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,

Oku_Text: ka ɛ́ nè kenjenmen ɛ ndaa-eykokse Feyin, Zakalia me lii è se nyoke kɛley.
English_NIV_Equivalent: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

Oku_Text: Əbliye nè ɛ̀ abfon wɛ nɛ luun fo nyok kɛley ɛ chok wɛn ge ab lii è se baate se abchio ndaa-eykokse, è baate se nyoke keley.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside.

Oku_Text: È loo sasen nɛ nchiy-ndaa-Feyin tiy ɛ eybee abkoy abtaa se ketatan kɛ əb nè nyoken keley.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.

Oku_Text: Kelame gwel Zakalia, ab me se fane naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear.

Oku_Text: Di nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi me suuy ge: Zakalia! Ghàà yɛ fan. Feyine yiomen ɛyjem ə yiɛ, nɛ Elizabet wiy ə vie nɛy bii wán əbluumen, è wè chio eyghel ə wen ge Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the angel said to him: "Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to call him John.

Oku_Text: É nɛy bii wán nɛ̀ vin è wè suŋle naa se ŋaŋ, nɛ ghel se eydio lake;
English_NIV_Equivalent: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth,

Oku_Text: bek ka əb nɛy ye ki fekeey fɛngantene eyshic na Feyine. Əb jia nàà kum amnduk əmtaa se wen abchio naa se nkan. Keyioy Kelanene nɛy lun se wen eyte mɛ è dye jii jii se wen nɛ əb bo bɛɛy kɛn ɛ no ə wen əblɛɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born.

Oku_Text: Əb nàà kuy ɛ fekte kenəə ə Feyin se ghele Islaele, è ghel se eyidie se kubsen tetem ə ghen, è kase fiife se Bààba Feyin na ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He will bring back many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.

Oku_Text: Feyine ka nè fò kediak se wen ken ghee-ghee se Elija se ammən, ge əb ye ndu ɛ Feyine mbiy, è bense tetem əbáá, ɛ́ ye koŋe ghon ə ghen, è bense ghel ghɛ ɛ́ yionen wiy se Feyine ɛ ghene keele Feyin, è gese siyse ghel ghɛ ɛ́ báá beeme Feyin ɛ ghene beeme, è ye lu kebataen se fiisene wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the parents to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord."

Oku_Text: Zakalia bif se nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi ge: Lóó mɛ keele se ghe, ne mɛ ngesenmen sɛ?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Zechariah asked the angel, "How can I be sure of this? I am an old man and my wife is well along in years."

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa-fise ge: Lu mɛ Gabliel, nɛ mɛ fɛle sakghɛ əbjuŋ yin se wɛ̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The angel said to him, "I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to tell you this good news.

Oku_Text: Yen-a! Wɛ̀ ye lu kechichi se chin ka abnəə beeme ntum yin mɛ gwiye n se wɛ̀ báá beeme tenc nɛy gan ɛ əbliy wɛ Feyine choken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And now you will be silent and not able to speak until the day this happens, because you did not believe my words, which will come true at their appointed time."

Oku_Text: Ka Zakalia ghene nchiy-ndaa-Feyine suŋnen nduun se əbtam di,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he stayed so long in the temple.

Oku_Text: è chiyte ge əb beene moo leey fane, kante ge əb se beene moo lɔɔ-a. Əb nè nɛy fày è baksen ghel ghi, è loo ge yi se suŋnene ke me ghak. Əb me gok kechiichi, è se feke ken əbnəə feken. Ghele yen di lu nɛ koo ge kuumese Feyine di lu nɛ koo wen ɛ ndaa-eykokse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he came out, he could not speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he kept making signs to them but remained unable to speak.

Oku_Text: Zakalia ncy meyse abwey eyfcl na wen, è me kase kfəəy se wen ə bey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When his time of service was completed, he returned home.

Oku_Text: Əbliye se keŋte nduu, Elizabet we wiy ə wen me chia nè əblɛɛ, è jia se lete ngen yen ɛ mame ndaa, è suuye ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion.

Oku_Text: Yibey! Bààba lu nɛ ab cham eysen ə yom, kechii kwen əb chakse kenghuumen se mɛ ketuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "The Lord has done this for me," she said. "In these days he has shown his favor and taken away my disgrace among the people."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Elizabet nè njoŋ ntuufe, əblɛɛ ə wen vi è chin njoŋ ntuufe, Feyine me gese tum nchiy-ndaa na wen ye Gabliel se ntek ə Nazalet ɛ Galilee,
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a town in Galilee,

Oku_Text: ge əb ndu è teefe ntum se wán wɛ abluumen, nɛ ɛ́ tonte ye ab beey ghàà na abfon we David.
English_NIV_Equivalent: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.

Oku_Text: Nchiy ndaa nèy ye lu wiy ə Josef we wel eykfəə na abfon we David. Nchiy ndaa nèy ye lu wiy ə lii, è chakte Mɛɛle ge: Mɛ chakte wɛ̀! Bààba toknen nè wɛ̀, nɛ əb gesemen bose wɛ̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The angel went to her and said, "Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you."

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle yio, ke se noo eytem ə wen naa se ŋaŋ, ab me se kante ge se lu eynkii eychakte eyke le-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi me suuy se wen ge: Mɛɛle! Ghàà yɛ fan, Feyine lu nɛ Fe buk wɛ̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary; you have found favor with God.

Oku_Text: Yee ke ge wè nɛy chia nè əblɛɛ, è bii wán ge wè nɛy chia nè əblɛɛ, è bii wán əbluumen, è chio eyghel ə wen ge Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you are to call him Jesus.

Oku_Text: Əb nèy ye lu wel əbtaa, nɛ Feyin fengaŋtene toŋte wen ge: Wán ə wom, é Bààba Feyin nèy gese fo abtaawe se wen, è ab kok ketie è ye sak əbfon ken ka Ba eykfəə na ghen wɛ David nè saken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David,

Oku_Text: Əb nàà kok əbfon ɛ eysak ə wene jia yi mɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and he will reign over Jacob’s descendants forever; his kingdom will never end."

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle yio di, è me bif se nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi ge: Lòó ke gan di lɛɛy, nɛ mɛ beey ghàà yɛ abluumen?
English_NIV_Equivalent:
"How will this be," Mary asked the angel, "since I am a virgin?""
1801,Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin fise se wen ge: Keyioy Kelanene nèy gwiy se wɛ̀

Oku_Text: Bek kii nè kine, è nàà ye toŋte wán nè vin ge: Wán ə Feyin. Yen-a! Wele ndaa na ghen wɛ Elizabet wɛ ɛ́ nè suuyen ge əb lu kekan nè əblɛɛ ɛ leenen ab luun ɛ njoŋ ntuufe, nè nɛy bii wán ge kenəə nè wiy kè lòó ke ghak Feyin ge nɛɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Even Elizabeth your relative is going to have a child in her old age, and she who was said to be unable to conceive is in her sixth month.

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle me fise ge: Mɛ lu wel eyfcl na Feyine. Ke ko gan san ken ka wè suuyen. Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi me lòò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "I am the Lord’s servant," Mary answered. "May your word to me be fulfilled." Then the angel left her.

Oku_Text: Se lu ken ɛ amchii fo, Mɛɛle me lòò è lie jii è kok chikiy-chikiy ɛ ntek eylee eylake kfanse ɛ Judea.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that time Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea,

Oku_Text: Əb kok di è lii ɛ Zakalia ɛ ndaa, è chakte Elizabet.
English_NIV_Equivalent: where she entered Zechariah’s home and greeted Elizabeth.

Oku_Text: Ken ka Elizabet yion eychakte Mɛɛle ɛ leghe, wán me ghayle ɛ mɛm na ghene. Keyioy Kelanene me gwiy è lii è lun se Elizabet eytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

Oku_Text: Əb suuy se Mɛɛle ɛ gie eytaa ge: Feyine bosemen wè chia ka ab bosen əbkiy ɛjim, è gese bose sɛy wán wɛ wè nɛy bii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In a loud voice she exclaimed: "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!

Oku_Text: Lu eynkii mbukma eyke yin lɛ, ye Feyine nɛɛyen ge No Báába ə wom gwiy è ghan mɛ?
English_NIV_Equivalent: But why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

Oku_Text: Wè kè lii è chakte mɛ ɛ leghe, wán wɛ ab luun ɛ mɛ əblɛɛ me ghayle nè eysanjlee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.

Oku_Text: Ke lu kejuŋ se wè bek ka wè beemenmen ge Báába Feyin lunse nkakma ye əb nè kaken wè so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Blessed is she who has believed that the Lord would fulfill his promises to her!"

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle fise ge: Eytem ə yome kokse Bààba Feyin,
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Mary said: "My soul glorifies the Lord

Oku_Text: nɛ mɛ yio abjuŋ nɛ eytem ə yome, saŋle bek Feyin wɛ mboyse yom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,

Oku_Text: Əb lu nɛ əb kuumemen mɛ, ne mɛ lu wel eyfel na wene wɛ̀ nchek. Se lu nɛ ghel ejime nàà yɛ toŋte mɛ ge: Wel wɛ Feyine nè bosen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant. From now on all generations will call me blessed,

Oku_Text: Bek ka Feyin wɛ abtaawe nɛɛyenmen əbnəə abghakene se mɛ, eyghel ə wene lu eylanen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for the Mighty One has done great things for me—holy is his name.

Oku_Text: Əb koosen eysen ablim abjim, se ghel ejime ghɛ ɛ́ ngvamlen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His mercy extends to those who fear him, from generation to generation.

Oku_Text: Əb dinmen abtaawe ə wen, è sanse ghel ghɛ ɛ́ kwaaten ɛ ghene tetem ge ghene lu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has performed mighty deeds with his arm; he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts.

Oku_Text: Əb saksemen tefon se ghene kabase, è tom ghele ghɛ ncheke se tefon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has brought down rulers from their thrones but has lifted up the humble.

Oku_Text: Əb yiesemen ghel ghɛ jeŋe yafen nè əbfua abjune, è jumte ghɛ amkaa eghoo yum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty.

Oku_Text: Əb lu nɛ əb kuume eykak yɛ əb nè foon se ɛbaa ɛbaa ghese, è gwiye se giamtene wel eyfel na wene wɛ Islacl.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has helped his servant Israel, remembering to be merciful

Oku_Text: Ka əb nè kaken ge yi nàà koosen eysen se Ablaham, ghene eykfəə wene əbliy abjim nɛ eymeye wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: to Abraham and his descendants forever, just as he promised our ancestors."

Oku_Text: Mɛɛle suŋnen di è chii ghene Elizabet ka njoŋ setal, è kase fiile se wene ndaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.

Oku_Text: Əbliy wɛ Elizabete bii ɛ ten nɛy kwen, əb me bii wán əbluumen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When it was time for Elizabeth to have her baby, she gave birth to a son.

Oku_Text: Ghel ghɛ ɛ́ chiin báksen se wen gese nè ghel eykfəə na wene yio ka Feyine chamen eysen ə wen è fo wán se wen, è me gwiy se chekle ghene ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they shared her joy.

Oku_Text: Əbchii lun eyfaame ghele gwiyke se neyte wán vi, è me se kenje ge ɛ́ chio eyghel ə wen ge Zakalia ka yɛ ba ə wene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him after his father Zechariah,

Oku_Text: Di no ə wen suuy ge: Engaŋ, eyghel ə wene lu Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but his mother spoke up and said, "No! He is to be called John."

Oku_Text: Ghele suuy se wen ge: Wɛ̀ ki wiy wel eykfəə wɛ ɛ́ toŋten ge Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They said to her, "There is no one among your relatives who has that name."

Oku_Text: É mɛ se feke nè eghoo se Ba ə wan, bife se wen ge əb se keŋe ge ɛ́ ye toŋte wán ə wen ge ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they made signs to his father, to find out what he would like to name the child.

Oku_Text: Zakalia fek ge ɛ́ gwiy nè kebaŋe fekake. É gwiy so, əb nyak ge: Eyghel ə wene lu ge Joon. Ghene yen di è me se lu nè abchiö əbyúméné.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He asked for a writing tablet, and to everyone’s astonishment he wrote, "His name is John."

Oku_Text: Ken ka əb nyaken ɛ leghe, əb me kase kèètè se ye suŋnen, kokse Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue set free, and he began to speak, praising God.

Oku_Text: Ghel ghɛ ɛ́ nè chiin báksen se wen yen abnəə nè vin, eyfane koo ghene. Əbnəə we tene abjim me saŋken, ghele se ficlc nduu so eylake kfanse ɛ Judea eyjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: All the neighbors were filled with awe, and throughout the hill country of Judea people were talking about all these things.

Oku_Text: Wel abjim we əb nè yion sakghc vin se kanteɛ, bife ge wan vin se nàà ye lu eynkii wel əbkc-a? Ghele yen ge keghoo ə Feyine lu ɛ wene kenəə.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who heard this wondered about it, asking, "What then is this child going to be?" For the Lord’s hand was with him.

Oku_Text: Zakalia keete se suŋnen di, Keyioy Kelanene lii è lun se wen ə eytem, əb me se fekte kenəə ə Feyin ge;
English_NIV_Equivalent: His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:

Oku_Text: Éykokse lu se Bay Feyin we Feyin ə ghele Islael. Əb gwiymen se giamtene tene ghene wel abtaa we
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has come to his people and redeemed them.

Oku_Text: Əb tinsemen wel abtaa we ab nàà ye lu mboyse ə yesen, nè əb lòò eykfəə na abfon we David we wel eyfcl na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David

Oku_Text: Lu kenəə kɛ əb nè kaken se ammaan, è chia ɛ giese ge ghele ntum na Feyine nè fekten ge,
English_NIV_Equivalent: (as he said through his holy prophets of long ago),

Oku_Text: yi nàà fii ghesen se eghoo na ghel ə abbaanese na ghesene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us—

Oku_Text: Əb nè suuy ge yi nàà koosen eysen se ɛbaa ghese əb nè lek se ba ə wes we Ablaham ge.
English_NIV_Equivalent: to show mercy to our ancestors and to remember his holy covenant,

Oku_Text: Yi nàà fii ghes se eghoo na ghele abbaanese na ghes, ka ghese ye fɛl se yi bo fane wiy,
English_NIV_Equivalent: the oath he swore to our father Abraham: to rescue us from the hand of our enemies, and to enable us to serve him without fear

Oku_Text: lu ghel elanene, nɛye ka yi koŋen, abchii wes abjim, ke ge yi yene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.

Oku_Text: Wán, é nàà ye toŋte wɛ̀ ge: Wele ntum na Bààba əbnganten, bek ge wɛ̀ nèy ye nduu ɛ mbiy na Bay, siyse jii ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And you, my child, will be called a prophet of the Most High; for you will go on before the Lord to prepare the way for him,

Oku_Text: Wɛ̀ nèy ye nɛɛye nɛ ghele keele ka lòó Feyine leyse mbeese ə ghen, è boyse ghene,
English_NIV_Equivalent: to give his people the knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins,

Oku_Text: bek ge, Feyin ə fcsene lóó ab ye koosen eysen se ghele se ŋaŋ. Əb nàà tum mboyse eyyio é ka chuy ye ey tòòn ɛ bvase bvase,
English_NIV_Equivalent: because of the tender mercy of our God, by which the rising sun will come to us from heaven

Oku_Text: è fo eyban se ghele ghii dii ghɛ ɛ́ luun ɛ nfiine, chii fane eykuo əbliy abjim, è gese ye dine jii se ghes ka lóó ghes ye chii eyzəəte.
English_NIV_Equivalent: to shine on those living in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the path of peace."

Oku_Text: Zakalia suŋnen di, wan ə wen ko me se kuye, ki eytof, foo eytem ə wen se Feyine naa ketaaan, è me ndu ɛ se chii abkwak se chin ka əb nè kerten se ye fekte kenəə ə Feyin ɛ Islael.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And the child grew and became strong in spirit; and he lived in the wilderness until he appeared publicly to Israel.

Oku_Text: Nè lu abliy wɛ nè biin Joon ɛ ten, Augustus we abfon ə Lûm chiase nchil ge ghel ejim ghe ɛ́ nè chiin ɛ əbtum we ab nè luun ɛ eyshaa eysake yɛ eylake Lûme ndu è nyak teghel ə ghen se ŋwaale ə kentaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.

Oku_Text: Kine nè lu kentanje ké mbiye se lie kelik. Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Kwelinius sake Silia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (This was the first census that took place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.)

Oku_Text: Ghele yio di, è me nduke se ghene ntekse, ɛ́ nyak teghel ə ghen se ŋwaale kentan j.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And everyone went to their own town to register.

Oku_Text: Josef lòò sɛy r Nazalet ɛ Galilee, è ndu ɛ ntek yɛ ɛ́ nè biin abfon wɛ David ɛ ten, nè tonte ge, Betlehem ɛ mame Judea, bek ka əb nè luun fo ghene Mɛɛle we kensiyse nè luun ge əb nèy ye lu wiy ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David.

Oku_Text: Ghene nè nduu kentan kin di nɛ Mɛɛle lu nɛ əblɛɛ. Se lu ka ghene nè luun ɛ Betlehem, əbliye me kwen wɛ Mɛɛle bii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him and was expecting a child. While they were there, the time came for the baby to be born,

Oku_Text: Əb me bii wán ə wenɛ wé mbiy, ne lu wán əbluumen. É limte wen nè ndayse, è nyinjse se kenak kɛ nyamsc sɛɛ yiɛ ɛ ten, bek ka kelike nè báá se lu se ghene ɛ keberle.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and she gave birth to her firstborn, a son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no guest room available for them.

Oku_Text: Se lu ken nè ntake fo, nɛ gay selee lu abkwak ɛ eyghane Betlehem toknen nè bvayse ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin me tum è fay ɛ ghene eyshie, eyban ə Feyine baa se naŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi me suuy se ghene ge: Ghàà ghen yɛ fan. Mɛ gwiy nè ntum eyjun ye ey gwiy nè eysanjlee eyhakene se wel abjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the angel said to them, "Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people.

Oku_Text: É biimen Mboyse ɛ ntek na abfon wɛ David ɛ kechii ké lane. Əb lu Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen. Əb lu Bààba.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is the Messiah, the Lord.

Oku_Text: Əbchayte we ghene nèy yen se geese keele ge wan nè vin lu wán wɛ me suuyen lu ge, ghene nèy yen wán nè ɛ́ limte nè ndayse, è nyinjse ɛ kenak kɛ nyamsc sɛɛ yiɛ ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger."

Oku_Text: Ka əb suŋnen è meyse ɛ leghe, ghene sasen, è me yen ne nchiy-ndaa-Feyin yi shi fo ghene selee naa se eydio, yeme njaŋ eykokse se Feyine, suuye ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Suddenly a great company of the heavenly host appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,

Oku_Text: “Eykokse lu se Feyine eyyio, nɛ eyzəəte lu ɛ ketuu ntic, se ghel ghɛ Feyine koŋen.”
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace to those on whom his favor rests."

Oku_Text: Nchiyse-ndaa na Feyine shii yem di è kase se fiile se eyyio ɛ leghe, gay shii me suuy se ngenjse ghene ge: Ghesen diaŋ ɛ Betlehem, è yen kenəə kin kɛ ke ganen, kɛ Feyine nɛɛyen ghesen keele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, "Let’s go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about."

Oku_Text: Ghene me fee-feen nè kenyujene, è dian ɛ Betlehem, è yen Mɛɛle ghene Josef, è gese yen wán ka ɛ́ nyinjse ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby, who was lying in the manger.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghene yenen wán vi di, ghene kum wán se fiɛl əbnəə abjim wè nchiy-ndaa-Feyin ké suuyen se ghene kum wán nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they had seen him, they spread the word concerning what had been told them about this child,

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim ghɛ ɛ́ nè yion sakghc vin se gay shii nè fiɛlen nè lò se lu nè abchiö əbyúméné.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.

Oku_Text: Se lu se Mɛɛle, nɛ əb yio abnəə nè vin naa əbjm, è lem se wen ə eytem, è se kwaate kum abghene naa se ŋan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.

Oku_Text: Gay shi me kase fiile, è se yeme kokse Feyin se əbnəə wè ken eyghcl ka nchiy-ndaa-Feyin nè suuyen se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had heard and seen, which were just as they had been told.

Oku_Text: Se lu eybam abchii eyfaame se ka è biin wán nè vin, è me neyte wen, è chio eyghel ə wen ge Jesus, ken eyghcl ka nchiy-ndaa-Feyin nè foon ne no ə wen geese chia nè əblɛɛ ə wene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On the eighth day, when it was time to circumcise the child, he was named Jesus, the name the angel had given him before he was conceived.

Oku_Text: Əbliye ncy me kwen wɛ ba ə wan ghene no ə wane lanse ngenge ghen, ken ka nchil ə Moses nè chioten bek wán we ghene nè biin. Josef ghene Mɛɛle me lie Jesus, è ɛ Jolesalem, se foo wen se Bay Feyin,
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the time came for the purification rites required by the Law of Moses, Joseph and Mary took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord

Oku_Text: nɛ lu ka ɛ́ nè nyaken ɛ ŋwaale-Feyin ge: “Wel bii wánè wɛ̌ mbiy nɛ lu wán əbluumen, fene əb lu se lose wán ten se fo se Bay Feyin.”
English_NIV_Equivalent: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, "Every firstborn male is to be consecrated to the Lord")

Oku_Text: Ghene gese fo sɛy eyfio se Feyine, se kase se ye lu kelaanen ɛ eyshie na Feyine, ken ka ɛysak ɛ Bay Feyine nè dinen. Eysake tene nè dine ge: Lòó wel fó abnlal əbbua, kene nŋvəə sebaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and to offer a sacrifice in keeping with what is said in the Law of the Lord: "a pair of doves or two young pigeons."

Oku_Text: Nè lu abliy fo nè wel əblee lu ɛ Jolesalem nè ɛ́ tonte ge, Simeon. Wel nè vin nè lò se nɛɛy əbnəə wè Feyine koŋen, kokse wen əbliy abjim, chiyte əbliy wè Feyine nèy koosen eysen se ghel eylake Islael, nɛ Keyioy Kelanene lu ɛ wen ə eytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.

Oku_Text: Nè lu di nè Keyioy Kelanene kak wen ge, əb jia nàà kuo buu ge əb yen Vi we ghene Mɛɛle nè nduun nè Jesus se ndaa-eykokse, se neey abnəə vin ka eysake dinen, Keyioy Kelanene neey ge Simeon ndu se ndaa-eykokse fo ken əbliy fo sɛy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.

Oku_Text: Əb ndu è chin fo, è me fii wán ten, è koyten è se foo eykokse se Feyine, suuye ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Moved by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what the custom of the Law required,

Oku_Text: Oo Báába, wè lu nè wè lunse nkakma ə yiɛ san, ne koŋ è wè vase mɛ, we wel eyfel na wè ɛ leenen, è mɛ kuo nè amnyiaŋe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying: "Sovereign Lord, as you have promised, you may now dismiss your servant in peace.

Oku_Text: Mɛ lu nɛ mɛ loote yen nè eshiɛ ə ghome, ka wè ncy boyse ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For my eyes have seen your salvation,

Oku_Text: Wè siysemen mboyse, è tinsen ka lòó wel abjim yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: which you have prepared in the sight of all nations:

Oku_Text: Əb lu kekaaken se əbtume ka lòó ghene keele wè, nè əb nèy lose eyghele ghel ə ghiɛ ghɛ Islael.
English_NIV_Equivalent: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and the glory of your people Israel."

Oku_Text: Josef ghene Mɛɛle yio kenkaa ke Simeon nè kaan ɛ wan ə ghene ketuu, abchiö yúm ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The child’s father and mother marveled at what was said about him.

Oku_Text: Simeon fó eybose se ghene, è suuy se Mɛɛle we no ə wan ghan ge: Yen-a, wan vin lu nè chok Feyin, ne əb nèy neey è ghele Islael loole se eydio, è nèy è elee saake saaken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother: "This child is destined to cause the falling and rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,

Oku_Text: Əb nèy gese ye lu əbchayte wè lòòn se Feyine, nɛ ndio ghele tunse tunse wen. Ka ghene tunse wen di, è tekwaate ghen te ghene leten ɛ ghene tetem me yensen ɛ ndan. Eysene wan ə vie nè vin nèy neey, è eytem ə yiɛ ye koo di ka é soo nè fefiake.
English_NIV_Equivalent: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your own soul too."

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nè abvii əblee lu ɛ Jolesalem ɛ́ tonte ge, Anna, ne lu wán ə Fanuel, we wel eykfəə na Asha. Əbvii nè vin nè lu nɛ əb ngesenmen naa se ŋjaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There was also a prophet, Anna, the daughter of Penuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old; she had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,

Oku_Text: Ghene lum ə wen nè chii ken nguk saamba, lu wiy ə nkfəə se yiake ka ab nè chinen nguk njio-sekeek amfaame, è jia yi gok se ndaa-eykokse naa kechii kemok, nduu, foo eykokse se Feyine nè nchuy gese nɛ ntake, bame abfua-abyúéné, jeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and then was a widow until she was eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.

Oku_Text: Se lu ken ka Simeon nè suŋnen se Josef ghene Mɛɛle, əbvii vi me kokte gwiye, è bèèlè Feyin, è suŋnen kum wán wɛ Jesus se ghel ejime fo ghɛ ɛ́ nè chiyten abliy wè Feyine nèy fayse Jolesalem se ngekse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Coming up to them at that very moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Josef ghene Mɛɛle meyse neey naa əbnəə abjim, ken eyghel ka eysak ə Bààba Feyine dinen, è me kase fiile se ghene ntek, r Nazalet ɛ Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.

Oku_Text: Jesus kuy, è se lu wán əbtaa, nɛ eytofe lun se wen ə ketuu, nɛ Feyine gese koŋe wen naa ɛ jii eylee nduu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.

Oku_Text: Ba ə wen ghene no ə wen nè sec nduu ɛ Jolesalem nguk sejim, se yie Kechii Eyboyse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Festival of the Passover.

Oku_Text: Jesus nèy chin nguk njio-sebaa eyvam, è Jolesalem se yie Kechii Eyboyse ka nchiinen eylak na ghen nè dinen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he was twelve years old, they went up to the festival, according to the custom.

Oku_Text: É meyse eyyie Kechii Eyboyse ki, Josef ghene Mɛɛle loo fiile se Nazalet, Jesus me gok ɛ Jolesalem, ne ba ə wen ghene no ə wen ke ge wan lu ɛ ghene kenon, è nèy wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After the festival was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.

Oku_Text: Ghene jel se kechii keful ko loo kiy ɛ mɛm na ghene ndaa na ghene, gese nè sunse ghene, è jia se kenje wen ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Thinking he was in their company, they traveled on for a day. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.

Oku_Text: Əbchii chia abtal ghene ndintese ɛ ndaa-eykokse, è se yiote əbnəə we ghene dinten, gese bifte tebif se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they did not find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him. After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim ghɛ ɛ́ nè yioten əbnəə ə wen gese nè tefise ə wene nè lò se lu nè abchiö əbyúméné ka əb loote keen əbnəə, gese fise tebif kejuŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who heard him was amazed at his understanding and his answers.

Oku_Text: Ka no ə wen ghene ba ə wen yenen wen, kelame gwel ghene, wè nɛɛy ge: Wan ə wom, wɛ̀ neey ghes kin se ghes lɛ? Ghese ba ə vie kè luŋe keŋe wɛ̀ naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When his parents saw him, they were astonished. His mother said to him, "Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you."

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghen kè keŋe mɛ se ghe? Ghen kè báá se ke ge mɛ kè lu se ye lu ɛ ndaa na ba ə wom fen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Why were you searching for me?" he asked. "Didn't you know I had to be in my Father’s house?"

Oku_Text: Di Josef ghene Mɛɛle jia keele kenəə kɛ əb nè suuyen se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they did not understand what he was saying to them.

Oku_Text: Jesus ghene no ə wen, nè ba ə wen me lòò, è chio se Nazalet. Jesus se yionen se ghene naa kejuŋ. No ə wen se ki eywaate abnəə we ab nè suuyen ɛ wen eytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But his mother treasured all these things in her heart.

Oku_Text: Jesus gese kuye se wen eytof, nɛ Feyine koŋe wen, nɛ ghel ghɛ ɛ́ nè keen wen gese koŋe wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.

Oku_Text: Oku_Text
English_NIV_Equivalent: English_NIV_Equivalent

Oku_Text: Nè lu ka Tibelius nè sakenmen ka əbfon ə Lûm se nguk njio setan eyvam, nè Bontius Bailet sake ɛ wen eyshas eylake Judea, ne ko nè lu ɛ ngukse fo nɛ Helod Antibas sake ɛ Galilee, ne wan-no wene Filib sake ɛ Itulea ghene Tlakonitis, ne Lisanias sake ɛ Abilene,
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar—when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Iturea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene—

Oku_Text: ne Annas ghene Kaiafas lu abtuu se ghé ntanjlee eylake Judea, Feyine tinse ntum se Joon wɛ wán ə Zakalia ɛ əbkwak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: during the high-priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.

Oku_Text: əb me se ghane ɛ ntaame yɛ abghooye jio Joldan fekte se ghele ge: Ghen kubsen tetem ə ten è fiile se eybame mbee é é fò moo se ghen, ka Feyine leyse mbee sen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He went into all the country around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

Oku_Text: Ke nè gane ken ka wele ntum na Feyine wɛ Ezaya nè nyakenmen ge: "Yio ka giɛ wele janen ɛ əbkwak ge: Siyse jii se Báǎba neyte kevəə jii se wen é ke yɛ nduu ɛ titiy!
English_NIV_Equivalent: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet: "A voice of one calling in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.

Oku_Text: È lunse tefian tejim, è kɛs tetaŋ ghene kfən sejim é se yɛ lu mbok. È tiyse jii se ngoten é se ye lu ɛ titiy, è lunse tebee é jiise yɛ lu ɛ mbok.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Every valley shall be filled in, every mountain and hill made low. The crooked roads shall become straight, the rough ways smooth.

Oku_Text: È ghel ejime yen ka Feyine boysen ghel."
English_NIV_Equivalent: And all people will see God’s salvation.'"

Oku_Text: Ghel ejime nè lǒ fày è gwiy ge Joon fo moo se ghene. Joon suuy se ghene ge: Ghone yiose, suuy ndɛ se ghen ge lòó ghen nyinj è boy se ngek se Feyine shiin se mak se ghele?
English_NIV_Equivalent: John said to the crowds coming out to be baptized by him, "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?

Oku_Text: Ghen ye neey əbnəə abjunje se din ge ghen kubsenmen tetem ə ten. Ghǎà ghen ye suuy se ken ngen ə sene ge: Ghes lu ghel eyshaa-ndaa na Ablaham, nɛ ghese boymen se ngekse. Mɛ suuy se ghen ge lòó Feyine bense etiy ghin fen se ghon na Ablaham.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance. And do not begin to say to yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our father.' For I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.

Oku_Text: Njam se shi eyshaa fekak kebaaten ka é seɛ fekak fe fe ken ɛ eghan. Naa fekak feke fɛ gwel è koom wiy əmtam lu nɛ é gwel è kwaay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The ax is already at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire."

Oku_Text: Kenone ghel ki yio è me bif se Joon ge: Lòó ghes neey ghɛ ɛ leenen?
English_NIV_Equivalent: "What should we do then?" the crowd asked.

Oku_Text: əb fise ge: Wel loo ki nday sebaa, é ab fo se semoke se wel we əb băǎ se ki se nkan, wele yɛ ki abfua-abyíéne, é əb fò ghel ghé ɛ băǎ se ki naa se nkan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: John answered, "Anyone who has two shirts should share with the one who has none, and anyone who has food should do the same."

Oku_Text: Naa ghel eyfii shile gwiy sêy se fii moo è bif se Joon ge: Ndinte, lòó ghes neey ghɛ?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. "Teacher," they asked, "what should we do?""

Oku_Text: əb fise ge: Ghǎà ghen ye fii shile chia ka ke luun ge ghen ye fii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Don’t collect any more than you are required to," he told them.

Oku_Text: Ghel eyghoŋ elee ko bif sêy se Joon ge: Ghǎà ghen ye fise ge: Ghǎà ghen ye kwantene ghel, səəkə əbkaa se ghene, nɛ ghǎà ghen ye toye ghel se lòlò. Ghen ye lu kekulen nè əbkaa wɛ ghene lien.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then some soldiers asked him, "And what should we do?" He replied, "Don’t extort money and don’t accuse people falsely—be content with your pay."

Oku_Text: Ka ghel ejime nè chiyten eygyiy ə Vi we Feyine nè tetem ge Joon se shi vi nè vi-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people were waiting expectantly and were all wondering in their hearts if John might possibly be the Messiah.

Oku_Text: Joon keele è me fise se ghene ge: Mɛ foo nɛ lu moo se ghen, dí nè wel əbnganten ablee nèy gwiy, nɛ mɛ fene mɛ băǎ se lu kekwenten ka lòó me yayte. əb nèy ye foo nɛ lu Keyioy Kelanene gese nè eghoo, ye
English_NIV_Equivalent: John answered them all, "I baptize you with water. But one who is more powerful than I will come, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.

Oku_Text: əb kǐ eyshen ə wen è kfiy se wen əbjwak, è baate mayse ten è nyok abjwak, è waate ɛ keghoo, ye se əbvas wɛ ab baalen əbliy abjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His winnowing fork is in his hand to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his barn, but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire."

Oku_Text: Joon nè fekte sakghe əbjuŋ nè vin le ɛ jiise jiise se ghele, janje suuye ge kiy ghene, ghene ko kubsen se ghene jii sebee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And with many other words John exhorted the people and proclaimed the good news to them.

Oku_Text: Helod nè zaamen ge ke băǎ se lu ɛ titiy se wen se kase se lie Helodias, ne lu wiy wan-no ə wen we Helod se wen əbvii, nè lǒ ko gese momle əbnəə naa se eydio we Joon nè zaamen wen se ten, è me gese koo Joon è vii se nchak, se gise se əbnəə nê ghan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when John rebuked Herod the tetrarch because of his marriage to Herodias, his brother’s wife, and all the other evil things he had done, Herod added this to them all: He locked John up in prison.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ kechii kelee, nɛ é geese koo Joon, əb fo moo se ghele Jesus loo jem, abchio eyyio dise.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When all the people were being baptized, Jesus was baptized too. And as he was praying, heaven was opened,

Oku_Text: Keyioy Kelanene saake è guo ɛ wen abghun, lu ka kenlal. Giɛ səəkə se eyyio ge: Wè lu wán ə wom wɛ mɛ koŋen, nɛ mɛ gese sanjle naa se ŋan kum wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and the Holy Spirit descended on him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven: "You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased."

Oku_Text: Jesus nè keete eyfɛl ə wen nɛ əb shi ka nguk ammtal. É nè ke ge ab lu wán ə Josef wɛ nè luun wán ə Heli.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli,

Oku_Text: Heli nè lu wán ə Mattat wɛ wán ə Levi, we nè lu wán ə Melki wɛ wán ə Jannai, we nè lu wán ə Josef.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

Oku_Text: Josef nè vin nè lu wán ə Mattattias we wán ə Amos, we nè lu wán ə Nahum wɛ wán ə Esli, we nè lu wán ə Naggai.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

Oku_Text: Naggai nè lu wán ə Maat we wán ə Mattattias, we nè lu wán ə Semein wɛ wán ə Josech wɛ nè luun wán Joda.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josech, the son of Joda,

Oku_Text: Joda nè lu wán ə Joanan we wán ə Lesa, we nè lu wán ə Zelubbabel wɛ wán ə Shealtiel, we nè lu wán Neli.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

Oku_Text: Neli lu wán ə Melki wɛ wán ə Addi, we nè luun wán ə Kosam we wán ə Elmadam wɛ nè luun wán ə El.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,

Oku_Text: El nè lu wán ə Joshua we wán ə Elieze, we nè luun wán ə Jolim we wán ə Mattat, we nè luun wán Levi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

Oku_Text: Levi nè lu wán ə Simeon we wán ə Juda, we nè luun wán ə Josef we wán ə Jonam we nè luun wán Eliakim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,

Oku_Text: Eliakim nè lu wán ə Melea, we wán ə Menna we nè luun wán ə Mattata we nè wán ə Natan, we nè luun wán ə David.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

Oku_Text: David nè lu wán ə Jesse we wán ə Obet, we nè luun wán ə Boaz we wán ə Salmon, we nè luun wán ə Nahshon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,

English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Arni, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

Oku_Text: Juda nè lu wán ə Jakob we wán ə Ayzek, we nè luun wán ə Ablaham we wán Tela, we nè luun wán ə Nahol.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,

English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

Oku_Text: Lamek nè lu wán ə Metusela we wán ə Enok, we nè luun wán ə Jaled we wán ə Mahalalel, we nè luun wán ə Kenan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Cainan,

Oku_Text: Kenan nè lu wán ə Enosh we wán ə Set, we nè luun wán ə Adam we Feyine nè bomen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

Oku_Text: Jesus me kase fiile se jio Joldan, nɛ Keyioy Kelanene nè lu ɛ wen ə eytem naa se ŋaŋ. Keyioy vi me neey Jesus, è ndu nè wen ɛ əbkwak, ²Satan se momse wen ɛ ten əbchii eyfaame sebaa. əbchii nè vin abjim, Jesus ke ke ge əb dii ken, è me jenɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, left the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness, where for forty days he was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and at the end of them he was hungry.

Oku_Text: Satan me suuy se wen ge: Lòó wè lu wán ə Feyine, suuy se etiy ghin fen ge é bense əmchio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread."

Oku_Text: Jesus fise se wen ge: É nè nyaken ɛ nwaale-Feyin ge: "Ke ge əmchio mòk nɛ wel jia mboyse wen ey-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone.'""
1934,Satan me neey Jesus

Oku_Text: Satan me suuy se wen ge: Mɛ nǎǎ ye fo abtaa nè vin abjim se wè, gese nè eyseen eyjun yê tefon nè vin, bek ka é lu nɛ əb fo ghen abjim se mɛ, nɛ mɛ fese fò se wel wɛ mɛ koŋen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he said to him, "I will give you all their authority and splendor; it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to.

Oku_Text: Lòó wè ye jame eyshic na yom, abghene nè vin abjim nèy ye lu we wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If you worship me, it will all be yours."

Oku_Text: Jesus fise se wen ge: É nè nyaken ɛ nwaale-Feyin ge: "Jame eyshic na Bay Feyin na Feyin ghen, è fɛl se wen wen-moke."
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.'""
1938,Satan me gese neey Jesus

English_NIV_Equivalent:
For it is written: 'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you carefully; they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.'""
1940,Jesus fise se wen ge: É nè nyaken ge: ""Ghǎà wè ye momse Bay Feyin na Feyin ghen."""

Oku_Text: Jesus me kase fiile se Galilee, nɛ abtaa na Keyioy Kelanene lu ɛ wen ə eytem. Sakghɛ ə wen me saanken ɛ eysak fo eyjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread through the whole countryside.

Oku_Text: əb nè fekten ghel ɛ ndaa-eyjame na ghen, ghel ejim me se kokse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He was teaching in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.

Oku_Text: Jesus me ndu ɛ Nazalet, ntek ye é nè biin wen ɛ ten. əbchii kə eyzəəte nɛ ab liì ɛ ndaa-eyjame ken ka ab nè lò neey, è me lii è siyse ge ab ye transcribe nwaale-Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom. He stood up to read,

Oku_Text: əb me fo nwaale ə wele ntum na Feyine wɛ Ezaya se wen, ab yayte, è yen eysak wɛ é nè nyaken ɛ ten ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: and the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. Unrolling it, he found the place where it is written:

Oku_Text: "Keyioy ə Bay Feyine lu ɛ mɛ ə eytem, bek ka əb nè neey mɛ ge mɛ fekte sakghɛ əbjuŋ se ghel ghé eb băǎ se ki. əb tumen mɛ ge mɛ fekte mboyse se ghel ghé é vii ghene se nchak, nese fo eysene se ghele abfua-abyíéne, è fekte ge ghel ghé é ngvəəse ghene nèy ye mboy,
English_NIV_Equivalent: "The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free,

Oku_Text: ¹⁹è fekte ge əbliy wɛ Báǎba Feyine nèy ye boyse ghel ə wen me kwen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor."

Oku_Text: Jesus kfiy nwaale vi di, è me fo se wel eyfɛl, è me jame eyshee. Ghel ejim ghé é nè luun ɛ ndaa-eyjame di toknen eysen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fastened on him.

Oku_Text: əb me keete se suuy se ghene ge: əbnəə wɛ ghen yionen di, me gan ɛ leghe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He began by saying to them, "Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing."

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim me se suuy sakghɛ əbjuŋ kum wen, è chiyte kum əbnəə əbjuŋ we ab nè suuyen, bife ge: Kenəə kɛ wán ə Josef nè vin lu wen nè-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his lips. "Isn’t this Joseph’s son?" they asked.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Mɛ keele ge ghen nèy ye suuy njǎŋ əmoke se mɛ ge: "Wele nchak boyse wè eysen. Neey əbnəə wɛ ghes yionen ge wè kì ɛ Kafelnaum, fo fen sêy ɛ ntek ə vie."
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Jesus said to them, "Surely you will quote this proverb to me: 'Physician, heal yourself!' And you will tell me, 'Do do here in your hometown what we have heard that you did in Capernaum.'""
1952,Jesus me suuy sêy ge: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge wele ntum na Feyine wiy kelaanen ɛ ntek ə wen."

Oku_Text: Ghen keele ge əbchii wɛ Elija nè luun ɛ ten, nɛ ke bɛey ghǎǎ ye chin keful njon abtal nè chin ntuufe, njon kə eyseen me koo ɛ ntie fɛn eyjim, nɛ əbkfəə nè lǒ ɛ Isiael naa se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I assure you that there were many widows in Israel in Elijah’s time, when the sky was shut for three and a half years and there was a severe famine throughout the land.

Oku_Text: Di Feyine bɛey tum Elija se wel ə Isiael mok-a, ab me tum wen se wel əbkfəə əmoke ɛ ntek ə Salefat, ntek ye Sidon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them, but to a widow in Zarephath in the region of Sidon.

Oku_Text: Gese nè əbliy wɛ wele ntum na Feyine wɛ Elisha nè luun ɛ ten, nɛ ghel ə eychii se eydio nè luun ɛ Isiael, ne wiy kelaanen kɛ əb bɛey boyse eychii ə wen mok-a, ab me boyse Naaman we wel ə Silia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And there were many in Israel with leprosy in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed—only Naaman the Syrian."

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim ghé é nè luun ɛ ndaa-eyjame di yio di, kfəl me se koo ghene naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: All the people in the synagogue were furious when they heard this.

Oku_Text: Ghene me lii è lúúse Jesus se eybvase ntek, è ndu nè wen eyyio ɛ kfən ntek fo, ge ghene yayte wen se eybvase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They got up, drove him out of the town, and took him to the brow of the hill on which the town was built, in order to throw him off the cliff.

Oku_Text: Dî Jesus me lǎǎ ɛ kebaaten ə ghene, è me ndu ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he walked right through the crowd and went on his way.

Oku_Text: Jesus me ndu ɛ ntek ə Kafelnaum ɛ Galilee. əbchii kə eyzəəte, əb me se fekten ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath he taught the people.

Oku_Text: Ghele me chiyte kum kenəə kɛ ab nè fekten, bek ka ab nè suuyen nè abtaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They were amazed at his teaching, because his words had authority.

Oku_Text: Wel əblee lu ɛ ndaa-eyjame di nɛ wele keyioy əbe lu ɛ wen ə eytem. əb me lii è ghan ɛ giɛ eytaa ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by a demon, an impure spirit. He cried out at the top of his voice,

Oku_Text: "He! Jesus we Nazalet! Wè koŋen ge wè neey ghesen ghɛ-a? Wè gwiyimen ge wè boyse ghesen-a? Mɛ keele wel wɛ wè lu: Wè lu Wel Kelanene we Feyine."
English_NIV_Equivalent:
"Go away! What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!""
1963,Jesus zaam keyioy əbe vi ge: ""Chichi! Lòò ɛ wen ə eytem!"" Keyioy əbe vi me kookse wel vi ɛ ntie eyshie na ghel ejim

Oku_Text: Kelame gwel ghel ejim, ghene me se bife se ghene-ghene ge: Kenəə kɛ wel vin lu eynkii-a? əb nè fise nè abtaa gese nè kenyinjene se teyioy əbe, è ghene lòò-a!
English_NIV_Equivalent: All the people were amazed and said to each other, "What words these are! With authority and power he gives orders to impure spirits and they come out!"

Oku_Text: Sakghɛ ə Jesus me saanken ɛ eysak fo eyjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And the news about him spread throughout the surrounding area.

Oku_Text: Jesus me lii è lòò ɛ ndaa-eyjame, è ndu ɛ ndaa na Simon. No-wiy ə Simon nè koo eychii eytaa, nɛ ghene me chakte Jesus ge ab boyse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus left the synagogue and went to the home of Simon. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Jesus to help her.

Oku_Text: Jesus me ndu è siyse wen ɛ eyshie, è zaam eychii vi, eychii vi me lòò. əbvii vi me lii sasen, è me se fɛlen ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he bent over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and began to wait on them.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka chuy nè nduu è din, ghel ejim ghé é nè koo teychii teytaa-teytaa me gwiy nè ghene se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At sunset, the people brought to Jesus all who had various kinds of sickness,

Oku_Text: Jesus me fii eghoo na wen siyse ɛ wel ejim mok-moke, è boyse ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and laying his hands on each one, he healed them.

Oku_Text: Teyioy əbe gese lòò ɛ ghel sê eydio ə eytem, ghanen ge: Wè lu wán ə Feyin!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Moreover, demons came out of many people, shouting, "You are the Son of God!"

Oku_Text: Dî Jesus me zaam ghene, ke ge ghene suuy kenəə mok-a, bek ka ghene nè keele ge əb lu Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Messiah.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ntake nè ney, Jesus me lòò è ndu ɛ əbkwak. Ghel me se kenje wen, è te yen wen, ghene me bǎǎ se beene wen ge ab lòò se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At daybreak, Jesus went out to a solitary place. The people were looking for him and when they came to where he was, they tried to keep him from leaving them.

Oku_Text: Dî Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Mɛ nǎǎ gese ye fekte sakghɛ əbjuŋ kum tefon na Feyine ɛ tefon sê eydio sêy, bek ka Feyine nè tumen mɛ ge mɛ gwiy nè eyfɛl nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he said, "I must proclaim the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns also, because that is why I was sent."

Oku_Text: əb me se fekten kenəə Feyine ɛ ndaa-eyjame ɛ eysak ə Judea eyjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea.

Oku_Text: Nè lu abliy fo nɛ Jesus nè siyse eychie na eymnduk ə Galilee, nɛ eymnduk kə eydio tatan wen, è nǎǎ koo eychie na wen ge ghene yio kenəə kɛ Feyine nè suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One day as Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, the people were crowding around him and listening to the word of God.

Oku_Text: əb me yen amnduk ambaane sebaa ɛ eychie na eymnduk, ghel eyghoŋ-te-shile di me lie ɛ ten, è se ghoŋte eynchil ə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He saw at the water’s edge two boats, left there by the fishermen, who were washing their nets.

Oku_Text: Jesus me ndu ɛ mnduk ə Simon, è chakte Simon ge ab chəək wen ken fə̀ fə̀ se eychie. Jesus me jame eyshee ɛ mnduk fo, è se fekten ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little from shore. Then he sat down and taught the people from the boat.

Oku_Text: əb meyse fekten ghel di, è me suuy se Simon ge: Chəək mnduk vin è ndu nè wen eyyio ɛ mame mnduk, è ghen yayte eynchil ə ghen ge ghen ghoŋ shile.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, "Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch."

Oku_Text: Simon fise ge: Ndinte! Ghes nè fɛl nchuy fɛn abjim, ke ke ge ghes ghoŋ mnduk. Dí bek ka wè suuyen, mɛ nǎǎ ye yayte eynchil vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Simon answered, "Master, we’ve worked hard all night and haven’t caught anything. But because you say so, I will let down the nets."

Oku_Text: Ghene yayte eynchil di, è me ghoŋte shile naa se eydio, è eynchil kə eete.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they had done so, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to break.

Oku_Text: Ghene me se feke nè eghoo se ba-shile na ghene ghé é nè luun ɛ mnduk əmoke ge ghene gwiy nè giamtene ghene. Ghene me gwiy, è me jeme amnduk ambaane di nè shile naa se ŋaŋ, è amnduk di me bǎǎ se neey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Simon Bita yenen kenəə vin di, è me ndu è jame eyshic na Jesus, suuye ge: Bay, lòò se yom, bek ka mɛ lu wel eybame.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus’ knees and said, "Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!"

Oku_Text: Simon ghene ghel ejim ghé é nè luun nè wen chiyte naa se ŋaŋ kum eyshaa shile yɛ ghene ghoŋten di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For he and all his companions were astonished at the catch of fish they had taken,

Oku_Text: Wel-shile na Simon we Jems ghene Joon ghé é nè luun teychii na Jebedi gese me chiyte. Jesus me suuy se Simon ge: Ghǎà wè ye fan. Se keete ɛ leghe wè nǎǎ ye ghoŋ ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and so were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, Simon’s partners. Then Jesus said to Simon, "Don’t be afraid; from now on you will fish for people."

Oku_Text: Ghene me chəək amnduk di ɛ eychie, è me lòò əbnəə abjim, è me koŋ Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè luun ɛ ntek əmoke, wel əblee lu ɛ ten nɛ eychii-eghoo chiì wen naa se ŋaŋ. Se lu ka ab yenen Jesus di, è me jame eyshic, è me chakte Jesus ge: Bay, lòó wè koŋen, wè fese boyse mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man came along who was covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell with his face to the ground and begged him, "Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean."

Oku_Text: Jesus me tinse eghoo, è me fii se wen, suuye ge: Mɛ koŋen. Boy-a! Ken e leghe, eychii-eghoo vi me lòò ɛ wen ə eytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean!" And immediately the leprosy left him.

Oku_Text: Jesus me zaam wen ge: Ghǎà wè ye suuy se wel əmoke. Dí ndu è se neeye wè se ntanle, è wè fò eysak se Feyine ka Moses nè chioten, ge ghele yen ge wè me boy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus ordered him, "Don’t tell anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them."

Oku_Text: Dî sakghɛ ə Jesus me saanken naa se ŋaŋ, nɛ ghel sê eydio gwiy se yio kenəə kɛ ab nè suuyen, è ghene boy ɛ teychii na ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet the news about him spread all the more, so that crowds of people came to hear him and to be healed of their sicknesses.

Oku_Text: Dî Jesus me se kase nduu ɛ əbkwak, è se yeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Jesus often withdrew to lonely places and prayed.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ kechii kelee nɛ Jesus nè fekten ghel, nɛ ghel eysak na Balisi ghene tefise te nchil nè luun ɛ njon fo, ghene gwiy sêy r Galilee, Judea, gese nè Jolesalem. Abtaa na Bay lu ɛ Jesus ə eytem ge ab ye boyse ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One day Jesus was teaching, and Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there. They had come from every village of Galilee and from Judea and Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was with him to heal the sick.

Oku_Text: Ghel əblee me gwiy nè wel eykuo-eykul ɛ kenak, ghene me bǎǎ se neey wen ɛ ndaa eyshie na Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some men came carrying a paralyzed man on a mat and tried to take him into the house to lay him before Jesus.

Oku_Text: Bek ka ghele nè eydio, ghene jia yen jii ge ghene neey wen ɛ ten mok-a, è me gese ndu eyyio ɛ ketuu ndaa, è ghene yayte tetaŋ, è ghene yayte wel vi nè kenak ə wen ɛ kebaaten ə ghele eyshie na Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they could not find a way to do this because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on his mat through the tiles into the middle of the crowd, right in front of Jesus.

Oku_Text: Jesus yenen ge ghene beene wen di, è me suuy se wel vi ge: Babey, mɛ lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, "Friend, your sins are forgiven."

Oku_Text: Tefise te nchil ghene Balisi me keete è se kwaate ge: Wel vin lu ndɛ-a? əb nè fise Feyin. Wel əmoke fese lòò əbnəə abbaanese, lòó ke lu Feyin wen-moke-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent:
The Pharisees and the teachers of the law began thinking to themselves, "Who is this fellow who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?""
1996,Jesus me keele əbnəə wɛ ghene nè kwaaten di

Oku_Text: Sê é ghaŋte: Ge "Mɛ lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghu" sê "Lii sasen è ndu"-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?

Oku_Text: Dî mɛ nǎǎ ye neeye ghen keele ge Wán-wel-eyfɛl lu nè abtaa ɛ ntie ge ab lòò əbnəə abbaanese. Jesus me suuy se wel eykuo-eykul vi ge: Mɛ suuy se wè ge lii sasen, è wè fii kenak ə vie, è ndu ɛ ndaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins." So he said to the paralyzed man, "I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home."

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe wel vi me lii sasen ɛ eyshie na ghene, è me fii kenak wɛ ab nè luun ɛ ten, è me kase fiile ɛ ndaa, kokse Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God.

Oku_Text: Kelame gwel ghel ejim, ghene me se kokse Feyin, è me fane naa se ŋaŋ, suuye ge: Ghes yenenmen əbnəə abghakene ɛ kechii kě lane.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone was amazed and gave praise to God; they were filled with awe and said, "We have seen remarkable things today."

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe Jesus me lòò, è me yen wel eyfii-shile nè é toŋte ge Levi, ab nè jame eyshee ɛ ndaa-eyfii-shile. Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Koŋ mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector by the name of Levi sitting at his tax booth. "Follow me," Jesus told him,

Oku_Text: Levi me lòò əbnəə abjim, è me lii sasen, è me koŋ Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and Levi got up, left everything and followed him.

Oku_Text: Levi me neey eyjun eychak-ghel na Jesus ɛ ndaa na wen. Ghel sê eydio ghé é nè fii-shile lu nè Jesus gese nè ghel əmoke, ghene jame eyshee ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Levi held a great banquet for Jesus at his house, and a large crowd of tax collectors and others were eating with them.

Oku_Text: Balisi ghene tefise te nchil me keete è se kante se ghel eyghoŋ na Jesus ge: Ghen chame eyshee, è se nǎǎ nè ghel eyfii-shile ghene ghel eybame se ghɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law who belonged to their sect complained to his disciples, "Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?""

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: Ghel ghé ncheken bǎǎ se bife se wele-nchak-boyse-wel, dí ghel ghé é koon eychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered them, "It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.

Oku_Text: Mɛ bɛey gwiy ge mɛ ye yio ghel ejunje, dí mɛ gwiy ge mɛ ye yio ghel eybame ge ghene kubsen tetem ə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance."

Oku_Text: Ghele me suuy se Jesus ge: Ghel eyghoŋ na Joon nè chame eyzəə, è ghene yeme naa se eydio, ka ghel eyghoŋ na Balisi nǎǎ neey sêy, dí ghel eyghoŋ na vie ghene ko chame eyshee è se nǎǎ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They said to him, "John’s disciples often fast and pray, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours go on eating and drinking."

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: Ghen fese suuy se ghel ghé é nè yion chii na kensiyse ge ghene chame eyzəə ka kensiyse nè chiin nè ghene-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, "Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while he is with them?

Oku_Text: əbliy nèy ye gwiy wɛ əb nèy ye neey kensiyse fo ɛ ghene kebaaten, ghene nèy ye chame eyzəə ɛ əbliy fo sêy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; in those days they will fast."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghene ge: Wel əmoke bǎǎ se kfiy nwaale kě meen kfaksen ɛ nwaale kě loote. Lòó ab neey sêy, ab me momse nwaale kě meen vi, nɛ nwaale kě meen vi bǎǎ se jame nè nwaale kě loote.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He told them this parable: "No one tears a piece out of a new garment to patch an old one. Otherwise, they will have torn the new garment, and the patch from the new will not match the old.

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke gese bǎǎ se nyok amnduk ammeen ɛ nwaale-nduk yê kě loote. Lòó ab neey sêy, amnduk ammeen vi nèy ye yayte nwaale-nduk yê kě loote di, amnduk di me gwel, nɛ nwaale-nduk di me gwel sêy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the new wine will burst the skins; the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined.

Oku_Text: Dî əb lu ke ge əb nyok amnduk ammeen ɛ nwaale-nduk yê kě meen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No, new wine must be poured into new wineskins.

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke wɛ ab nǎǎ amnduk amloote bǎǎ se koŋ amnduk ammeen mok-a, bek ka ab suuyen ge: Amnduk amloote di me bǎǎ.
English_NIV_Equivalent:
And no one after drinking old wine wants the new, for they say, 'The old is better.'""

English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to them, I ask you

English_NIV_Equivalent: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law were furious and began to discuss with one another what they might do to Jesus.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Jesus nè nduun eyyio ɛ kfən əmoke ge ab ye yeme. əb me neey nchuy vi abjim è yeme se Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One of those days Jesus went out to a mountainside to pray, and spent the night praying to God.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ntake nè ney, è me yio ghel eyghoŋ na wen, è kfiy ghel njon-sebaa ɛ kebaaten ə ghene, è toŋte ghene ge ghel ntum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When morning came, he called his disciples to him and chose twelve of them, whom he also designated apostles:
