Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghene băǎ se fiile amkaa fo di, wel-bòòse-amkaa vi me suuy se ghene ge ab lòòmen amkaa fo. Simon, mɛ bifte wè ge wel wɛ ab nǎǎ ye koŋen wel-bòòse-amkaa vi naa se ŋaŋ lu ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Neither of them had the money to pay him back, so he forgave the debts of both. Now which of them will love him more?"
2105,Simon fise ge: Mɛ ke ge wel wɛ ab lòòmen amkaa eyjun na wen. Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Wè fise kelaanen."

Oku_Text: Jesus me kase fiile se wel-əbvii vi, è me suuy se Simon ge: Wè yen wel-əbvii nè vin-a? Mɛ ndu ɛ ndaa na vie, wè jia fo moo se mɛ ge mɛ lɔ̀se eychie na yom-a. Dí wel-əbvii nè vin lɔ̀semen eychie na yom nè ammtə̀ ə wen, è fiise nè ammtse ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he turned toward the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? I came into your house. You did not give me any water for my feet

Oku_Text: Wè jia bose mɛ-a, dí wel-əbvii nè vin keete se bose eychie na yom nɛ mɛ me gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You did not give me a kiss, but this woman, from the time I entered, has not stopped kissing my feet.

Oku_Text: Wè jia lɔ̀se amnduk ɛ ketuu yom-a, dí wel-əbvii nè vin lɔ̀semen amnduk kəbe ɛ eychie na yom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You did not put oil on my head, but she has poured perfume on my feet.

Oku_Text: Bek kii nɛ mɛ suuy se wè ge: əbnəə abbaanese na wen abjun abjim əb lòòmen, bek ka ab koŋen mɛ naa se ŋaŋ. Dí wel wɛ Feyine lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na wen fə̀ fə̀, ab koŋen Feyine fə̀ fə̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Therefore, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven—as her great love has shown. But whoever has been forgiven little loves little."
2110,Jesus me suuy se wel-əbvii vi ge: Mɛ lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghu."

Oku_Text: Ghel ghé é nè chame eyshee nè wen di me keete è se kwaate ge: Wel vin lu ndɛ-a wɛ ab gese lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghel-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The other guests began to say among themselves, Who is this who even forgets sins?""

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wel-əbvii vi ge: Wè lu wel eybose bek ka wè beenemen mɛ. Ndu nè eyzəəte.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you; go in peace.""

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe Jesus me ndu ɛ teychie-te-nteke gese nè teychie-te-teyioy, è se fekten sakghɛ əbjuŋ kum tefon na Feyin. Ghel ntum na wen njon-sebaa nè luun nè wen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this, Jesus traveled about from one town and village to another, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with him,

Oku_Text: gese nè əbvii əblee ghé Jesus nè boysemen ghene ɛ teychii na ghene gese nè teyioy əbe. əbvii nè vin lu: Malia (wɛ ab nè toŋten ge Malia Magdalene), wɛ Jesus nè lúúsemen teyioy əbe njon-sebaa ɛ wen ə eytem,
English_NIV_Equivalent: and also some women who had been cured of evil spirits and diseases: Mary (called Magdalene) from whom seven demons had come out;

Oku_Text: Joana (we wiy ə Kuza wɛ ab nè luun wele-kfə̀-tefon we Helod), Suzana, gese nè əbvii əmoke sê eydio. əbvii nè vin me se fɛlen Jesus ghene ghel ntum na wen nè əbkaa na ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Joanna the wife of Chuza, the manager of Herod’s household; Susanna; and many others. These women were helping to support them out of their own means.

Oku_Text: Ghel sê eydio me lòò ɛ teychie-te-nteke è se koo se Jesus. Se lu ka ghene nè nduu ɛ eyshie na wen di, è me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghene ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: While a large crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from town after town, he told this parable:

English_NIV_Equivalent: Some fell on rocky ground, and when it came up, the plants withered because they had no moisture.

English_NIV_Equivalent: bek ka: 'Ghene yen jii

Oku_Text: Eychak-ghene kum njǎŋ nè vin lu nè vin: Amka lu kenəə na Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God."

Oku_Text: Amka ghé é nè gwel ɛ kebaaten eynjon lu ka ghel ghé é nè yion kenəə na Feyin, nɛ Satan nè gwiy è fii kenəə di ɛ tetem ə ghene, ge ghene băǎ se beene è ghene boy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those along the path are the ones who hear, and then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.

Oku_Text: Amka ghé é nè gwel ɛ eyshaa etiy lu ka ghel ghé é nè yion kenəə na Feyin, è ghene lake è beene. Dí bek ka ghene băǎ lu nè ammtə̀, ghene beene se əbliy fə̀ fə̀. Se lu ka kensiyse me gwiy se ghene di, ghene me lòò eysene se Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those on the rocky ground are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away.

Oku_Text: Amka ghé é nè gwel ɛ kebaaten amchii lu ka ghel ghé é nè yion kenəə na Feyin, dí ghene se kwaate kum eylake ə ntie vin gese nè kum amkaa gese nè kum amkaa-amjun na wen, è nɔ̀se kenəə na Feyine di, è ghene băǎ se bii amka mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by life’s worries, riches and pleasures, and they do not mature.

Oku_Text: Dí amka ghé é nè gwel ɛ ntie kəjuŋ lu ka ghel ghé é nè lu nè tetem əbjuŋ è ghene yio kenəə na Feyin è ghene bose wen, è ghene bose sêy ɛ nchuy fɛn abjim è ghene bii amka.

English_NIV_Equivalent: ge ghel ghé é nè nduun ɛ ndaa di me yen eyseen."

English_NIV_Equivalent: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be known or brought out into the open.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ ghen yio jii se kenəə kɛ ghen nè yionen. Bek ka wel ejim wɛ ab lu nè jii, Feyine nǎǎ ye fo se wen. Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel yionen di, è ghene me koŋ wen. Jesus me lake ghene, è me fekte se ghene kum tefon na Feyin, è me boyse ghel ghé é nè koon teychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but the crowds learned about it and followed him. He welcomed them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and healed those who needed healing.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka chuy nè bǎǎ se ndu è din di, ghel ntum njon-sebaa me gwiy se Jesus, è ghene suuy se wen ge: "Tum ghel nè vin ge ghene ndu ɛ teychie-te-nteke gese nè teychie-te-teyioy ghé é luun ɛ ntie ghes nè vin, ge ghene yen eyshee è ghene yen ntie-eyjame, bek ka ghes lu ɛ əbkwak nè vin."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Late in the afternoon the Twelve came to him and said, "Send the crowd away so they can go to the surrounding villages and countryside and find food and lodging, because we are in a lonely place here."

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: "Fòen ghen eyshee se ghene ghene chame." Ghene me fise ge: "Ghes lu nè amchio əbtaa wen-moke gese nè shile baane, lòó ke lu ka wè koŋen ge ghes ndu è chə̀nse eyshee se ghel sê eydio nè vin-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, "You give them something to eat." They answered, "We have only five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all this crowd."

Oku_Text: (Ghel ejim me lu ghel 5,000.) Jesus me suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge: "Jameen ghel nè vin ɛ eyshaa-eyshaa, eyshaa əmoke ghel keful eyfaame."
English_NIV_Equivalent: (About five thousand men were there.) But he said to his disciples, "Have them sit down in groups of about fifty each."

Oku_Text: Ghene me neey sêy è ghel ejim me jame eyshee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The disciples did so, and everyone sat down.

Oku_Text: Jesus me fii amchio əbtaa di ghene shile baane di, è me keele eyyio ɛ eyshaa, è me kokse Feyin kum wen. Jesus me kfaksen è me fo se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge ghene fò se ghel ejim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, he gave thanks and broke them. Then he gave them to the disciples to distribute to the people.

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim me chame è ghene bii. Ghel eyghoŋ na Jesus me ghoŋte eyshaa-eyshee yê kě meen di è me jeme teyghoŋ njon-sebaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Jesus nè yemen nè wen-moke, nɛ ghel eyghoŋ na wen me gwiy se wen. Jesus me bife ghene ge: "Ghel suuy ge mɛ lu ndɛ-a?"
English_NIV_Equivalent:
Once when Jesus was praying in private and his disciples were with him, he asked them, "Who do the crowds say I am?""
2187,Ghene me fise ge: ""Ghel əmoke suuy ge wè lu Joon Nfoo Moo

Oku_Text: Jesus me bife ghene ge: "Dí ghen suuy ge mɛ lu ndɛ-a?" Bita me fise ge: "Wè lu Vi we Feyin nè Yiosen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "But what about you?" he asked. "Who do you say I am?" Peter answered, "God’s Messiah."

Oku_Text: Jesus me zaam ghene naa se ŋaŋ ge ghene ghǎà ye suuy se wel əmoke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus strictly warned them not to tell this to anyone.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: "Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye yen kensiyse naa se eydio, è ghel Isiael ghene ntanle gese nè tefise te nchil me bǎǎ se lake wen, è ghene fə̀lte ketuu na wen, dí nè lu ɛ kechii ketal ab nǎǎ ye tin ɛ eykuo."
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he said, "The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, and he must be killed and on the third day be raised to life."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel ejim ge: "Lòó wel əmoke me koŋen ge ab koŋ mɛ, ab lòò ɛ wen ə eytem è ab fii eynchak na wen ɛ kechii ejim è ab koŋ mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to them all: "Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.

Oku_Text: Bek ka wel ejim wɛ ab nè koŋen ge ab boyse kfəl na wen nǎǎ ye gwel wen, dí wel ejim wɛ ab nè fole kfəl na wen bek ka yom nǎǎ ye boyse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For whoever wants to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for me will save it.

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke nǎǎ ye yen jii ɛ ntie vin ge ndɛ-a, lòó ab me yen ntie vin abjim dí è me gwel-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: What good is it for someone to gain the whole world, and yet lose or forfeit their very self?

Oku_Text: Wel ejim wɛ ab nè kooken eysen se mɛ gese nè kum kenəə na yom, Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye kook sêy eysen se wen ɛ leghe wɛ ab nè kase fiilen nè abtaa na wen gese nè na Báǎba Feyin gese nè na nchiyse-ndaa ghé é nè luun kelaanen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of them when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge ghel əmoke lu nè vin ghé eb băǎ se koo eykuo, nɛ ghene nǎǎ ye yen tefon na Feyin."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè suuyen əbnəə nè vin di, è me lii è ndu ɛ əbliy nè luun keful sebaa, è me fii Bita, Joon ghene Jems, è ghene ndu ɛ kfən əmoke ge ghene ye yeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: About eight days after Jesus said this, he took Peter, John and James with him and went up onto a mountain to pray.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè yemen di, eymok na wen me bense, è eychak na wen me bense è me fua kəbe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed, and his clothes became as bright as a flash of lightning.

Oku_Text: Wel əblee me keete se ye suŋnen nè Jesus, ghene lu Moses ghene Elija,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Two men, Moses and Elijah, appeared in glorious splendor, talking with Jesus.

Oku_Text: ghene nè bense naa se ŋaŋ. Ghene nè suuyen kum eykuo na Jesus wɛ ab nè luun se neey ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They spoke about his departure, which he was about to bring to fulfillment at Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Bita ghene babey na wen nè koo eychii naa se ŋaŋ, dí ghene me lii è yen abtaa na Jesus gese nè wel əblee ghé é nè siyse nè wen di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Peter and his companions were very sleepy, but when they became fully awake, they saw his glory and the two men standing with him.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka wel əblee di nè bǎǎ se lòò ɛ eyshie na Jesus, Bita me suuy se Jesus ge: "Ndinte, ab me bǎǎ bek ka ghes nè luun nè vin. Ghes chiì teyn-ndaa-amchii tetal: əmoke lu we vie, əmoke we Moses, nɛ əmoke we Elija." (Bita nè suuyen sêy ka ab nè keele kenəə kɛ ab nè suuyen mok-a.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: As the men were leaving Jesus, Peter said to him, "Master, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah." (He did not know what he was saying.)

Oku_Text: Bita nè suuyen sêy di, è nchak me gwiy è kfəl ghene. Se lu ka nchak di nè kfəl ghene di, ghene me fanen naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While he was speaking, a cloud appeared and covered them, and they were afraid as they entered the cloud.

Oku_Text: əbnəə me lòò ɛ nchak di ə eytem suuye ge: "Wel vin lu Wán ə yom wɛ mɛ me yiosen. Ghen yioen kenəə na wen!"
English_NIV_Equivalent: A voice came from the cloud, saying, "This is my Son, whom I have chosen; listen to him."

Oku_Text: əbnəə di nè meyse di, Jesus me siyse nè wen-moke. Ghel eyghoŋ na Jesus me bose əbnəə nè vin ɛ tetem ə ghene, è ghene bɛey suuy se wel əmoke əbnəə wɛ ghene nè yenen di ɛ əbliy fo mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the voice had spoken, they found that Jesus was alone. The disciples kept this to themselves and did not tell anyone at that time what they had seen.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka chuy nè nduu è din di, Jesus ghene ghel eyghoŋ na wen me kase fiile ɛ kfən di, nɛ ghel sê eydio me gwiy se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day, when they came down from the mountain, a large crowd met him.

Oku_Text: Wel əblee ɛ kebaaten ə ghele di me ghan ge: "Ndinte, mɛ chakte wè ge wè yen wán ə yom, bek ka ab lu wán ə yom wen-moke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A man in the crowd called out, "Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.

Oku_Text: Keyioy əbe me se kookse wen, è ab me se ghanen ken e leghe. Keyioy vi me se kfə̀lte wen è ab me se lòò eynsə̀ ɛ eynnyii, è ab me se fɛlen wen naa se eydio è jia lòò ɛ wen ə eytem mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A spirit seizes him and he suddenly screams; it throws him into convulsions so that he foams at the mouth. It scarcely ever leaves him and is destroying him.

Oku_Text: Mɛ me chakte ghel eyghoŋ na vie ge ghene lúúse keyioy vi, dí ghene bɛey neey mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: I begged your disciples to drive it out, but they could not."

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise ge: "Ghen ghel ghé eb băǎ se beene, è ghen me se neey jii kəbe, mɛ nǎǎ ye lu nè ghen, è mɛ yio-lake nè ghen naa se ndɛ-a? Gwiy nè wán ə vie nè vin."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "You unbelieving and perverse generation," Jesus replied, "how long shall I stay with you and put up with you? Bring your son here."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka wán vi nè gwiyimen di, keyioy əbe vi me kookse wen ɛ ntie è ab me se kfə̀lte wen naa se eydio. Dí Jesus me zaam keyioy əbe vi, è me boyse wán vi, è me fo wen se babey na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Even while the boy was coming, the demon slammed him to the ground and threw him into a convulsion. But Jesus rebuked the impure spirit, healed the boy and gave him back to his father.

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim me chiyte naa se ŋaŋ kum abtaa na Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they were all amazed at the greatness of God.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel ejim nè chiyten kum əbnəə abjim wɛ Jesus nè neeyennen di, Jesus me suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: While everyone was marveling at all that Jesus did, he said to his disciples,

Oku_Text: "Ghen boseen əbnəə nè vin ɛ teyioy ə ghen: Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye koo ɛ eghoo na ghel."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Listen carefully to what I am about to tell you: The Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men."

Oku_Text: Dí ghel eyghoŋ na wen me băǎ se keele kenəə di mok-a, bek ka Feyine nè bosen wen ɛ eghoo na ghene ge ghene băǎ se keele. Ghene me gese fanen ge ghene bife Jesus kum kenəə di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they did not understand what this meant. It was hidden from them, so that they did not grasp it, and they were afraid to ask him about it.

Oku_Text: Ghel eyghoŋ na Jesus me keete è se kwaate ge wel wɛ ab nganten ɛ kebaaten ə ghene lu ndɛ-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: An argument started among the disciples as to which of them would be the greatest.

Oku_Text: Jesus me keele əbnəə wɛ ghene nè kwaaten di, è me fii wán nchek əmoke è siyse wen ɛ eyshie na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus, knowing their thoughts, took a little child and had him stand beside him.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: "Wel ejim wɛ ab nè lake wán nchek nè vin ɛ eylake ə yom, ab nè lake mɛ. Nɛ wel ejim wɛ ab nè lake mɛ, ab nè lake Wel wɛ ab me tumen mɛ. Bek ka wel wɛ ab nganten ɛ kebaaten ə ghen abjim lu wel wɛ ab nganten naa se eydio."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to them, "Whoever welcomes this little child in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me. For it is the one who is least among you all who is the greatest."

Oku_Text: Joon me suuy ge: "Ndinte, ghes yenenmen wel əmoke nè lúúsen teyioy əbe ɛ eylake ə vie, nɛ ghes me zaam wen bek ka ab băǎ lu wel ə ghes mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Master," said John, "we saw someone driving out demons in your name and we tried to stop him, because he is not one of us."

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Ghǎà ghen ye zaam wen, bek ka wel wɛ ab băǎ se baanese nè ghen lu wel ə ghen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Do not stop him," Jesus said, "for whoever is not against you is for you."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka chuy nè bǎǎ se koo ge Feyine nǎǎ ye fii Jesus eyyio ɛ eyshaa di, Jesus me kfiy ketuu na wen ge ab ndu ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As the time approached for him to be taken up to heaven, Jesus resolutely set out for Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: è me tum ghel ɛ mbiy na wen. Ghene me ndu ɛ ntek əmoke we ghel ə Samalia ge ghene ye siyse jii se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he sent messengers on ahead, who went into a Samaritan village to get things ready for him;

Oku_Text: Dí ghel ə ntek fo me bǎǎ se lake Jesus bek ka ab nè nduun ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but the people there did not welcome him, because he was heading for Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel eyghoŋ na wen Jems ghene Joon yenen di, è ghene me suuy ge: "Bay, wè koŋen ge ghes yio ge eynnyii nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ eyshaa è me fole ghene-a?"",When the disciples James and John saw this
English_NIV_Equivalent: they asked

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me kase fiile è me zaam ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Jesus turned and rebuked them.

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe ghene me ndu ɛ ntek əmoke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he and his disciples went to another village.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghene nè nduun ɛ eynchii di, wel əblee me suuy se Jesus ge: "Mɛ nǎǎ ye koŋ wè ɛ eysak ejim wɛ wè nè nduun ɛ ten."
English_NIV_Equivalent: As they were walking along the road, a man said to him, "I will follow you wherever you go."

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se wen ge: "Te-eysak-mey lu nè teyghu, nɛ eynyen-eytaa lu nè teynyuu, dí Wán-wel-eyfɛl băǎ lu nè eysak wɛ ab nè yemen ɛ ten mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wel əmoke ge: "Koŋ mɛ." Dí wel vi me fise ge: "Bay, fò eysak se mɛ ge mɛ ndu geese è me lúúse báǎba Feyine."
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to another man, "Follow me." But he replied, "Lord, first let me go and bury my father."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Lòòen ghel eykuo ge ghene lúúse ghel eykuo na ghene, dí wè ndu è fekte kum tefon na Feyin."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to him, "Let the dead bury their own dead, but you go and proclaim the kingdom of God."

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke gese me suuy ge: "Bay, mɛ nǎǎ ye koŋ wè, dí fò geese eysak se mɛ ge mɛ ndu è me koo-ndaa se ghel ɛ ndaa na yom."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Still another said, "I will follow you, Lord; but first let me go back and say goodbye to my family."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Wel ejim wɛ ab nè fii eghoo se eyghɛ njon dí è me kase fiile è keele ɛ eynchie, ab băǎ kelaanen se tefon na Feyin mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, "No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God."

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe Bay me kfiy ghel əmoke njon-sebaa gese nè baane, è me tum ghene baane-baane ɛ mbiy na wen ɛ teychie-te-nteke gese nè teychie-te-teyioy ghé ab nè nduun ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of him to every town and place where he was about to go.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: "Eysak-eyyale me bii naa se eydio, dí ghel eyghoŋ băǎ se nduun mok-a. Lu bek kii nɛ ghen yeme se Bay-eyyale ge ab ye tum ghel eyghoŋ ɛ eysak-eyyale na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He told them, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.

Oku_Text: Nduen! Yen-a, mɛ nè tumen ghen ka te-wán-be-eysak ɛ kebaaten te-eysak-eybee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.

Oku_Text: Ghǎà ghen ye fii kfən-amkaa, ghǎà eynchii-eyjame, ghǎà teychak-te-eychie, è ghen ghǎà ye yion koo-ndaa se wel əmoke ɛ eynchii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not take a purse or bag or sandals; and do not greet anyone on the road.

Oku_Text: Lòó ghen me ndu ɛ ndaa əmoke, ghen geese suuy ge: 'Eyzəəte nǎǎ ye lu ɛ ndaa nè vin.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: When you enter a house, first say, ’Peace to this house.’

Oku_Text: Lòó wel-eyzəəte me lu ɛ ten, eyzəəte na ghen nǎǎ ye lu ɛ wen ə eytem. Dí lòó ab băǎ lu ɛ ten mok-a, eyzəəte na ghen nǎǎ ye kase fiile se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If someone who promotes peace is there, your peace will rest on them; if not, it will return to you.

Oku_Text: Ghen jameen ɛ ndaa fo wen-moke, è ghen chame eyshee è nyok amnduk wɛ ghene nè fo se ghen, bek ka wel-eyghoŋ kelaanen ge ab ye yen eysak-eyyio na wen. Ghǎà ghen ye chinsen ɛ ndaa əmoke ndu ɛ ndaa əmoke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Stay there, eating and drinking whatever they give you, for the worker deserves his wages. Do not move around from house to house.

Oku_Text: Lòó ghen me ndu ɛ ntek əmoke è ghene lake ghen, ghen chameen eyshee wɛ ghene nè fo se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When you enter a town and are welcomed, eat what is offered to you.

Oku_Text: Ghen boyseen ghel ghé é nè koon teychii ɛ ntek fo, è ghen suuy se ghene ge: 'Tefon na Feyine me koo ɛ eyshie na ghen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, ’The kingdom of God has come near to you.’

Oku_Text: Dí lòó ghen me ndu ɛ ntek əmoke è ghene băǎ se lake ghen mok-a, ghen lòòen ɛ teynchie-nte fo è ghen suuy ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when you enter a town and are not welcomed, go into its streets and say,

Oku_Text: 'Ghes kfə̀lte teychii-nte na ghen nè vin wɛ ab nè fii ghes ɛ eychie, ge ghene keele ge ghene nè neeyennen kəbe. Dí keeleen geese ge tefon na Feyine me koo ɛ eyshie na ghen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Even the dust of your town we wipe from our feet as a warning to you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God has come near.’

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge nè lu ɛ əbliy-eynyak-əbnəə Feyine nǎǎ ye yen eysen se ghel ə Sodom geese nɛ ghel ə ntek fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey wè ntek ə Kolazin! Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey wè ntek ə Betsayda! Bek ka lòó əbnəə abghakene wɛ mɛ nè neeyennen ɛ kebaaten ə ghen nè neeyennen sêy ɛ ntek ə Taya ghene Sidon, ghel fo nǎǎ ye kubsen tetem ə ghene geese, è ghene chə̀nse teychak-te-əbnəə-əmbeey è ghene jame ɛ teymmtə̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

Oku_Text: Dí nè lu ɛ əbliy-eynyak-əbnəə Feyine nǎǎ ye yen eysen se ghel ə Taya ghene Sidon geese nɛ ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.

Oku_Text: Nɛ wè ntek ə Kafelnaum, wè ke ge Feyine nǎǎ ye fii wè eyyio ɛ eyshaa-a? Feyine nǎǎ ye fii wè naa se eydio ɛ eysak-eykuo!
English_NIV_Equivalent: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted to the heavens? No, you will go down to Hades.

Oku_Text: Wel ejim wɛ ab nè yion kenəə na ghen, ab nè yio kenəə na yom. Wel ejim wɛ ab nè lake ghen, ab nè lake mɛ. Nɛ wel ejim wɛ ab nè lake mɛ, ab nè lake Wel wɛ ab me tumen mɛ."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever listens to you listens to me; whoever rejects you rejects me; but whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me."

Oku_Text: Ghel njon-sebaa gese nè baane di me kase fiile nè eylake, è ghene suuy ge: "Bay, se lu ka ghes nè toŋten eylake ə vie di, teyioy əbe gese me neey kenəə na ghes."
English_NIV_Equivalent: The seventy-two returned with joy and said, "Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: "Mɛ yenen Satan wɛ ab nè gwelmen ɛ eyshaa ka eynnyii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, "I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.

Oku_Text: Yen-a, mɛ me fo abtaa se ghen ge ghen ndu ɛ ketuu teynnyii-ndaa gese nè tey-əbkuo-nchii, gese nè kenyinjene ɛ ketuu abtaa ejim we wel-eybee, nɛ əbnəə mok băǎ se neey jii kəbe se ghen mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have given you authority to trample on snakes and scorpions and to overcome all the power of the enemy; nothing will harm you.

Oku_Text: Dí ghǎà ghen ye lake bek ka teyioy əbe nè neeyen kenəə na ghen mok-a, dí lakeen bek ka eylake ə ghen nè saanken ɛ eyshaa."
English_NIV_Equivalent: However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven."

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Jesus nè laken naa se eydio ɛ Keyioy-əbjuŋ ə eytem, è me suuy ge: "Báǎba Bay we eyshaa gese nè ntie, mɛ kokse wè bek ka wè bosen əbnəə nè vin ɛ eghoo na ghel-eykeele-əbnəə gese nè ghel-eytof, è wè fo eysak se ghel-eytof-ka-wán-nchek. Eya Báǎba, bek ka ab me lu ken kɛ wè koŋen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that time Jesus, full of joy through the Holy Spirit, said, "I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do.

Oku_Text: Báǎba me fo əbnəə abjim se mɛ. Wel əmoke băǎ se keele Wán mok-a dí ka lu Báǎba. Nɛ wel əmoke băǎ se keele Báǎba mok-a dí ka lu Wán, gese nè wel wɛ Wán nè koŋen ge ab ye neey wen se wen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him."

Oku_Text: Jesus me kase fiile se ghel eyghoŋ na wen, è me suuy se ghene geese nè ghene wen-moke ge: "Ghen lu ghel eybose bek ka ghen yenen əbnəə nè vin!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he turned to his disciples and said privately, "Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge ghel-eytof sê eydio gese nè tefon sê eydio me koŋen ge ghene yen əbnəə wɛ ghen nè yenen nè vin, dí ghene jia yen-a. Ghene me koŋen ge ghene yio əbnəə wɛ ghen nè yionen nè vin, dí ghene jia yio-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: For I tell you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it."

Oku_Text: Ndinte-tefise-te-nchil əblee me lii è se bǎǎ se lake Jesus, è me bife wen ge: "Ndinte, mɛ nǎǎ ye neey jii ghɛ-a ge mɛ yen ntie-eyjame wɛ ab băǎ nè ammtə̀-a?"",On one occasion an expert in the law stood up to test Jesus. ""Teacher
English_NIV_Equivalent: he asked

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se wen ge: "əb nyakenmen ɛ tefise-te-nchil ge ndɛ-a? Wè nè lə̀ə̀sen ge ndɛ-a?"","What is written in the Law?"" he replied. ""How do you read it?""""
əb me fise ge: "'Koŋen Bay Feyin na vie nè tetem ə vie abjim, nè keyioy na vie abjim, nè abtaa na vie abjim, gese nè nè eylake na vie abjim,' nɛ, 'Koŋen wel-eyfɛl na vie ka kfəl na vie.'"
English_NIV_Equivalent: He answered, "’Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind’; and, ’Love your neighbor as yourself.’"

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Wè fise kelaanen. Neey sêy, nɛ wè nǎǎ ye boy."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "You have answered correctly," Jesus replied. "Do this and you will live."

Oku_Text: Dí ndinte-tefise-te-nchil vi me koŋen ge ab ye neey ge ab lu wel-ejunje, è me bife Jesus ge: "Wel-eyfɛl na yom lu ndɛ-a?"",But he wanted to justify himself
English_NIV_Equivalent: so he asked Jesus

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise ge: "Wel əblee nè lòò ɛ Jolesalem nè nduun ɛ Jeliko. əb me gwel ɛ eghoo na ghel-eychu, ghene me kfə̀lte teychak na wen, è ghene se kookse wen naa se eydio, è ghene lòò è se bose wen ɛ ten è ab bǎǎ se kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied: "A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, when he was attacked by robbers. They stripped him of his clothes, beat him and went away, leaving him half dead.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ kechii əmoke nɛ wele nchil əblee nè lòò ɛ eynchii fo, è yen wen, è ab me lake è kase fiile ɛ eynchie eymnduk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A priest happened to be going down the same road, and when he saw the man, he passed by on the other side.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ka wel ə Lefi əblee gese me gwiy è chin ɛ əbliy fo, è yen wen, è ab me gese lake wen è kase fiile ɛ eynchie eymnduk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So too, a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.

Oku_Text: Dí wel ə Samalia əblee wɛ ab nè nduun ɛ eynchii na wen me gwiy è chin ɛ əbliy wɛ wel vi nè bosen ɛ ten di. Se lu ka ab yenen wen di, è me koosen eysen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But a Samaritan, as he traveled, came where the man was; and when he saw him, he took pity on him.

Oku_Text: əb me ndu è nyak teynnyii na wen nè amnduk gese nè amchio, è me fii wen ɛ ketuu eynnyii-eytaa na wen, è me fii wen ɛ ndaa-eyjame-ghel-eyndu è boyse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put the man on his own donkey, brought him to an inn and took care of him.

Oku_Text: Chuy me nduu è din di, è me fii amkaa keful baane è fo se wele-kfə̀-ndaa-eyjame di, è me suuy ge: 'Boyse-a wel nè vin. Lòó wè me saanken amkaa əmoke nè boyse wen di, mɛ nǎǎ ye kase gwiy è me fo se wè.'"
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper. ’Look after him,’ he said, ’and when I return, I will reimburse you for any extra expense you may have.’

Oku_Text: Jesus me bife ge: "Wè ke ge ɛ kebaaten ghel tetal nè vin, wel wɛ ab lu wel-eyfɛl we wel wɛ ghel-eychu nè kooksemen di lu ndɛ-a?"","Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?""""
Ndinte-tefise-te-nchil vi me fise ge: "Lu wel wɛ ab nè koosen eysen se wen di." Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Ndu è wè ye neey sêy geese."
English_NIV_Equivalent: The expert in the law replied, "The one who had mercy on him." Jesus told him, "Go and do likewise."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus ghene ghel eyghoŋ na wen nè nduun ɛ eynchii di, Jesus me koo ɛ ntek əmoke. Wel-əbvii əblee nè é toŋte ge Mata me lake Jesus ɛ ndaa na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, he came to a village where a woman named Martha opened her home to him.

Oku_Text: Mata me lu nè nchek-əbvii ə wen nè é toŋte ge Malia. Malia me jame ɛ eychie na Jesus, è ab nè yion kenəə na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: She had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet listening to what he said.

Oku_Text: Dí Mata me se kwaate naa se eydio kum sêy sê eydio wɛ ab nè neeyennen di. əb me gwiy è suuy se Jesus ge: "Bay, wè yen ge nchek na yome me lòòmen sêy abjim se yom-moke-a? Suuy se wen ge ab ye baanese nè mɛ."
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Martha was distracted by all the preparations that had to be made. She came to him and asked, "Lord, don’t you care that my sister has left me to do the work by myself? Tell her to help me!"

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me fise se wen ge: "Mata! Mata! Wè kwaate è se fele eytem naa se eydio kum əbnəə sê eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Martha, Martha," the Lord answered, "you are worried and upset about many things,

Oku_Text: Dí sêy wen-moke di me lu kelaanen. Malia me yiosen sêy-əbjuŋ wɛ wel əmoke băǎ se fii ɛ eghoo na wen mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: but few things are needed—or indeed only one. Mary has chosen what is better, and it will not be taken away from her."

Oku_Text: Oku_Text
English_NIV_Equivalent: English_NIV_Equivalent

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Jesus nè yemen ɛ əbliy əmoke. Se lu ka ab me fə̀lten di, wel əblee ɛ kebaaten ghel eyghoŋ na wen me suuy se wen ge: "Bay, fekte ghes eyyeme geese nɛ Joon nè fektemen ghel eyghoŋ na wen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: One day Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said to him, "Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: "Lòó ghen nè yemen di, ghen suuyeen ge: 'Báǎba, neey ge eylake ə vie nǎǎ ye lu kelaanen. Neey ge tefon na vie nǎǎ ye gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, "When you pray, say: ’Father, hallowed be your name, your kingdom come.

Oku_Text: Fò eyshee se ghes ɛ kechii ejim wɛ ghes nè koŋen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Give us each day our daily bread.

Oku_Text: Lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghes, bek ka ghes gese nè lòòmen əbnəə abbaanese na ghel ejim ghé é nè neey jii ɛ eghoo na ghes. Ghǎà wè ye fii ghes ɛ eytem-eylake.'"
English_NIV_Equivalent: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who sins against us. And lead us not into temptation.’"

Oku_Text: Jesus me gese suuy se ghene ge: "Lòó wel əblee ɛ kebaaten ə ghen me ndu ɛ ndaa na babey na wen ɛ tetem-əbchuu, è ab ye suuy se wen ge: 'Babey, fò amchio tetal se mɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to them, "Suppose you have a friend, and you go to him at midnight and say, ’Friend, lend me three loaves of bread;

Oku_Text: Bek ka babey na yome me gwiy ɛ eynchii na wen è ab nè siyse ɛ ndaa na yom, nɛ mɛ băǎ lu nè eyshee mok-a ge mɛ fò se wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: a friend of mine on a journey has come to me, and I have no food to offer him.’

Oku_Text: Nɛ wel vi me fise ɛ ndaa ə eytem ge: 'Ghǎà wè ye fii mɛ eytem. Mɛ me nɔ̀semen eychie-ndaa, nɛ mɛ ghene babey na yome ghes nè yemen. Mɛ băǎ bɛey lii ge mɛ fo əbnəə mok se wè mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: And suppose the one inside answers, ’Don’t bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I can’t get up and give you anything.’

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge lòó ab băǎ lii ge ab ye fo amchio di se wen bek ka ab lu babey na wen mok-a, ab nǎǎ ye lii è fo se wen əbnəə abjim wɛ ab nè koŋen di bek ka ab nè chakte wen naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, even though he will not get up and give you the bread because of friendship, yet because of your shameless audacity he will surely get up and give you as much as you need.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Chakteen, nǎǎ ye fo se ghen. Keleeen, nǎǎ ye yen. Kookseen eychie-ndaa, nǎǎ ye fole se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "So I say to you: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.

Oku_Text: Bek ka wel ejim wɛ ab nè chakten nǎǎ ye yen, nɛ wel wɛ ab nè keleen nǎǎ ye yen, nɛ wel wɛ ab nè kooksen eychie-ndaa nǎǎ ye fole se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.

Oku_Text: Ndɛ-a ɛ kebaaten ə ghen wɛ ab lu báǎba, nɛ wán na wen me chakte shile, è ab me fii nnyii è fo se wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Which of you fathers, if your son asks for a fish, will give him a snake instead?

Oku_Text: Lòó ab me chakte mntə̀-eynyen, è ab me fii nchii è fo se wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Or if he asks for an egg, will give him a scorpion?

Oku_Text: Ghen ghel ghé eb nè neey jii nè vin keele ge ghen fò əbnəə əbjuŋ se babey na ghen. Lu geese nɛ Báǎba na ghen wɛ ab lu ɛ eyshaa nǎǎ ye fo Keyioy-əbjuŋ se ghel ghé é nè chakten wen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: If you then, though you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him!"

Oku_Text: Jesus nè lúúsen keyioy əbe əblee wɛ ab nè neey ge wel băǎ se suŋnen mok-a. Se lu ka keyioy əbe di nè lòòmen di, wel wɛ ab nè luun wel-əbkùk di me keete se ye suŋnen. Ghel ejim me chiyte naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus was driving out a demon that was mute. When the demon left, the man who had been mute spoke, and the crowd was amazed.

Oku_Text: Dí ghel əmoke me suuy ge: "Ab nè lúúsen teyioy əbe nè abtaa na Beelzebul wɛ ab lu wele-kfə̀ na teyioy əbe."
English_NIV_Equivalent: But some of them said, "By Beelzebub, the prince of demons, he is driving out demons."

Oku_Text: Ghel əmoke me koŋen ge ghene lake Jesus, è ghene chakte wen ge ab neey əmbeey əmoke ɛ eyshaa ge ghene yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Others tested him by asking for a sign from heaven.

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus nè keelen əbnəə wɛ ghene nè kwaaten di, è me suuy se ghene ge: "Lòó tefon əmoke me baanese ɛ tetem ə wen, tefon fo nǎǎ ye gwel. Nɛ lòó ghel ɛ ndaa əmoke me baanese ɛ tetem ə ghene, ndaa fo nǎǎ ye gwel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus knew their thoughts and said to them: "Any kingdom divided against itself will be ruined, and a house divided against itself will fall.

Oku_Text: Lòó Satan gese me baanese ɛ tetem ə wen, tefon na wen nǎǎ ye siyse ghɛ-a? Mɛ suuy sêy bek ka ghen suuy ge mɛ nè lúúsen teyioy əbe nè abtaa na Beelzebul.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom stand? I say this because you claim that I drive out demons by Beelzebub.

Oku_Text: Lòó mɛ nè lúúsen teyioy əbe nè abtaa na Beelzebul di, ghel eyghoŋ na ghen nè lúúsen teyioy əbe nè abtaa na ndɛ-a? Lu bek kii nɛ ghene nǎǎ ye suuy ge ghen lu ghel-əbame.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now if I drive out demons by Beelzebub, by whom do your followers drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.

Oku_Text: Dí lòó mɛ nè lúúsen teyioy əbe nè abtaa na Feyin di, ghen keeleen ge tefon na Feyine me gwiy se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But if I drive out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

Oku_Text: Lòó wel-eybee wɛ ab lu nè abtaa naa se ŋaŋ me siyse jii se ye chə̀nsen ndaa na wen nè kenyinjene, ntie na wen nǎǎ ye lu kelaanen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own house, his possessions are safe.

Oku_Text: Dí lòó wel əmoke wɛ ab nganten wen me gwiy è kookse wen di, ab nǎǎ ye fii kenyinjene na wen abjim wɛ ab nè beenen ge ab nǎǎ ye boyse wen di, è ab me kfaksen ntie na wen di se ghel əmoke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when someone stronger attacks and overpowers him, he takes away the armor in which the man trusted and divides up his plunder.

Oku_Text: Wel ejim wɛ ab băǎ se baanese nè mɛ lu wel-eybee na yom. Nɛ wel ejim wɛ ab băǎ se baanese nè mɛ ge ghes ghoŋte ghel mok-a, ab nè saankennen ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.

Oku_Text: Keyioy əbe kase fiile
English_NIV_Equivalent: "When an impure spirit comes out of a person, it goes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it. Then it says, ’I will return to the house I left.’

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ab nè kase fiilen di, è ab yen ge ghel me saankennen ɛ ndaa fo è ghene siyse jii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When it arrives, it finds the house swept clean and put in order.

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe ab me ndu è fii teyioy əmoke njon-sebaa ghé é nè be geese nɛ wen, è ghene ndu è ghene jame ɛ ten. əbliy wɛ wel vi nè bǎǎ se lu ɛ ten di me bǎǎ se be geese nɛ eynjon."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then it goes and takes seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and live there. And the final condition of that person is worse than the first."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè suuyen əbnəə nè vin di, wel-əbvii əblee ɛ kebaaten ghel sê eydio di me ghan ge: "Wel-əbvii wɛ ab nè fii wè, è wè nyok amchio na wen lu wel eybose!"
English_NIV_Equivalent: As Jesus was saying these things, a woman in the crowd called out, "Blessed is the mother who gave you birth and nursed you."

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me fise ge: "Eya, dí ghel ghé é nè yion kenəə na Feyin è ghene neey sêy lu ghel eybose naa se eydio."
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, "Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel nè saankennen di, Jesus me keete se ye suuy se ghene ge: "Ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin lu ghel-əbe. Ghene koŋen ge ghene yen əmbeey, dí Feyine băǎ se neey əmbeey mok se ghene mok-a dí ka əmbeey na Jona.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As the crowds increased, Jesus said, "This is a wicked generation. It asks for a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.

Oku_Text: Ka nɛ Jona me luun əmbeey se ghel ə Nineve di, lu geese nɛ Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye lu əmbeey se ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so also will the Son of Man be to this generation.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy-eynyak-əbnəə, wele-kfə̀-əbvii wɛ ab nè luun ɛ eysak eybvase na eytii nǎǎ ye lii è kùùne ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin, bek ka ab me lòò ɛ eysak eybvase ge ab ye yio kenəə-eykeele-əbnəə na Solomon. Dí yen-a, wel wɛ ab nganten Solomon lu nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, for she came from the ends of the earth to listen to Solomon’s wisdom; and now something greater than Solomon is here.

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy-eynyak-əbnəə, ghel ə Nineve nǎǎ ye lii è kùùne ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin, bek ka ghene me kubsen tetem ə ghene se lu ka ghene nè yionen kenəə kɛ Jona nè fektemen di. Dí yen-a, wel wɛ ab nganten Jona lu nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The men of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and now something greater than Jonah is here.

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke băǎ se fole nduk è bose nè wele-nduk sê ab me neey ɛ eyshaa kenak-a. Dí ab nè siyse nduk di eyyio ɛ ketuu, ge ghel ghé é nè nduun ɛ ndaa di me yen eyseen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "No one lights a lamp and puts it in a place where it will be hidden, or under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, so that those who come in may see the light.

Oku_Text: Eysene lu ka nduk na kfəl na vie. Lòó eysene na vie me lu kelaanen, kfəl na vie abjim nǎǎ ye lu ɛ eyseen ə eytem. Dí lòó eysene na vie băǎ lu kelaanen mok-a, kfəl na vie abjim nǎǎ ye lu ɛ eysə̀ ə eytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eyes are healthy, your whole body also is full of light. But when they are unhealthy, your body also is full of darkness.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ wè nǎǎ ye bose kfəl na vie ge eyseen wɛ ab lu ɛ eghoo na vie băǎ ye bense è bǎǎ lu eysə̀ mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: See to it then, that the light within you is not darkness.

Oku_Text: Lòó kfəl na vie abjim me lu ɛ eyseen ə eytem, è eysak mok băǎ lu ɛ eysə̀ mok-a, kfəl na vie abjim nǎǎ ye seen kelaanen geese nɛ nduk nè seenen ɛ eghoo na vie di."
English_NIV_Equivalent: If then your whole body is full of light, and no part of it dark, it will be just as full of light as when a lamp shines its light on you."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè fə̀lten suuy di, Balisi əblee me yio wen ge ab ye chame eyshee nè wen. Jesus me ndu è jame eyshee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus had finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him to eat with him; so he went in and reclined at the table.

Oku_Text: Balisi vi me chiyte naa se ŋaŋ se lu ka ab yenen ge Jesus jia suu eghoo geese nɛ ab nè chame eyshee mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the Pharisee was surprised when he noticed that Jesus did not first wash before the meal.

Oku_Text: Dí Bay me suuy se wen ge: "Ghen Balisi nè suun eynchie na kfən gese nè na eychak-eyyio, dí ɛ tetem ə ghen me jeme nè əbchu gese nè nè əbe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the Lord said to him, "Now then, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.

Oku_Text: Ghen ghel-eykfùk! Feyin wɛ ab me neeyennen eynchie na əbnəə di, ka ab me neey gese tetem ə wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: You foolish people! Did not the one who made the outside make the inside also?

Oku_Text: Fòen əbnəə wɛ ghen nè lu nè wen se ghel-eykfəə, è əbnəə abjim nǎǎ ye lu ɛ suun se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But now as for what is inside you—be generous to the poor, and everything will be clean for you.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey wè Balisi! Ghen nè foen ɛ kebaaten nchie ejim eymoke na teychie-eyyale na ghen geese nɛ miche ghene əbnəə əmoke abjim se Feyin, dí ghen lakeen ge ghen neey jii wɛ ab lu kelaanen, è ghen băǎ koŋen Feyin mok-a. Ghen nè fòen eyshee nè vin se Feyin, è ghen ghǎà ye lakeen əbnəə əmoke di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Woe to you Pharisees, because you give God a tenth of your mint, rue and all other kinds of garden herbs, but you neglect justice and the love of God. You should have practiced the latter without leaving the former undone.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey wè Balisi! Ghen koŋen ge ghen jame ɛ teychie-eyyio ɛ te-ndaa-eyjame, è ghen koŋen ge ghel yion koo-ndaa se ghen ɛ eysak eybvase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Woe to you Pharisees, because you love the most important seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey! Ghen lu ka teyn-ndaa-eykuo ghé eb băǎ se yen mok-a, è ghel me se ndu ɛ ketuu è ghene jia keele mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Woe to you, because you are like unmarked graves, which people walk over without knowing it."

Oku_Text: Ndinte-tefise-te-nchil əblee me fise se wen ge: "Ndinte, se lu ka wè nè suuyen əbnəə nè vin di, wè nè suuyen gese nè kum ghes əbe."
English_NIV_Equivalent: One of the experts in the law answered him, "Teacher, when you say these things, you insult us also."

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise ge: "Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey gese wè ndinte-tefise-te-nchil! Ghen nè foen eytem-eynyak-əbnəə sê eydio ɛ ketuu na ghel ghé é băǎ kelaanen se fii-a, dí ghen wen-moke băǎ se fii eghoo mok ge ghen baanese nè ghene mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, "And you experts in the law, woe to you, because you load people down with burdens they can hardly carry, and you yourselves will not lift one finger to help them.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey! Ghen nè chiì teyn-ndaa-eykuo na tefon na Feyin, ghel ghé é babey na ghen me fə̀lten ketuu na ghene di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Woe to you, because you build tombs for the prophets, and it was your ancestors who killed them.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ ghen nè neeyennen ge ghen beene nè əbnəə wɛ babey na ghen nè neeyennen di. Ghene me fə̀lte ketuu na tefon na Feyin, dí ghen nè chiì teyn-ndaa-eykuo na ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So you testify that you approve of what your ancestors did; they killed the prophets, and you build their tombs.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ Feyine me suuy ɛ tetem-eykeele-əbnəə na wen ge: 'Mɛ nǎǎ ye tum tefon na Feyin gese nè ghel ntum se ghene, ghene nǎǎ ye fə̀lte ketuu na ghel əmoke è ghene neey kensiyse se ghel əmoke.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Because of this, God in his wisdom said, ’I will send them prophets and apostles, some of whom they will kill and others they will persecute.’

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ Feyine nǎǎ ye nyak əbnəə abbaanese na ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin bek ka ammtə̀ na tefon na Feyin abjim wɛ ghel me se lǒ kfaksen nɛ mnduk nè keete di,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore this generation will be held responsible for the blood of all the prophets that has been shed since the beginning of the world,

Oku_Text: keete se ammtə̀ na Abel ndu è chin se ammtə̀ na Zakalia, wɛ ghene nè fə̀ltemen ketuu na wen ɛ kebaaten ntie-eykokse-Feyin gese nè eysak eyseen. Eya, mɛ suuy se ghen ge Feyine nǎǎ ye nyak əbnəə nè vin ɛ eghoo na ghel-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, this generation will be held responsible for it all.

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye yen əbnəə əmbeey wè ndinte-tefise-te-nchil! Ghen me bosen eychie-ndaa na eykeele-əbnəə. Ghen jia jame ɛ ten mok-a, è ghen zaamen ghel ghé é nè koŋen ge ghene jame ɛ ten di."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Woe to you experts in the law, because you have taken away the key to knowledge. You yourselves have not entered, and you have hindered those who were entering."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè lòòmen ɛ əbliy fo di, Balisi ghene tefise te nchil me keete è se kùùne wen è ghene se bife wen əbnəə sê eydio,
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus went outside, the Pharisees and the teachers of the law began to oppose him fiercely and to besiege him with questions,

Oku_Text: bek ka ghene nè kaken ge ghene yio kenəə kɛ Jesus nǎǎ ye suuy ge ghene bose wen ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: waiting to catch him in something he might say.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel sê eydio naa se eydio nè saankennen di, è ghene me se kfə̀lte se ghene-ghene, Jesus me keete se ye suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen geese ge: "Ghen boyseen ghen kum eychuu-eyshee na Balisi, wɛ ab lu əbe-əbchuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Meanwhile, when a crowd of many thousands had gathered, so that they were trampling on one another, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples, saying, "Be on your guard against the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

Oku_Text: əbnəə abbaanese wɛ ghene nè bosen di nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ eynjon, nɛ əbnəə abjim wɛ ghene nè neeyennen chichi di nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ eyseen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, or hidden that will not be made known.

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ əbnəə abjim wɛ ghen nè suuyen ɛ eysə̀ di nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ eyseen, nɛ əbnəə abbaanese wɛ ghen nè suuyen ɛ teyioy ɛ ndaa ə eytem di nǎǎ ye ghanen ɛ kfən-ndaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: What you have said in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the roofs.

Oku_Text: Babey na yom, mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Ghǎà ghen ye fan ghel ghé é nè fə̀lte kfəl, dí ghene băǎ bɛey neey jii ɛ eytem mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "I tell you, my friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.

Oku_Text: Dí mɛ nǎǎ ye neey ge ghen keele wel wɛ ab lu kəbe ge ghen fan wen: Faneen Feyin wɛ ab nè lu nè abtaa ge ab fə̀lte wel, è ab me fii wen ɛ eysak-eynyuu. Eya, mɛ suuy se ghen ge faneen wen!
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear him who, after your body has been killed, has authority to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear him.

Oku_Text: Ghel băǎ se chə̀nse te-wán-nyen-te-eytaa keful baane nè amkaa əbaane mok-a? Dí Feyine băǎ se lake wán-nyen-eytaa mok-moke ɛ kebaaten ghene mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.

Oku_Text: Eya, gese nè teynchie-te-eytuu na ghen abjim Feyine me saankenmen. Ghǎà ghen ye fan, bek ka ghen nganten te-wán-nyen-te-eytaa sê eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Indeed, the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Don’t be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Wel ejim wɛ ab nè beenen ɛ ketuu ghel ge ab lu wel ə yom, Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye beene gese ɛ ketuu nchiyse-ndaa na Feyin ge wel vi lu wel ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "I tell you, whoever publicly acknowledges me before others, the Son of Man will also acknowledge before the angels of God.

Oku_Text: Dí wel ejim wɛ ab nè ntanlen mɛ ɛ ketuu ghel, Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye ntanle wen gese ɛ ketuu nchiyse-ndaa na Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But whoever disowns me before others will be disowned before the angels of God.

Oku_Text: Wel ejim wɛ ab nè suuyen əbnəə əmbeey ɛ ketuu Wán-wel-eyfɛl, Feyine nǎǎ ye lòòmen əbnəə di se wen. Dí wel ejim wɛ ab nè suuyen əbnəə əmbeey ɛ ketuu Keyioy-əbjuŋ, Feyine băǎ se lòòmen əbnəə di se wen mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but anyone who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

Oku_Text: Lòó ghene me fii ghen ɛ te-ndaa-eyjame, ghene ndu nè ghen ɛ ketuu tefon gese nè ghel ghé é nè lu nè abtaa di, ghǎà ghen ye kwaate kum ntie wɛ ghen nǎǎ ye fise-a, ghene kum əbnəə wɛ ghen nǎǎ ye suuy-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "When you are brought before synagogues, rulers and authorities, do not worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say,

Oku_Text: Bek ka Keyioy-əbjuŋ nǎǎ ye fekte ghen ɛ əbliy fo əbnəə wɛ ghen nè koŋen ge ghen suuy."
English_NIV_Equivalent: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that time what you should say."

Oku_Text: Wel əblee ɛ kebaaten ghel sê eydio di me suuy se Jesus ge: "Ndinte, suuy se nchek na yom ge ab kfaksen ntie wɛ báǎba Feyine nè bosenmen di se ghes."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Someone in the crowd said to him, "Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me."

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me fise se wen ge: "Wel-eyfɛl, wel wɛ ab me kfiy mɛ ge mɛ lu wele-kfə̀-əbame ghene wele-kfak-ntie ɛ kebaaten ə ghen lu ndɛ-a?"",Jesus replied
English_NIV_Equivalent: Man

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel ejim ge: "Boyseen ghen, è ghen ghǎà ye koŋen amkaa naa se eydio, bek ka boy na wel băǎ lǒ ɛ eydio amkaa na wen mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to them, "Watch out! Be on your guard against all kinds of greed; life does not consist in an abundance of possessions."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghene ge: "Eysak-eyyale na wel-əbjuŋ əblee me bii naa se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he told them this parable: "The ground of a certain rich man yielded an abundant harvest.

Oku_Text: əb me keete se ye kwaate ɛ tetem ə wen ge: 'Mɛ nǎǎ ye neey jii ghɛ-a, bek ka mɛ băǎ lu nè eysak wɛ mɛ nǎǎ ye bose eyshee na yom ɛ ten mok-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: He thought to himself, ’What shall I do? I have no place to store my crops.’

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe ab me suuy ge: 'Ken kɛ mɛ nǎǎ ye neeyennen lu nè vin: Mɛ nǎǎ ye kfəl te-ndaa-eybose-eyshee na yom, è mɛ chiì teyn-ndaa ghé é nganten, mɛ bose amchio na yom gese nè ntie na yom abjim ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Then he said, ’This is what I’ll do. I will tear down my barns and build bigger ones, and there I will store my surplus grain and my goods.

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe mɛ nǎǎ ye suuy se kfəl na yom ge: Kfəl na yom, wè me bosen ntie-əbjuŋ sê eydio wɛ ab nǎǎ ye baanese nè wè naa se tefə̀ sê eydio. Zəəte-a, chame, nyok è wè lake.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: And I’ll say to myself, "You have plenty of grain laid up for many years. Take life easy; eat, drink and be merry."’

Oku_Text: Dí Feyine me suuy se wen ge: 'Wè wel-eykfùk! Nè lu ɛ əbchuu nè vin nɛ mɛ nǎǎ ye fii kfəl na vie. əbnəə abbaanese wɛ wè me bosenmen nè vin nǎǎ ye lu we ndɛ-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "But God said to him, ’You fool! This very night your life will be demanded from you. Then who will get what you have prepared for yourself?’

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: "Lu geese nɛ ab nè luun se wel wɛ ab nè saankennen amkaa bek ka kfəl na wen-moke, dí ab băǎ lu wel-əbjuŋ ɛ eyshaa na Feyin mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: "This is how it will be with whoever stores up things for themselves but is not rich toward God."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge: "Lu bek kii nɛ mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Ghǎà ghen ye kwaate kum kfəl na ghen ge ghen nǎǎ ye chame jii-a, ghene kum teychak na ghen ge ghen nǎǎ ye bɛey jii-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to his disciples: "Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat; or about your body, what you will wear.

Oku_Text: Bek ka kfəl na wel nganten eyshee, nɛ teychak na wel nganten teychak-te-amkaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.

Oku_Text: Yeneen te-nyen-te-eytaa: Ghene băǎ yale, è ghene băǎ ghoŋ, ghene băǎ lu nè ndaa-eybose-amchio mok-a, dí Feyine me se fo eyshee se ghene. Ghen nganten te-nyen-te-eytaa naa se eydio!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn; yet God feeds them. And how much more valuable you are than birds!

Oku_Text: Ndɛ-a ɛ kebaaten ə ghen wɛ ab nè kwaaten di, ab nǎǎ ye neey ge chuy na wen nǎǎ ye fɛl eyyio ɛ mbiy eynchie əmoke mok-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to your life?

Oku_Text: Lòó ghen băǎ neey jii wɛ ab lu nchichi nè vin mok-a, ghen kwaate kum əbnəə əmoke naa se ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Since you cannot do this very little thing, why do you worry about the rest?

Oku_Text: Yeneen te-mchii-te-eyyale nɛ ghene nè lə̀ə̀sen di: Ghene băǎ neey sêy, ghene băǎ lòte eynchii-eyjame mok-a. Dí mɛ suuy se ghen ge Solomon nè abtaa na wen abjim, ab jia bɛey jii geese nɛ mchii nè vin mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Consider how the wild flowers grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these.

Oku_Text: Lòó Feyine nè boyse mchii ɛ eysak, mchii wɛ ab lu nè vin ɛ kechii nè vin, chuy ab me gwel-a, ghene me fii è ghene fole ɛ eynnyii di, lu geese nɛ ab nǎǎ ye boyse ghen geese, wè ghel ghé eb băǎ se beene naa se eydio mok-a!
English_NIV_Equivalent: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today, and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, how much more will he cloth you—you of little faith!

Oku_Text: Ghǎà ghen ye kwaate kum əbnəə wɛ ghen nǎǎ ye chame-a, ghene kum əbnəə wɛ ghen nǎǎ ye nyok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And do not set your heart on what you will eat or drink; do not worry about it.

Oku_Text: Bek ka ghel-əbe-eyfɛl na leghe nè vin nè se kelee əbnəə nè vin abjim, dí Báǎba na ghen keele ge ghen koŋen əbnəə nè vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the pagan world runs after all such things, and your Father knows that you need them.

Oku_Text: Dí koŋeen tefon na Feyin, nɛ Feyine nǎǎ ye fo əbnəə nè vin abjim se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But seek his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.

Oku_Text: "Ghǎà ghen ye fan, wè te-wán-be-eysak, bek ka Báǎba na ghen me boyse eytem ge ab ye fo tefon na wen se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father has been pleased to give you the kingdom.

Oku_Text: Chə̀nseen əbnəə wɛ ghen nè lu nè wen di, è ghen fòen amkaa di se ghel-eykfəə. Neeyeen te-kfən-amkaa ghé é băǎ se gwel mok-a, gese nè amkaa-əbjuŋ ɛ eyshaa wɛ ab băǎ se mey mok-a, eysak wɛ wel-eychu băǎ se koo ɛ ten mok-a, nɛ eybe băǎ se chame əbnəə ɛ ten mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide purses for yourselves that will not wear out, a treasure in heaven that will never fail, where no thief comes near and no moth destroys.

Oku_Text: Bek ka eysak wɛ amkaa na ghen nè luun ɛ ten di, lu geese nɛ tetem ə ghen nǎǎ ye lu ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

Oku_Text: "Siyseen jii, è ghen boseen te-nduk na ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Be dressed ready for service and keep your lamps burning,

Oku_Text: Ghen neeyeen geese nɛ ghel-eyghoŋ ghé é nè kaken Bay na ghene ge ab ye lòò ɛ eynnyii-eykfiy-əbvii. Se lu ka ab me koo è kookse eychie-ndaa di, ghene me fole se wen geese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: like servants waiting for their master to return from a wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks they can immediately open the door for him.

Oku_Text: Ghel-eyghoŋ ghé é Bay na ghene nǎǎ ye gwiy è yen ge ghene nè kaken di, lu ghel eybose! Mɛ suuy se ghen ge Bay na ghene nǎǎ ye bose kfəl na wen, è ab me jame ghene è ab me fo eyshee se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them watching when he comes. Truly I tell you, he will dress himself to serve, will have them recline at the table and will come and wait on them.

Oku_Text: Lòó ab me gwiy ɛ tetem-əbchuu ghene ɛ teymmbo, è ab yen ge ghene nè kaken di, ghel fo lu ghel eybose!
English_NIV_Equivalent: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them ready, even if he comes in the middle of the night or toward daybreak.

Oku_Text: Keeleen geese ge lòó wele-kfə̀-ndaa me keeleen chuy wɛ wel-eychu nǎǎ ye gwiy di, ab băǎ se lake ge wel-eychu vi ye jame ɛ ndaa na wen mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.

Oku_Text: Ghen geese ghen siyseen jii, bek ka Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye gwiy ɛ əbliy wɛ ghen băǎ se keele ge ab nǎǎ ye gwiy mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him."

Oku_Text: Bita me bife ge: "Bay, wè nè suuyen njǎŋ nè vin se ghes geese nɛ, ghene wè nè suuyen se ghel ejim-a?"",Peter asked
English_NIV_Equivalent: Lord

Oku_Text: Bay me fise ge: "Wel-eyghoŋ-əbjuŋ wɛ ab nè keelen əbnəə lu ndɛ-a? Lu wel wɛ Bay na wen nǎǎ ye fii wen ɛ ketuu ghel-eyghoŋ na wen əmoke, ge ab ye se fo eyshee se ghene ɛ chuy na wen di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Lord answered, "Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their food allowance at the proper time?

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ wɛ Bay na wen nǎǎ ye gwiy è yen ge ab nè neeyennen sêy fo di, lu wel eybose.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It will be good for that servant whom the master finds doing so when he returns.

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge Bay na wen nǎǎ ye fii wen ɛ ketuu ntie na wen abjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Truly I tell you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.

Oku_Text: Dí lòó wel-eyghoŋ vi me suuy ɛ tetem ə wen ge: 'Bay na yom me yey geese ge ab kase fiile,' è ab me keete se ye fə̀lte ghel-eyghoŋ əmoke gese nè ghel-eyghoŋ-əbvii, è ab me se chame, ab nè nyok è ab me se yio-kfùk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But suppose that servant says to himself, ’My master is taking a long time in coming,’ and he then begins to beat the other servants, both men and women, and to eat and drink and get drunk.

Oku_Text: Bay na wel-eyghoŋ vi nǎǎ ye gwiy ɛ kechii wɛ ab băǎ se kaken mok-a, nɛ ɛ əbliy wɛ ab băǎ se keele mok-a. Bay na wen nǎǎ ye fii wen ɛ eysak na ghel ghé eb băǎ se beene mok-a, è ghene fə̀lte kfəl na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he is not aware of. He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ wɛ ab nè keelen əbnəə wɛ Bay na wen nè koŋen di, dí è ab băǎ se siyse jii ghene ab băǎ se neey əbnəə wɛ Bay na wen nè koŋen di mok-a, Feyine nǎǎ ye fə̀lte wen naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: "The servant who knows the master’s will and does not get ready or does not do what the master wants will be beaten with many blows.

Oku_Text: Dí wel-eyghoŋ wɛ ab băǎ se keele əbnəə wɛ Bay na wen nè koŋen di mok-a, è ab me neey əbe wɛ ab kelaanen ge ghene fə̀lte wen di, Feyine băǎ se fə̀lte wen naa se ŋaŋ mok-a. Wel ejim wɛ Feyine nè foen əbnəə sê eydio se wen, Feyine nǎǎ ye kase chakte əbnəə sê eydio se wen. Nɛ wel ejim wɛ Feyine nè fii wen ɛ ketuu əbnəə sê eydio, Feyine nǎǎ ye kase chakte sê eydio se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the one who does not know and does things deserving punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded; and from the one who has been entrusted with much, much more will be asked.

Oku_Text: "Mɛ me gwiy ge mɛ ye bose eynnyii ɛ eysak, nɛ mɛ me koŋen ge eynnyii di me seenmen geese!
English_NIV_Equivalent: "I have come to bring fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!

Oku_Text: Mɛ lu nè əbnəə sê eydio ge mɛ yen kensiyse, nɛ tetem ə yom băǎ se seen mok-a ndu è chin se lu ka əbnəə di nè meyse di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I have a baptism to undergo, and what constraint I am under until it is completed!

Oku_Text: Ghen keeleen ge mɛ me gwiy ge mɛ ye bose eyzəəte ɛ eysak-a? Mɛ suuy se ghen ge bɛey-a, dí mɛ me gwiy ge mɛ ye bose ambaanese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do you think I came to bring peace on earth? No, I tell you, but division.

Oku_Text: Keete ɛ kechii nè vin, lòó ghel keful eyfaame me lu ɛ ndaa əmoke, ghene nǎǎ ye baanese, tetal nè eghoo baane ghene baane nè eghoo tetal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: From now on there will be five in one family divided against each other, three against two and two against three.

Oku_Text: Ghene nǎǎ ye baanese, báǎba nè eghoo wán-kfəl, wán-kfəl nè eghoo báǎba, máǎma nè eghoo wán-əbvii, wán-əbvii nè eghoo máǎma, nɛ máǎma-kfə̀ nè eghoo wán-əbvii-kfə̀, wán-əbvii-kfə̀ nè eghoo máǎma-kfə̀."
English_NIV_Equivalent: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel ejim ge: "Lòó ghen me yen nchak nè lii ɛ eysak eybvase di, ghen suuy geese ge: 'əbnəə nǎǎ ye lǒ,' nɛ əbnəə me lǒ geese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to the crowd: "When you see a cloud rising in the west, immediately you say, ’It’s going to rain,’ and it does.

Oku_Text: Lòó chuy nè lii ɛ eytii di, ghen suuy ge: 'Eychii nǎǎ ye seen,' nɛ eychii me seen geese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And when the south wind blows, you say, ’It’s going to be hot,’ and it is.

Oku_Text: Ghen ghel-eychuu! Ghen nè keeleen eymok na eyshaa gese nè na ntie, dí ghen băǎ se keele əbnəə na leghe nè vin mok-a naa se ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky. How is it that you don’t know how to interpret this present time?

Oku_Text: "Ghen băǎ se fise əbnəə wɛ ab lu kelaanen mok-a naa se ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?

Oku_Text: Lòó wè nè nduun nè wel-eybee na vie ɛ ketuu wele-kfə̀-əbame di, neey kelaanen geese ɛ eynchii ge ghen neey eyzəəte, bek ka ab nǎǎ ye fii wè ɛ ketuu wele-kfə̀-əbame, nɛ wele-kfə̀-əbame me fii wè ɛ ketuu nchiyse-ndaa, è nchiyse-ndaa me fii wè ɛ ndaa-əbkuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As you are going with your adversary to the magistrate, try hard to be reconciled on the way, or your adversary may drag you off to the judge, and the judge turn you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se wè ge wè băǎ se lòò ɛ ten mok-a ndu è chin se lu ka wè me foen amkaa abjim wɛ ghene me kfiy se wè di."
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny."

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ ghel əmoke nè luun nè Jesus, è ghene fekte se wen kum ghel ə Galili ghé é Bay-Pilat nè fə̀ltemen ketuu na ghene di, è ammtə̀ na ghene me baanese nè na te-nyii-əbjuŋ na ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there were some present at that time who told Jesus about the Galileeans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: "Ghen keeleen ge ghel ə Galili nè vin me yenen kensiyse sêy bek ka ghene nè luun ghel-əbe geese nɛ ghel ə Galili əmoke-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, "Do you think that these Galileeans were worse sinners than all the other Galileeans because they suffered this way?

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge bɛey-a! Dí lòó ghen băǎ se kubsen tetem ə ghen mok-a, ghen abjim nǎǎ ye kuo geese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, no! But unless you repent, you too will all perish.

Oku_Text: Ghene kum ghel njon-sebaa gese nè baane ghé é nchii-kfən-ndaa ɛ Siloam nè gwelmen ɛ ketuu na ghene è fə̀lte ketuu na ghene di-a? Ghen keeleen ge ghene me luun ghel-əbe geese nɛ ghel ə Jolesalem əmoke-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Or those eighteen who died when the tower in Siloam fell on them—do you think they were more guilty than all the others living in Jerusalem?

Oku_Text: Mɛ suuy se ghen ge bɛey-a! Dí lòó ghen băǎ se kubsen tetem ə ghen mok-a, ghen abjim nǎǎ ye kuo geese."
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, no! But unless you repent, you too will all perish."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghene ge: "Wel əblee me lu nè tey-əbkuo wɛ ab nè é toŋte ge fik ɛ eysak-eyyale na wen. əb me ndu è kelee əbkuo ɛ ten, dí ab băǎ yen mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he told this parable: "A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard, and he went to look for fruit on it but did not find any.

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe ab me suuy se wel-eyyale na wen ge: 'Yen-a, mɛ me gwiymen naa se tefə̀ tetal ge mɛ ye kelee əbkuo ɛ tey nè vin, dí mɛ jia yen-a. Fə̀lten ketuu na wen! əb nè meyte eysak kɛ-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he said to the man who took care of the vineyard, ’For three years now I’ve been coming to look for fruit on this fig tree and haven’t found any. Cut it down! Why should it use up the soil?’

Oku_Text: Dí wel-eyyale vi me fise ge: 'Bay, lòò geese tey nè vin naa se tefə̀ nè vin. Mɛ nǎǎ ye chə̀lte teychie-nte na wen è mɛ bose amnduk ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: ’Sir,’ the man replied, ’leave it alone for one more year, and I’ll dig around it and fertilize it.

Oku_Text: Lòó ab me kfən ɛ tefə̀ nè lǒ di, ntie nǎǎ ye lu kelaanen. Dí lòó ab băǎ kfən mok-a, ken e leghe wè me fə̀lten ketuu na wen.'"
English_NIV_Equivalent: If it bears fruit next year, fine! If not, then cut it down.’"

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ kechii-eyzəəte nɛ Jesus nè fektemen ɛ ndaa-eyjame əmoke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On a Sabbath Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues,

Oku_Text: Wel-əbvii əblee me lu ɛ ten wɛ keyioy əbe me neeyenen wen wel-əbkùk naa se tefə̀ njon-sebaa gese nè baane. əb me kùken naa se eydio, è ab băǎ se lii kelaanen mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and a woman was there who had been crippled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over double and could not straighten up at all.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè yenen wen di, è me yio wen è suuy se wen ge: "Wel-əbvii, wè me boymen ɛ koon na vie!"
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw her, he called her forward and said to her, "Woman, you are set free from your infirmity."

Oku_Text: Jesus me fii eghoo na wen ɛ ketuu na wen. əb me lii kelaanen geese ɛ əbliy fo, è me kokse Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he put his hands on her, and immediately she straightened up and praised God.

Oku_Text: Dí wele-kfə̀-ndaa-eyjame me fɛlen eytem bek ka Jesus nè boysemen wel ɛ kechii-eyzəəte di. əb me suuy se ghel ejim ge: "Chuy keful eyfaame lu ɛ ten ge ghel ye neey sêy ɛ ten. Ghen gwiyeen ge ghen ye yen boyse ɛ kechii fo, dí ghǎà ghen ye gwiy ɛ kechii-eyzəəte mok-a."
English_NIV_Equivalent: Indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, the synagogue leader said to the people, "There are six days for work. So come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath."

Oku_Text: Bay me fise se wen ge: "Wè wel-əbe-əbchuu! Ghen abjim băǎ se kfaksen kfən na ghen ghene nnyii na ghen ɛ ndaa è fii wen è ndu è fo amchio ɛ kechii-eyzəəte mok-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Lord answered him, "You hypocrites! Doesn’t each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from the stall and lead it out to give it water?

Oku_Text: Nɛ wel-əbvii nè vin wɛ ab lu wán-əbvii we Abaham, wɛ Satan nè kə̀men wen naa se tefə̀ njon-sebaa gese nè baane nè vin-a? Ab băǎ kelaanen ge ghes kfaksen wen ɛ kechii-eyzəəte nè vin-a?"",Then should not this woman
English_NIV_Equivalent: a daughter of Abraham

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè suuyen sêy di, eysen me kookse ghel-eybee na wen abjim. Dí ghel ejim me laken naa se eydio kum əbnəə abghakene abjim wɛ Jesus nè neeyennen di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he said this, all his opponents were humiliated, but the people were delighted with all the wonderful things he was doing.

Oku_Text: Jesus me bife ge: "Tefon na Feyine lu geese nɛ ndɛ-a? Mɛ nǎǎ ye kfiy wen nè ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus asked, "What is the kingdom of God like? What shall I compare it to?

Oku_Text: Ab lu geese nɛ mntə̀-eynyen na maste wɛ wel əblee nè fii è ndu è yale ɛ eysak-eyyale na wen. Ab me lə̀ò è bǎǎ lu tey, nɛ te-nyen-te-eytaa me chiì teynyuu na ghene ɛ te-mchii na wen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: It is like a mustard seed, which a man took and planted in his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds perched in its branches."

Oku_Text: Jesus me gese bife ge: "Mɛ nǎǎ ye kfiy tefon na Feyine nè ndɛ-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Again he asked, "What shall I compare the kingdom of God to?

Oku_Text: Ab lu geese nɛ eychuu-eyshee wɛ wel-əbvii nè fii è baanese ɛ eghoo amchio-əbkaŋ-amnduk naa se amchio me baanese è lǒ abjim."
English_NIV_Equivalent: It is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into about sixty pounds of flour until it worked all through the dough."

Oku_Text: Jesus me lòò ɛ teychie-te-nteke gese nè teychie-te-teyioy nè fektemen è ab nè nduun ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus went through the towns and villages, teaching as he made his way to Jerusalem.

English_NIV_Equivalent: Lord

English_NIV_Equivalent: Once the owner of the house gets up and closes the door, you will stand outside knocking and pleading, ’Sir, open the door for us.’ But he will answer, ’I don’t know you or where you come from.’

Oku_Text: Dí ab nǎǎ ye kase fise se ghen ge: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge mɛ băǎ se keele eysak wɛ ghen nè lòòmen ɛ ten mok-a. Ghen lòòen ɛ mbiy na yom, ghen ghel ghé eb nè neeyennen əbe!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "But he will reply, ’I don’t know you or where you come from. Away from me, all you evildoers!’

Oku_Text: Ghen nǎǎ ye yen Abaham, Isiaak, Jakop gese nè tefon na Feyin abjim ɛ tefon na Feyin ə eytem, dí ghene me fii ghen ɛ eynjon. Ghen nǎǎ ye chə̀me eysheen è ghen kwaate.

English_NIV_Equivalent: Indeed there are those who are last who will be first, and first who will be last."

Oku_Text: Nè lu ɛ əbliy fo nɛ Balisi əmoke nè gwiyimen è suuy se Jesus ge: "Lòò ɛ ntie nè vin è ndu ɛ eysak əmoke, bek ka Helod koŋen ge ab ye fə̀lte wè."
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said to him, "Leave this place and go somewhere else. Herod wants to kill you."

Oku_Text: Lu bek kii nɛ mɛ nǎǎ ye ndu ɛ eynchii na yom ɛ chuy nè vin, ɛ lòó gese ɛ kechii nè lǒ di, bek ka Feyine băǎ se lake ge ghene fə̀lte wele ntum na wen ɛ eysak əmoke mok-a dí ka ɛ Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In any case, I must press on today and tomorrow and the next day—for surely no prophet can die outside Jerusalem!

English_NIV_Equivalent: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you were not willing.

Oku_Text: Yen-a, Feyine me lake ndaa na vie. Mɛ suuy se wè ge wè băǎ se kase yen mɛ mok-a ndu è chin se lu ka wè me suuy ge: 'Eyzəəte nǎǎ ye lu ɛ ketuu Wel wɛ ab nè gwiy ɛ eylake na Bay.'"
English_NIV_Equivalent: Look, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, ’Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.’"

Jesus asked the Pharisees and experts in the law, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath or not?""
