English_NIV_Equivalent: When he noticed how the guests picked the places of honor at the table, he told them this parable:

Oku_Text: "Lòó wel me yio wè ɛ eykfiy-əbvii di, ghǎà wè ye jame ɛ teychie-eyyio mok-a. Bek ka wel wɛ ab nganten wè nǎǎ ye lu ɛ ten,
English_NIV_Equivalent: "When someone invites you to a wedding feast, do not take the place of honor, for a person more distinguished than you may have been invited.

Oku_Text: nɛ wel wɛ ab me yio ghen abjim di nǎǎ ye gwiy è suuy se wè ge: 'Fò eysak se wel nè vin.' Ken e leghe eysen nǎǎ ye kookse wè, è wè me ndu è jame ɛ teychie wɛ ab lu ɛ mbiy di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If so, the host who invited both of you will come and say to you, ’Give this person your seat.’ Then, humiliated, you will have to take the least important place.

Oku_Text: Dí lòó wel me yio wè di, ndu è wè jame ɛ teychie-mbiy, ge lòó wel wɛ ab me yio wè di me gwiy di, ab ye suuy se wè ge: 'Babey na yom, ndu ɛ eyshaa eyyio.' Ken e leghe wè nǎǎ ye yen eyyio ɛ ketuu ghel ghé é nè chamen eyshee nè wè di abjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when you are invited, take the lowest place, so that when your host comes, he will say to you, ’Friend, move up to a better place.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all the other guests.

Oku_Text: Bek ka wel ejim wɛ ab nè lə̀ə̀sen kfəl na wen, Feyine nǎǎ ye shǎlte wen, nɛ wel wɛ ab nè shǎlten kfəl na wen, Feyine nǎǎ ye lə̀ə̀sen wen."
English_NIV_Equivalent: For all those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wel wɛ ab me yionen wen di ge: "Lòó wè nè neeyennen eykfiy-əbvii-əbchuu ghene wele-kfaksen-ntie di, ghǎà wè ye yio babey na vie, ghene te-nchek na vie, ghene ghel ɛ ndaa na vie, ghene ghel-eykfùk ghé é nè lu ɛ ntek ə eytem nè wè di mok-a. Bek ka ghene nǎǎ ye kase yio wè, è ghene me baanese əbnəə wɛ wè me neeyennen di se wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to his host, "When you give a luncheon or dinner, do not invite your friends, your brothers or sisters, your relatives, or your rich neighbors; if you do, they may invite you back and so you will be repaid.

Oku_Text: Dí lòó wè nè neeyennen eykfiy-əbvii di, yioen ghel-eykfəə, ghel ghé é nè koon-əbmey, ghel-eybùk gese nè ghel-eybense.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when you give a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, the blind,

Oku_Text: Wè nǎǎ ye lu wel eybose bek ka ghene băǎ lu nè amkaa ge ghene baanese se wè mok-a. Feyine nǎǎ ye baanese se wè ɛ əbliy-eynyak-əbnəə ɛ ketuu ntie-əbjuŋ na ghel-əbjuŋ."
English_NIV_Equivalent: and you will be blessed. Although they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous."

Oku_Text: Se lu ka wel əblee ɛ kebaaten ghel ghé é nè chamen eyshee nè wen di me yionen sêy di, è me suuy se Jesus ge: "Wel ejim wɛ ab nǎǎ ye jame eyshee ɛ tefon na Feyin di lu wel eybose!"
English_NIV_Equivalent: When one of those at the table with him heard this, he said to Jesus, "Blessed is the one who will eat at the feast in the kingdom of God."

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: "Wel əblee me neeyennen eykfiy-əbvii-əbchuu, è ab me yio ghel sê eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, "A certain man was preparing a great banquet and invited many guests.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka əbliy-eykfiy-əbvii me koo di, ab me tum wel-eyghoŋ na wen ge ab ye suuy se ghel ghé é ab nè yionen di ge: 'Gwiyeen, bek ka əbnəə abjim me siysemen jii.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: At the time of the banquet he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, ’Come, for everything is now ready.’

Oku_Text: Dí ghene abjim me keete è se kfiy ntie na ghene. Wel wɛ ab nè eychie di me suuy ge: 'Mɛ me chə̀nsemen eysak, nɛ mɛ nè koŋen ge mɛ ndu mɛ ye yen eysak fo. Mɛ nè chakte wè ge wè kfiy ntie na yom.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "But they all alike began to make excuses. The first said, ’I have just bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please excuse me.’

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke me suuy ge: 'Mɛ me chə̀nsemen nnyii njon-sebaa, nɛ mɛ nè nduun ge mɛ ye yen-a ge ghene nǎǎ ye bɛey neey sêy-a. Mɛ nè chakte wè ge wè kfiy ntie na yom.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Another said, ’I have just bought five yoke of oxen, and I’m on my way to try them out. Please excuse me.’

Oku_Text: Wel əmoke gese me suuy ge: 'Mɛ me bosemen əbvii nè vin, lu bek kii nɛ mɛ băǎ bɛey gwiy mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "Still another said, ’I just got married, so I can’t come.’

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ vi me kase fiile è fekte se Bay na wen əbnəə abjim. Ken e leghe wele-kfə̀-ndaa me fɛlen eytem, è me suuy se wel-eyghoŋ na wen ge: 'Ndu ɛ eynjon geese ɛ te-ntek gese nè ɛ te-teyioy, è wè fii ghel-eykfəə, ghel ghé é nè koon-əbmey, ghel-eybense gese nè ghel-eybùk nè gwiy ɛ ten.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: "The servant came back and reported this to his master. Then the owner of the house became angry and ordered his servant, ’Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the town and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind and the lame.’

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ vi me suuy ge: 'Bay, mɛ me neeymen geese nɛ wè nè suuy di, dí eysak lu gese ɛ ten.'

Oku_Text: 'Ab me yio è suuy ge: "Báǎba Abaham, kfiy eytem ɛ ketuu na yom è wè tum Lazalos ge ab ye chòte eynchie-eghoo na wen ɛ ammtə̀ è ab me koonen eylem na yom, bek ka mɛ nè yen kensiyse naa se eydio ɛ eynnyii nè vin.""",'So he called to him
English_NIV_Equivalent: Father Abraham

Oku_Text: 'Dí Abaham me fise se wen ge: "Wán na yom, keeleen ge se lu ka wè nè luun ɛ eysak di, wè me bosen amkaa-əbjuŋ, nɛ Lazalos me yen amkaa-əbe. Dí nè vin, ab nè lake eytem ɛ ntie nè vin, dí wè nè se yenen kensiyse."",'But Abraham replied
English_NIV_Equivalent: Son

Oku_Text: '"Nɛ ɛ ketuu əbnəə nè vin abjim di, eyjame nè vin me chiì ɛ kebaaten ə ghes nè ghen, ge ghel ghé eb nè koŋen ge ghene lǒ ɛ mbiy fo ge ghene gwiy ɛ ntie nè vin di bǎǎ se neey mok-a, ghene ghel ghé eb nè koŋen ge ghene lǒ ɛ mbiy fo ge ghene gwiy ɛ ntie nè vin di bǎǎ se neey mok-a.""",'""And besides all this
English_NIV_Equivalent: between us and you a great chasm has been fixed

Oku_Text: 'Wel-eykfùk vi me suuy ge: "Eya, báǎba, mɛ nè chakte wè ge wè tum Lazalos ɛ ndaa na báǎba na yom, bek ka mɛ lu nè te-nchek tetal ɛ ten. Ab ye suuy se ghene ge ghene ghǎà ye gwiy ɛ eysak-eynyuu nè vin mok-a.""",'He answered
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then I beg you

Oku_Text: 'Dí Abaham me fise ge: "Moses gese nè tefon na Feyin me lu nè ghene, ghene yioen əbnəə wɛ ghene me suuy di."",'Abraham replied
English_NIV_Equivalent: They have Moses and the Prophets; let them listen to them."""

Oku_Text: 'Wel-eykfùk vi me fise ge: "Bɛey-a, báǎba Abaham, dí lòó wel me lǒ ɛ kebaaten ghel ghé é nè kuomen di è ab ndu ɛ eghoo na ghene di, ghene nǎǎ ye kubsen tetem ə ghene.""",'""No
English_NIV_Equivalent: father Abraham

Oku_Text: 'Dí Abaham me suuy se wen ge: "Lòó ghene bǎǎ bɛey yio Moses gese nè tefon na Feyin mok-a, ghene bǎǎ bɛey yio wel moke wɛ ab me kase fiile ɛ eytem ɛ ketuu ghel ghé é nè kuomen di mok-a."",'He said to him
English_NIV_Equivalent: If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets

Oku_Text: əbnəə ghé é nè neeyennen ghel ge ghene neey əbe
English_NIV_Equivalent: Sin, Faith, Duty

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge: 'əbnəə ghé é nè neeyennen ghel ge ghene neey əbe di nǎǎ ye lu geese.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to his disciples: 'Things that cause people to stumble are bound to come,'

Oku_Text: 'Dí wel wɛ ab nè neeyennen ghel ge ghene neey əbe di nǎǎ ye yen kensiyse naa se eydio!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'but woe to anyone through whom they come.'

Oku_Text: 'Ab nǎǎ ye bǎǎ lu əbjuŋ se wen ge ghene kə̀ fə̀ eychie-əbkuo ɛ ketuu nnyii na wen è ghene fii wen ɛ eynnyii-eykfiy geese nɛ ge ab neey wel moke ɛ kebaaten te-wán-be-eysak nè vin ge ab neey əbe.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'It would be better for them to be thrown into the sea with a millstone tied around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.'

Oku_Text: 'Ghen boyseen ghen-moke!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'So watch yourselves!'

Oku_Text: 'Lòó nchek na vie me neey əbe di, fə̀lte wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'If your brother or sister sins against you, rebuke them;'

Oku_Text: 'Lòó ab me kubsen tetem ə wen di, lòòmen əbe di se wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'and if they repent, forgive them.'

Oku_Text: 'Lòó ab me neey əbe ɛ ketuu na vie naa se chuy njon-sebaane ɛ chuy moke di, è ab me kase fiile ɛ eghoo na vie naa se chuy njon-sebaane è suuy ge: "Mɛ me kubsen tetem ə yom," lòòmen əbe di se wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Even if they sin against you seven times in a day and seven times come back to you saying "I repent," you must forgive them.'

Oku_Text: əbliy-eyseen
English_NIV_Equivalent: Faith and Duty

Oku_Text: Nchiyse-ndaa me suuy se Bay ge: 'Boyse əbliy-eyseen ə ghes.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: The apostles said to the Lord, 'Increase our faith!'

Oku_Text: Bay me fise se ghene ge: 'Lòó əbliy-eyseen ə ghen me luun nchichi geese nɛ mntə̀-eynyen na maste di, ghen nǎǎ ye bɛey suuy se tey-əbkuo nè vin ge: "Fə̀lte wè ɛ eysak nè vin è wè ndu è wè fik ɛ eynnyii-eykfiy," nɛ tey nè vin nǎǎ ye yio kenəə na ghen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, 'If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, "Be uprooted and planted in the sea," and it will obey you.'

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ nè eychie na wen
English_NIV_Equivalent: Servant and Duty

Oku_Text: 'Ndɛ-a ɛ kebaaten ə ghen wɛ ab lu nè wel-eyghoŋ wɛ ab nè yale eysak ghene ab nè keelen te-nnyii, nɛ wel-eyghoŋ vi me gwiy ɛ eysak di, ab nǎǎ ye bɛey suuy se wen ge: "Gwiye geese wè jame è wè chame eyshee"-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Suppose one of you has a servant plowing or looking after the sheep, and he comes in from the field. Will you say to him, "Come along now and sit down to eat"?'

Oku_Text: 'Bɛey-a, ab nǎǎ ye suuy se wen ge: "Siyse eyshee na yom, è wè boyse kfəl na vie è wè bɛey eyshee se mɛ ndu è chin se lu ka mɛ me chame è mɛ nyok di, ken e leghe wè me kase chame è wè nyok"?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Won't you rather say, "Prepare my supper, get yourself ready and wait on me while I eat and drink; after that you may eat and drink"?'

Oku_Text: 'Wel-eyghoŋ vi me neey əbnəə wɛ Bay na wen nè suuy di, dí Bay vi băǎ bɛey lake eytem ɛ ketuu na wen mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Will he thank the servant because he did what he was told to do?'

Oku_Text: 'Lu geese nɛ ghen abjim, lòó ghen me neeymen əbnəə abjim wɛ Feyine nè suuy di, suuyeen ge: "Ghes lu te-wel-eyghoŋ ghé eb nè neeyennen eychie na ghes, ghes me neeymen ntie wɛ ab kelaanen ge ghes neey di."",'So you also
English_NIV_Equivalent: when you have done everything you were told to do

Oku_Text: Jesus boyse ghel njon ghé é nè koon-əbmey
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus Heals Ten Men With Leprosy

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè nduun ɛ Jolesalem di, ab me lòò ɛ kebaaten ə Samalia gese nè Galili.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now on his way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ab nè jame ɛ ntek əblee di, ghel njon ghé é nè koon-əbmey me lòò wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he was going into a village, ten men who had leprosy met him.

Oku_Text: Ghene me lii è ghene lə̀ə̀ ɛ əbkwak, è ghene yio naa se ŋaŋ ge: 'Jesus, Ndinte, kfiy eytem ɛ ketuu na ghes!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: They stood at a distance and called out in a loud voice, 'Jesus, Master, have pity on us!'

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè yenen ghene di, è me suuy se ghene ge: 'Nduen ghen ye yen te-wele-kfə̀-əbame.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he saw them, he said, 'Go, show yourselves to the priests.'

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghene nè nduun di, ghene me boy geese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And as they went, they were cleansed.

Oku_Text: Wel moke ɛ kebaaten na ghene me yen ge ab me boymen, è ab me kase fiile è ab nè kokse Feyin naa se ŋaŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God in a loud voice.

Oku_Text: Ab me gwiy è ab me kuo ɛ ketuu teychak na Jesus è ab nè lake eytem ɛ ketuu na wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He threw himself at Jesus' feet and thanked him—

Oku_Text: Wel nè vin nè lu wel ə Samalia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and he was a Samaritan.

Oku_Text: Jesus me bife ge: 'Ghes băǎ boyse ghel njon mok-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus asked, 'Were not all ten cleansed?'

Oku_Text: 'Ghel baane-ge-baane nè lu ndɛ-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Where are the other nine?'

Oku_Text: 'Bɛey wel moke ɛ kebaaten na ghene me kase fiile ge ab ye kokse Feyin mok-a dí ka wel-eychu nè vin-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Has no one returned to give praise to God except this foreigner?'

Oku_Text: Ken e leghe Jesus me suuy se wen ge: 'Lii wè ndu, əbliiy-eyseen ə vie me boyse wè.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to him, 'Rise and go; your faith has made you well.'

Oku_Text: Gwiy-eytefon na Feyin
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Coming of the Kingdom of God

Oku_Text: Balisi me bife Jesus kum əbliiy wɛ tefon na Feyin nǎǎ ye gwiy di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once, on being asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come,

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: 'Tefon na Feyin băǎ se gwiy geese nɛ əbnəə wɛ ghel nǎǎ ye yen nè eynnyii mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, 'The coming of the kingdom of God is not something that can be observed,'

Oku_Text: 'Wel băǎ bɛey suuy ge: "Yen-a, ab lu nè vin," ghene ge: "Ab lu nè fo" mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'nor will people say, "Here it is," or "There it is,"'

Oku_Text: 'Bek ka tefon na Feyin lu ɛ tetem ə ghen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'because the kingdom of God is in your midst.'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge: 'əbliiy nǎǎ ye gwiy wɛ ghen nǎǎ ye koŋen ge ghen yen chuy moke we Wán-wel-eyfɛl, dí ghen băǎ se yen mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said to his disciples, 'The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.'

Oku_Text: 'Ghel nǎǎ ye suuy se ghen ge: "Yen-a, ab lu nè fo," ghene ge: "Ab lu nè vin."'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'People will tell you, "There he is!" or "Here he is!"'

Oku_Text: 'Ghǎà ghen ye ndu, ghene ge ghen ye fole ghene mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Do not go running off after them.'

Oku_Text: 'Bek ka geese nɛ eynnyii-eyseen nè lii ɛ ntie moke ɛ eyshaa na eysak è ab me lə̀te ndu è chin ɛ ntie əmoke di, lu geese nɛ Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye lu ɛ chuy na wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'For the lightning, such as flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí ab kelaanen ge ab ye yen kensiyse naa se eydio geese, è ghel na leghe nè vin nǎǎ ye ntanle wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.'

Oku_Text: 'Geese nɛ ab nè luun ɛ te-chuy na Noa di, lu geese nɛ ab nǎǎ ye bǎǎ lu ɛ te-chuy na Wán-wel-eyfɛl.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man.'

Oku_Text: 'Ghel nè chamen eyshee, ghene nè nyok, è ghel-eyfɛl nè bosen əbvii, è ghel-əbvii nè nduun ɛ eghoo əbvii ndu è chin ɛ chuy wɛ Noa nè jame ɛ eychie-əbkuo di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'People were eating, drinking, marrying and being given in marriage up to the day Noah entered the ark.'

Oku_Text: 'Nnyii me gwiy è ab me fə̀lte ghene abjim.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Then the flood came and destroyed them all.'

Oku_Text: 'Lu geese nɛ ab nè luun ɛ te-chuy na Lot di: Ghel nè chamen eyshee, ghene nè nyok, ghene nè chə̀nse amkaa, ghene nè fòen amkaa, ghene nè yale eysak è ghene nè chiì te-ndaa.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'It was the same in the days of Lot. People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí ɛ chuy wɛ Lot nè lòòmen ɛ Sodom di, eynnyii gese nè amchio-eyjame me gwelmen ɛ eyshaa geese nɛ eynnyii-eyseen è ab me fə̀lte ghene abjim.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.'

Oku_Text: 'Lu geese nɛ ab nǎǎ ye lu ɛ chuy wɛ Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ eynnyii di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'It will be just like this on the day the Son of Man is revealed.'

Oku_Text: 'ɛ chuy fo, wel ejim wɛ ab nǎǎ ye lu ɛ ketuu kfən-ndaa, nɛ amkaa na wen lu ɛ ndaa ɛ tetem di, ghǎà ab ye jame ge ab ye fii amkaa na wen mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'On that day no one who is on the housetop, with possessions inside the house, should go down to get them.'

Oku_Text: 'Lu geese nɛ wel ejim wɛ ab nǎǎ ye lu ɛ eysak-eyyale di, ghǎà ab ye kase fiile ge ab ye fii amkaa na wen mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Likewise, no one in the field should go back for anything.'

Oku_Text: 'Keeleen kum əbvii we Lot.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Remember Lot's wife!'

Oku_Text: 'Wel ejim wɛ ab nè koŋen ge ab ye boyse kfəl na wen, ab nǎǎ ye meymene, dí wel ejim wɛ ab nè meymenen kfəl na wen, ab nǎǎ ye boyse.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Whoever tries to keep their life will lose it, and whoever loses their life will preserve it.'

Oku_Text: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge: ɛ əbchuu fo, ghel baane nǎǎ ye bɛey chie ɛ ketuu eynchie əmoke; Feyine nǎǎ ye fii moke è ab me lake moke.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.'

Oku_Text: 'Ghel-əbvii baane nǎǎ ye saanken ge ghene saanken amchio; Feyine nǎǎ ye fii moke è ab me lake moke.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Two women will be grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.'

Oku_Text: '(Ghel baane nǎǎ ye lu ɛ eysak-eyyale; Feyine nǎǎ ye fii moke è ab me lake moke.)'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other left.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me bife wen ge: 'Bay, əbnəə nè vin nǎǎ ye lòò ɛ ndɛ-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Where, Lord?' they asked.

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: 'ɛ eysak wɛ kfəl-eykuo nè luun ɛ ten di, lu fo nɛ teychie nǎǎ ye saankennen ɛ ten.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, 'Where there is a dead body, there the vultures will gather.'

Oku_Text: Njǎŋ kum wel-əbvii-əbchuu nè wele-kfə̀-nduŋ
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Parable of the Persistent Widow

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghel eyghoŋ na wen ge ab ye neeyennen ghene ge ghene nǎǎ ye se kookse Feyin naa se eydio è ghǎà tetem ə ghene ye mey mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus told his disciples a parable to show them that they should always pray and not give up.

Oku_Text: Ab me suuy ge: 'Ntek əblee me lu wɛ wele-kfə̀-nduŋ əblee me lu ɛ ten, wɛ ab băǎ se kfiy Feyin mok-a, nɛ ab băǎ kase lu nè eyyio ɛ ketuu wel mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said: 'In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor cared what people thought.'

Oku_Text: 'Wel-əbvii-əbchuu əblee me lu ɛ ntek fo wɛ ab nè se nduun ɛ eghoo na wele-kfə̀-nduŋ vi è suuy ge: "Siyse ntie na yom ɛ ketuu wel wɛ ab nè neeyennen əbe ɛ ketuu na yom."",'And there was a widow in that town who kept coming to him with the plea
English_NIV_Equivalent: Grant me justice against my adversary.""" Prada"

Oku_Text: 'Wele-kfə̀-nduŋ vi me jia koŋen naa se tefə̀.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'For some time he refused.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí ken e leghe ab me suuy ɛ tetem ə wen ge: "Dí mɛ nè băǎ se kfiy Feyin mok-a, nɛ mɛ nè băǎ lu nè eyyio ɛ ketuu wel mok-a di, mɛ nǎǎ ye siyse ntie na wel-əbvii-əbchuu nè vin bek ka ab nè meyte mɛ.'""
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But finally he said to himself, "Even though I don't fear God or care what people think, yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will see that she gets justice,"'

Oku_Text: '"Dí mɛ băǎ se neey mok-a, ab nǎǎ ye se gwiy ndu è chin se lu ka ab me neey mɛ ge mɛ mey."",'""so that she won't eventually come and attack me!""'" Prada"
Bay me gese suuy ge: 'Yioen əbnəə wɛ wele-kfə̀-nduŋ-əbe vi nè suuy di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: And the Lord said, 'Listen to what the unjust judge says.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí Feyine băǎ se siyse ntie na ghel ghé ab nè keelen ghene di, ghé eb nè kookse wen ɛ chuy gese nè ɛ əbchuu mok-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'And will not God bring about justice for his chosen ones, who cry out to him day and night?'

Oku_Text: 'Ab nǎǎ ye lə̀ə̀ se ghene-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Will he keep putting them off?'

Oku_Text: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge ab nǎǎ ye siyse ntie na ghene geese ɛ əbliiy fo.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I tell you, he will see that they get justice, and quickly.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí se lu ka Wán-wel-eyfɛl nǎǎ ye gwiy di, ab nǎǎ ye yen əbliiy-eyseen ɛ eyshaa-eyyale-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'However, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?'

Oku_Text: Njǎŋ kum Balisi gese nè wele-nyak-amkaa-tefon
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Parable of the Pharisee and the Tax Collector

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy njǎŋ nè vin se ghel ghé eb nè keeleen ge ghene lu ghel-əbjuŋ, è ghene me ntanle ghel əmoke abjim di ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: To some who were confident of their own righteousness and looked down on everyone else, Jesus told this parable:

Oku_Text: 'Ghel baane me jame ɛ ndaa-eyjame ge ghene ye kookse Feyin: Wel moke lu Balisi, nɛ wel əmoke lu wele-nyak-amkaa-tefon.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.'

Oku_Text: 'Balisi me lii è ab nè kookse Feyin ɛ tetem ə wen ge: "Feyin, mɛ nè lake eytem ɛ ketuu na vie, bek ka mɛ băǎ lu geese nɛ ghel əmoke mok-a: ghel-eychuu, ghel-əbe, ghel-eybvase ghene geese nɛ wele-nyak-amkaa-tefon nè vin di."",'The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed: ""God
English_NIV_Equivalent: I thank you that I am not like other people—robbers

Oku_Text: '"Mɛ nè băǎ se chame eyshee naa se chuy baane ɛ chuy njon-sebaa moke-moke, è mɛ nè fii amkaa njon ɛ ketuu amkaa njon-sebaane abjim wɛ mɛ nè yenen di."",'""I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.""" Prada"
'Dí wele-nyak-amkaa-tefon vi me lə̀ə̀ ɛ əbkwak.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But the tax collector stood at a distance.'

Oku_Text: 'Ab băǎ bɛey koŋen ge ab ye lii eytem ɛ eyshaa mok-a, dí ab me kùùne kfəl na wen è suuy ge: "Feyin, kfiy eytem ɛ ketuu na yom wɛ mɛ lu wel-əbe!""
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, "God, have mercy on me, a sinner." Prada"

Oku_Text: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Wel nè vin me kase fiile ɛ ndaa na wen è Feyine me lake wen, dí Balisi vi băǎ bɛey lake wen mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I tell you that this man, rather than the other, went home justified before God.'

Oku_Text: 'Bek ka wel ejim wɛ ab nè lə̀ə̀sen kfəl na wen, Feyine nǎǎ ye shǎlte wen, nɛ wel wɛ ab nè shǎlten kfəl na wen, Feyine nǎǎ ye lə̀ə̀sen wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'For all those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.'

Oku_Text: Jesus boyse te-wán-be-nchichi
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Little Children and Jesus

Oku_Text: Ghel nè fii te-wán-be-nchichi na ghene se Jesus ge ab ye fii eghoo na wen ɛ ketuu na ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: People were also bringing babies to Jesus for him to place his hands on them.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ghel eyghoŋ nè yenen di, ghene me fə̀lte ghel ghé é nè fii te-wán di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.

Oku_Text: Dí Jesus me yio te-wán di è suuy ge: 'Lakeen te-wán-be-nchichi ge ghene gwiy ɛ eghoo na yom, ghǎà ghen ye fə̀lte ghene mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Jesus called the children to him and said, 'Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them,'

Oku_Text: 'Bek ka tefon na Feyin lu kfəl ghel ghé eb lu geese nɛ ghene nè vin di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.'

Oku_Text: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Wel ejim wɛ ab băǎ se jame tefon na Feyin geese nɛ wán-əbnyinyek mok-a, ab băǎ bɛey jame ɛ ten mok-a.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Truly I tell you, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.'

Oku_Text: Wele-kfùk-əbnyinyek
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Rich and the Kingdom of God

Oku_Text: Wel-eyghoŋ əblee me bife Jesus ge: 'Ndinte-əbjuŋ, mɛ nǎǎ ye neey ndɛ-a ge mɛ ye yen eytem-eyzəəte-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: A certain ruler asked him, 'Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?'

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se wen ge: 'Wè nè yio mɛ ge mɛ lu wel-əbjuŋ bek kii-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Why do you call me good?' Jesus answered.

Oku_Text: 'Wel-əbjuŋ băǎ lu mok-a dí ka Feyin moke.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'No one is good—except God alone.'

Oku_Text: 'Wè me keeleen nchil: "Ghǎà wè ye neey eybvase mok-a, ghǎà wè ye fə̀lte wel mok-a, ghǎà wè ye chuu mok-a, ghǎà wè ye suuy eynchuu ɛ ketuu wel mok-a, kfiy eyyio ɛ ketuu báǎba na vie gese nè máǎma na vie."",'You know the commandments: ""You shall not commit adultery
English_NIV_Equivalent: you shall not murder

Oku_Text: Wel vi me fise ge: 'Mɛ me se neeyennen sêy nè vin abjim naa se lu ka mɛ nè luun wán-əbnyinyek di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'All these I have kept since I was a boy,' he said.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè yioyen sêy di, è me suuy se wen ge: 'əbnəə moke lu gese wɛ wè băǎ se neey mok-a: Ndu è wè fò amkaa na vie abjim è wè fii amkaa-əbkaŋ di se ghel-eykfəə.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, 'You still lack one thing. Sell everything you have and give to the poor,'

Oku_Text: 'Wè nǎǎ ye yen amkaa-əbjuŋ ɛ eyshaa-eyyale.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'and you will have treasure in heaven.'

Oku_Text: 'Ken e leghe wè me gwiy è wè fole mɛ.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Then come, follow me.'

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ab me yioyen sêy di, eytem na wen me mey bek ka ab nè luun wel-eykfùk-əbchuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he heard this, he became very sad, because he was very wealthy.

Oku_Text: Jesus me yen ge eytem na wen me meymen, è me suuy ge: 'əb lu əbnəə-əbmba kelaanen ge ghel-eykfùk ye jame ɛ tefon na Feyin!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus looked at him and said, 'How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!'

Oku_Text: 'əb lu nchichi ge nnyii-wey nè vin-a (kamel) ye jame ɛ eychie-nduk-eychul-nduk geese nɛ ge wele-kfùk ye jame ɛ tefon na Feyin.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.'

Oku_Text: Ghel ghé eb nè yion di me bife ge: 'Lòó ab me lu geese di, ndɛ-a nǎǎ ye boyse-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those who heard this asked, 'Who then can be saved?'

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise ge: 'əbnəə ghé ghel băǎ bɛey neey mok-a lu əbnəə ghé Feyine nè bɛey neey.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, 'What is impossible with man is possible with God.'

Oku_Text: Peter me suuy ge: 'Yen-a, ghes me lake ndaa na ghes è ghes fole wè.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Peter said to him, 'We have left all we had to follow you!'

Oku_Text: Jesus me fise se ghene ge: 'Mɛ suuy se ghen ge: Wel ejim wɛ ab nè lake ndaa na wen, ghene əbvii na wen, ghene te-nchek na wen, ghene báǎba na wen, ghene máǎma na wen, ghene te-wán na wen ɛ ketuu tefon na Feyin di,'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Truly I tell you,' Jesus said to them, 'no one who has left home or wife or brothers or sisters or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God'

Oku_Text: 'ab nǎǎ ye yen geese naa se tefə̀ sê eydio ɛ əbliiy nè vin, è ab me yen eytem-eyzəəte ɛ əbliiy wɛ ab nè gwiyimen nè vin di.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'will fail to receive many times as much in this age, and in the age to come eternal life.'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise geese kum kuo na wen
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus Predicts His Death a Third Time

Oku_Text: Jesus me fii ghel njon-sebaa-ge-baane vi è suuy se ghene ge: 'Yeneen-a, ghes nè nduun ɛ Jolesalem nè vin, nɛ əbnəə abjim wɛ tefon na Feyin nè chə̀men ɛ ketuu Wán-wel-eyfɛl di nǎǎ ye lòò.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus took the Twelve aside and told them, 'We are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written by the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.'

Oku_Text: 'Ghene nǎǎ ye fii wen ɛ eghoo ghel-eychu, è ghene nǎǎ ye kùùne wen, ghene ntanle wen, è ghene saafə̀ wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'He will be delivered over to the Gentiles. They will mock him, insult him and spit on him;'

Oku_Text: 'Ghene nǎǎ ye baase wen è ghene fə̀lte wen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'they will flog him and kill him.'

Oku_Text: 'Dí nè lu ɛ chuy ketal nɛ ab nǎǎ ye kase fiile ɛ eytem.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'On the third day he will rise again.'

Oku_Text: Dí ghel eyghoŋ na wen băǎ se yio əbnəə nè vin mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The disciples did not understand any of this.

Oku_Text: Ntie sêy nè vin me luun kfə̀men ɛ ketuu na ghene, è ghene băǎ se keele əbnəə wɛ Jesus nè suuy di mok-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Its meaning was hidden from them, and they did not know what he was talking about.

Oku_Text: Jesus boyse wel-eybense ɛ Jeliko
English_NIV_Equivalent: A Blind Beggar Receives His Sight

Oku_Text: Se lu ka Jesus nè kooksemen ɛ Jeliko di, wel-eybense moke me chie ɛ eychie-ndaa è ab nè lə̀ò amkaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ab me yioyen ghel sê eydio nè lòò di, ab me bife ge: 'əbnəə ghɛ-a nè se lòò nè vin-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.

Oku_Text: Ghene me fise se wen ge: 'Jesus we Nasalet nè lòò.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: They told him, 'Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.'

Oku_Text: Ab me yio ge: 'Jesus, Wán we Dafit, kfiy eytem ɛ ketuu na yom!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: He called out, 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!'

Oku_Text: Ghel ghé eb nè nduun ɛ mbiy di me fə̀lte wen ge ab ye lə̀ə̀.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Those who led the way rebuked him and told him to be quiet,

Oku_Text: Dí ab me gese yio geese ge: 'Wán we Dafit, kfiy eytem ɛ ketuu na yom!'
English_NIV_Equivalent: but he shouted all the more, 'Son of David, have mercy on me!'

Oku_Text: Jesus me lə̀ə̀ è me suuy ge ghene fii wen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus stopped and ordered the man to be brought to him.

English_NIV_Equivalent: When he came near, Jesus asked him,

Oku_Text: 'Wè nè koŋen ge mɛ neey ndɛ-a se wè-a?'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'What do you want me to do for you?'

Oku_Text: Ab me fise ge: 'Bay, mɛ nè koŋen ge mɛ ye yen.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Lord, I want to see,' he replied.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: 'Yen-a, əbliiy-eyseen ə vie me boyse wè.'
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to him, 'Receive your sight; your faith has healed you.'

Oku_Text: Ab me yen geese ɛ əbliiy fo, è me fole Jesus è ab nè kokse Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, praising God.

Oku_Text: Ghel ejim me yen di, è ghene abjim me kokse Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When all the people saw it, they also praised God.

Oku_Text: Jesus ndu è lii e Jeliko è se diansene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through.

Oku_Text: Wel ablee nè lu fo ne é tonte ge Zakaeus, nè lu ketuu se ghel eyfii shile, gese lu wel abghaken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A man was there by the name of Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was wealthy.

Oku_Text: Əb nè kɛŋe se yene wel we luun Jesus, di ke se taa bek ngose wel ye ey nè luun fo nè ǝb gese lu wel ǝbtəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He wanted to see who Jesus was, but because he was short he could not see over the crowd.

Oku_Text: Əb me nyin è ndu se mbiy è kok se fekake sikamole ka ab yen, bek ka Jesus nè chian & jii fo.

English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus reached the spot, he looked up and said to him, 'Zacchaeus, come down immediately. I must stay at your house today.'

Oku_Text: Əb me səǝke chikiy chikiy è fiisen Jesus nè eysanlee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he came down at once and welcomed him gladly.

Oku_Text: Ghel ɛjime yen kenǝǝ ten è se vəke ge: Əb se nduu se chii ghene wele mbeese.
English_NIV_Equivalent: All the people saw this and began to mutter, 'He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.'

Oku_Text: É ndu è lii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Zacchaeus stood up

Oku_Text: Zakaeus ney teem è suuy se Jesus ge: Yen-a Báàba, mẹ nêy gianen ǝbfua ә wom e feteten, è fo eybee eylee se ghe ketane.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we me nè boonmen lu ne me seese ǝblik abkeek. Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Ghele ghe ndaa fene boymen lan bek ge wel vin lu sey wán ə Ablaham.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to him, 'Today salvation has come to this house, because this man, too, is a son of Abraham.

Oku_Text: Bek ge Wanwel nè gwiy se ken, se gese se boyse ghii dii ghe é leyenmen.

English_NIV_Equivalent: He said: 'A man of noble birth went to a distant country to have himself appointed king and then to return.

English_NIV_Equivalent: 'He was made king, however, and returned home. Then he sent for the servants to whom he had given the money, in order to find out what they had gained with it.

English_NIV_Equivalent: '"Because you have been trustworthy in a very small matter, take charge of ten cities."

Oku_Text: Əblee me gwiy, è suuy ge: Báàba, kaba a yie yi yine ka me nè kulen e kensake ndəyse è lete.

English_NIV_Equivalent:
I was afraid of you, because you are a hard man. You take out what you did not put in and reap what you did not sow.""

Oku_Text: Wê ne ke ge me lu wel eyshie eytaa, lie ke me báà lem gese kaa ke me báà vii.

Oku_Text: Ghene fise ge: Báàba shii keghene,

English_NIV_Equivalent: When he came near the place where the road goes down the Mount of Olives, the whole crowd of disciples began joyfully to praise God in loud voices for all the miracles they had seen:

Oku_Text: ǝb fise ge: Me suuye se ghen ge: Ghene gok e chichi, é etiye ye lak.

Oku_Text: Jesus loo baksen, è mak eshie è yen Jolesalem, ketuu diile wen, ab səkse əmnshie, è kèsèn nè eydiye, Suuye ge: Wel lie ge ghen ke kenǝǝ ke lòó ke neɛy é ghene ghe Feyine ye lu kejun lan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As he approached Jerusalem and saw the city, he wept over it and said, 'If you, even you, had only known on this day what would bring you peace—'

English_NIV_Equivalent: 'They will dash you to the ground, you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another,'

Oku_Text: Suuye se ghene ge: É nyak e ŋwaale-Feyin ge: Ndaa a yom lu ndaa eyjem, ghene sey me bense se mbvək echorŋ.

English_NIV_Equivalent: One day as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple courts and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, together with the elders, came up to him,

English_NIV_Equivalent: 'John's baptism—was it from heaven, or of human origin?'

Oku_Text: Satan me lii se eytem na Judas we é nè tonten ge Iskaliot, we nè luun wel ǝblee e mame ghon-eyfel na Jesus ghe njio-sebaa eyvəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, one of the Twelve.

Oku_Text: ǝb me ndu è yen ghele mbiy se ghé ntaŋlee, ghene ǝbtuu se ghel ghe é chiyen ndaa-eykokse Feyin, ghene ghene se siyse jii ye ǝb nêy din Jesus é koo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And Judas went to the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard and discussed with them how he might betray Jesus.

Oku_Text: Ghene yio kii në kine, è se saŋlee, è me lem ge é nêy fo abkaa se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They were delighted and agreed to give him some money.

Oku_Text: Judas me beeme, è se kerne jii ye lòó ab din Jesus é koo ne ghele ke wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He consented, and watched for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them when no crowd was present.

Oku_Text: Kechii ke é seen wán keyak e ten e ǝbliy eyyir keban ke too ghàà feshik, è kan ki ney kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.

Oku_Text: Jesus me tum Bita ghene Joon, è suuy se ghene ge: Ghen ndu è siyse abfua-ǝbyíéne eyyie Kechii Eyboyse, é ghesen nềy yie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, 'Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me bif se wen ge: Kelik ke wè konen ge ghes fel abfua-ǝbyíéne në vin e ten lu ghááy?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Where do you want us to prepare for it?' they asked.

Oku_Text: ǝb suuy se ghene ge: Yen-al Ghene loo lii e ntek, é ghene ghe wel konen ne ǝb beɛy nton a moo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He replied, 'As you enter the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you.

Oku_Text: Ghen ndu e wen eybam è lii e ndaa ye ǝb lii e ten,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Follow him to the house that he enters,'

Oku_Text: è suuy se ba a ndaa fo ge: Ndinte bife ge eybee ndaa na ghel eyghane ye ghene ghe ghon-eyfel nə yii nêy yis Kechii Eyboyse e ten se yaay-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'and say to the owner of the house, "The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?"'

Oku_Text: ǝb me din kelik se ghen ne ke ghake ge cybee ndaaye eyyio ne é loote siyse, é ghen me fel ǝbfua-ǝbyiéne ten fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'He will show you a large room upstairs, all furnished. Make preparations there.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me lòò è ndu e ntek fo, è yen əbnəə əbjim ne ǝb lu ken ka Jesus kè suuyen, è fel ǝbfua-abyíćne eyyie Kechii Eyboyse fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They left and found things just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover.

Oku_Text: əbliye ney kwen, Jesus ghene ghe ghe ntum na wene me diom kelik kemok se ye yie,
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the hour came, Jesus and his apostles reclined at the table.

Oku_Text: ǝb suuy se ghene ge: Me kè lô se loote ki shii ge ghesen yie Kechii Eyboyse kin në me geese yene ngek.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he said to them, 'I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.

Oku_Text: Me suuye se ghen ge: Me jia bo yi yie ǝbfua-ǝbyićne në vin se chin ka kenǝǝ ke kum ǝbghene ney kwen ɛəbliy we Feyine saken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it finds fulfillment in the kingdom of God.'

Oku_Text: ǝb me lie əbbom əmnduk, è bèèlè Feyin è suuy ge: Ghen ko è giate è kal è konen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After taking the cup, he gave thanks and said, 'Take this and divide it among you.

Oku_Text: Ghen ye ke ge me jia bo yî nəə əmnduk min se chin ka eysak a Feyine yî gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For I tell you I will not drink again from the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.'

Oku_Text: ǝb lie keban, è bèèlè Feyin è bekte è fo se ghene, è suuy ge: Kine lu əbghun ə wom we me fő bek ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, 'This is my body given for you;'

Oku_Text: Ghene yese, ghen ye neeye & jii ghan se ye kuume me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'do this in remembrance of me.'

Oku_Text: Ghene meyse eyyie, ab lie abbom amnduk, è jel ken so e jii në ghan è suuye ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the same way, after the supper he took the cup, saying,

Oku_Text: Əmnduk min lu kelek kefse ke kum əmndurŋ mə mom me am lejen bek ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.'

Oku_Text: Dî mba, ne wel we ǝb nêy din me é koo lu fen, yie ghese wen kel symok.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But the hand of him who is going to betray me is with mine on the table.'

Oku_Text: Wanwele nây kuo ka Feyine nè kakenmen, di ne lòn lu se wel we əb nëy din wen é koo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'The Son of Man will go as it has been decreed. But woe to that man who betrays him!'

Oku_Text: Ghene me keɛte è se kante se ghene ngense ge se lu nde e ghene mam we ab nży mom kenǝǝ në kin-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They began to question among themselves which of them it might be who would do this.

Oku_Text: Ghene me gese kèètè se ye bek ge se lu nde e ghene mam we ab chian ghel ɛjim-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: A dispute also arose among them as to which of them was considered to be greatest.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Tefon abtume ki abtaawe se ye sake ghele ghen, nè ghel ghe é saken ghel kwante ghele tell ge é ye tonte ghene ge ngiamtense.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to them, 'The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those who exercise authority over them call themselves Benefactors.

Oku_Text: Dì ng ke báà se lu se ye lu di se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But you are not to be like that.

Oku_Text: Wel we ab chian ghel ejim e ghene məm lu se ye lu ka wán ǝbtele, nè wel e mbiy lu se ye fel Ka wände.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Instead, the greatest among you should be like the youngest, and the one who rules like the one who serves.

Oku_Text: Wel we luun ketuu lu nde? Wel we ab diom, é gwiy nè ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne se wen moo wel we ab gwiyen so-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: For who is greater, the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who is at the table?'

Oku_Text: Le báà se lu wel we ǝb diom, é gwiy nè ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne se wen-a? Dî ne me fele e ghene məm Ka wánde.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But I am among you as one who serves.'

Oku_Text: Ghen lu ghel ghe é kulen eytem, è gok ghesen e me eymomse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You are those who have stood by me in my trials.'

Oku_Text: Me me se foo ǝbtaawe eysak se ghen ken ka Ba ǝ wom nè foon se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'And I confer on you a kingdom, just as my Father conferred one on me,'

Oku_Text: Με yî loo sak ǝbfon ə wom, é ghesen ye yié gese nǝǝ kelik kemok, é ghen kok kabase, è ye sake tekfəə ghele Islael te njio-sebaa eyvəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.'

Oku_Text: Jesus ney lòò è mbaate Bita ge: Simon, Simon, Satan lonmen jii se fefte ghen ka wel fefte esan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift all of you as wheat.

Oku_Text: Di ne me lu ne me jem se wè Simon ge Feyine nɛɛy é wè teem ketaan ghene wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail.'

Oku_Text: É nêy loo lu e ǝbliy we wè kasen è fiile se me é wè giamten ghone-no vie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.'

Oku_Text: Bita fise ge: Báàba me lu kebaaten se lii e nchak ghesoo, se gese se kuo ghesoo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he replied, 'Lord, I am ready to go with you to prison and to death.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Bita, me suuy se wè ge ken nè ntǝke lan lumngvəə nêy ye ghan ge ǝb ton ne wè maamen me kan etal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'I tell you, Peter, before the rooster crows today, you will deny three times that you know me.'

Oku_Text: ǝb me gese suuy se ghene ge: Nè lu ǝbliy we me nè tumen ghen ghàà ǝbkaa, ghàà kebam, ghàà guose evǝǝ, ghen nè lô tan kefaa? Ghene fise ge: Aay, wel nè báà tan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus asked them, 'When I sent you without purse, bag or sandals, did you lack anything?' 'Nothing,' they answered.

Oku_Text: Əb suuy se ghene ge: Ké leenene lu ne wel ki ǝbkaa əb lis, wele ye ki kebam ab lie, wele jia ye ki fiak, ǝb feene ndǝyse kenton nə wen è yun fiak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, 'But now if you have a purse, take it, and also a bag; and if you don't have a sword, sell your cloak and buy one.

Oku_Text: Me suuy se ghen ge: Kenǝǝ ke é nè nyakenmen & waale-Feyin kum me lu se kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It is written: 'And he was numbered with the transgressors'; and I tell you that this must be fulfilled in me.

Oku_Text: É nè nyak ge: É nè chinten wen ghene ghele mbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yes, what is written about me is reaching its fulfillment.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me suuy ge: Yen-a Báàba, fiakse shi fen sebaa. Jesus fise ge: Ke kwen di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The disciples said, 'See, Lord, here are two swords.' 'That's enough,' he replied.

Oku_Text: Jesus me lòò è kok ɛ kfən Olif ghaa ǝb nè lóó ǝb ye kok; ghon-eyfel na wen kok ghene ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and his disciples followed him.

Oku_Text: Ghene kok è chin fo e leghe ǝb suuy se ghene ge: Ghen ye jem fe eymomse necy é wel fey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On reaching the place, he said to them, 'Pray that you will not fall into temptation.'

Oku_Text: Əb suuy di, è me vase ghene è chia è ndute se tylaay, è təm eləy, è se jeme ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: He withdrew about a stone's throw beyond them, knelt down and prayed,

Oku_Text: Ba a wom, fii abbome ngɛk vin se me, e lefe wè korjen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me;'

Oku_Text: Di ne lòó wè neey ken ka wè kejen, ghàà ngɛy ne lu ka me kernen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'yet not my will, but yours be done.'

Oku_Text: Nchiy-ndaa-Feyin me sǝǝke eyyio, è nexy ǝb se ki ǝbtaawe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: An angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.

Oku_Text: Əb nè yio cylum se narŋ, è me se jeme naa ketaan ne kezǝksen a wene chemke se ntie ka əbmal əmndurŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And being in anguish, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground.

Oku_Text: Əb meyse zyjem, è kase ndu ghaa ghon-eyfel nə wene kè luun, è yên nɛ ghene buune buunen bek nkwaate se nane.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he rose from prayer and went back to the disciples, he found them asleep, exhausted from sorrow.

Oku_Text: Əb me bif se ghene ge: Lu bek ghe we ghene buunen? Ghen loole, è ye jem fe eymomse negy é wel fey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Why are you sleeping?' he asked them. 'Get up and pray so that you will not fall into temptation.'

Oku_Text: Ka Jesus sunnen di e leghe kenone ghele fay è gwiy fo, ne wel ǝblee e mame ghé ntume ghe njio-sebaa eyvəm we é nè tonten ge Judas dine jii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While he was still speaking a crowd came up, and the man who was called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them.

Oku_Text: Əb kokte gwiy e Jesus cybee è baamé wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He approached Jesus to kiss him,

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: Judas, wè baamé Wanwel ka wè chakte chakten se din ge é ko-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: but Jesus asked him, 'Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?'

Oku_Text: Ghon-eyfel nə Jesus keele kenǝǝ ke ke loo è gan, è me bif ge: Báàba, ghes gwete nè fiakse-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus' followers saw what was going to happen, they said, 'Lord, should we strike with our swords?'

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee e ghene mam me gwel kekos se ketuu se ghé ntaŋlee è kef ketuule ke ǝbkoy ǝbtaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.

Oku_Text: Jesus key ge: Ke kwen di! È me kum ketuule wel vi, ke seese abghun se fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Jesus answered, 'No more of this!' And he touched the man's ear and healed him.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghele mbiy se ghé ntarŋlee, nè ghel efélene ghe ndaa-eykokse Feyine gese nè chee se se kè gwiyen se koo wen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders, who had come for him,

Oku_Text: Ghen gwiy nè fiakse gese nè ǝbbule ge é koo me ne me lu abchon-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Am I leading a rebellion, that you have come with swords and clubs?'

Oku_Text: Ghesen nè lóó é ye lu e ndaa-cykokse Feyine ǝbchii əbjim ne ghen koo wiy me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Every day I was with you in the temple courts, and you did not lay a hand on me.'

Oku_Text: Dî ne ǝbliy ə wene kwenmen, ne lu e kediak ne mbi nfiine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But this is your hour—when darkness reigns.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me koo Jesus è loo ndu nè wen se bey nə ketuu se ghé ntarjlee, Bita se nyiake gwiye ke wene naa əbtəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then seizing him, they led him away and took him into the house of the high priest. Peter followed at a distance.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka é nè kwaayenmen əbvəs e eyfume ntetembey fo, Bita me gwiy è diom ghene ghele è se yósê.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And when some there had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter sat down with them.

Oku_Text: Wánde abvii ǝblee we ǝb në félen e bey fo yen ka Bita diom e ǝbghun ǝbvəs, è loote kiy, è suuy ge: Wel vin nè lu sey ghene wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A servant girl saw him sitting there in the firelight. She looked closely at him and said, 'This man was with him.'

Oku_Text: Bita maa, è suuy ge: Əbvii, me báà se ke wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he denied it. 'Woman, I don't know him,' he said.

Oku_Text: Se moote ken le wel ǝblee gese yen Bita è suuy se wen ge: Wè lu sey wele ngoo na Jesus. Bita fise ge: Əbluumen, me wiy wel ǝblee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A little later someone else saw him and said, 'You also are one of them.' 'Man, I am not!' Peter replied.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka keleŋ əbliy nè chian, wel ablee gese suuy ketaan ge: Ke loote lu se abluuy ge wel vin lu wele ngoo na Jesus bek ge ǝb lu wele wê Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: About an hour later another asserted, 'Certainly this fellow was with him, for he is a Galilean.'

Oku_Text: Di Bita fise ge: Əbluumen, me báà se ke kenǝǝ ke we toylen kin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Peter replied, 'Man, I don't know what you're talking about!'

Oku_Text: Lumngvəə me toŋ ken se ǝbliy ne ab bo suŋnene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just as he was speaking, the rooster crowed.

Oku_Text: Báàba benle è soo eyshie & Bita abghun, əb me kuume kenǝǝ ke ǝb kè suuyen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word the Lord had spoken to him:

Oku_Text: Ken πε ntəke lan, lumngvəə nêy ye ghan ge ǝb ton në wê maamen me karn etal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Before the rooster crows today, you will disown me three times.'

Oku_Text: Əb kuume di, è fǝy ndu jii bey è kèsèn nẻè eydiye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he went outside and wept bitterly.

Oku_Text: Ghel ghe é nè chiyen Jesus ghi me se buu Jesus gese tele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The men who were guarding Jesus began mocking and beating him.

Oku_Text: Ghene kul eyshie a wen, è tel wen, è suuy ge: Chio ge se tel nde wè-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They blindfolded him and demanded, 'Prophesy! Who hit you?'

Oku_Text: Ghene nè lɵ̂ gese suuy əbnəə əbyafene se wen naa se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they said many other insulting things to him.

Oku_Text: Əbtəke bvəs è yio, chee se eylak foo, nè ghele mbiy se ghé ntaŋlee gese nè ghel ghe é nè dinten tesak konen, è lie Jesus è ndu so se ghene eytiye nsak è suuy ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: At daybreak the council of the elders of the people, both the chief priests and the teachers of the law, met together, and Jesus was led before them.

Oku_Text: L0o lu ne lu wè wel we Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen, wè suuy se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'If you are the Messiah,' they said, 'tell us.'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Me suuy se ghen é ghen jia beeme kenəə ke me suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'If I tell you, you will not believe me,'

Oku_Text: Në me bif eybif se ghen é ghen jia fise.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'and if I asked you, you would not answer.'

Oku_Text: Dî ne se kèètè e leenen lu ne Wanwele nây diom e ǝbkoy ǝbtaa nə Feyine, We Ba-Əbtaawe-Əbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the mighty God.'

Oku_Text: Ghene ghel ejim bif ge: Ke dine ge wè lu wán Feyin-a? Əb fise ge: Ke lu ka ghen suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They all asked, 'Are you then the Son of God?' He replied, 'You say that I am.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me suuy ge: Ghesen bo shii wiy wel Eyshir. Ghesen yiomen kenǝǝ ke ke fǝyen & wen ǝbchio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they said, 'Why do we need any more testimony? We have heard it from his own lips.'

Oku_Text: Ghene lòò naa ghel abjim è ndu nè Jesus se Bailet,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the whole assembly rose and led him off to Pilate.

Oku_Text: è me kɛɛte se ye toy əbnəə ɛ wen abghun suuye ge: Ghes nè yen bie vin ne ǝb fəmse ghele ghesen suuye ge ghàà ghene ye lake shile se ǝbfon ə Lûm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they began to accuse him, saying, 'We have found this man subverting our nation. He opposes payment of taxes to Caesar'

Oku_Text: Əb nè suuye sey ge yi lu Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen ne lu abfon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and claims to be Messiah, a king.'

Oku_Text: Bailet bif se wen ge: Lu wề abfon a ghele Juus-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: So Pilate asked Jesus, 'Are you the king of the Jews?'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Ke lu ka wè suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You have said so,' Jesus replied.

Oku_Text: Bailet suuy se ghele mbiy se ghé ntarŋlee ghene kenone ghel kii ge: Me báà yen kejas a wel vin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Pilate announced to the chief priests and the crowd, 'I find no basis for a charge against this man.'

Oku_Text: Dî ghene soote se Bailet ketaan ge: Əb ghane neeye ge ghele ye taa ketuu, dinte əbnəə wen në vi eylake Judea cyjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they insisted, 'He stirs up the people all over Judea by his teaching.

Oku_Text: Əb nè kɛɛte e Galilee, se lu ne ǝb gwiymen è chin so se fen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He started in Galilee and has come all the way here.'

Oku_Text: Bailet yio di e leghe è bif ge: Wel vin chèmè lu wele Galilee-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: On hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.

Oku_Text: Se lu ka ab keelen ge Jesus lòò e kelik ne ke lu e eyshaa eysak nə Helod, ǝb me suuy ge é lie wen è ndu so se Helod.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he learned that Jesus was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod,

Oku_Text: Nè lu əbliy fo ne Helod lu & Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: who was also in Jerusalem at that time.

Oku_Text: Helod yen Jesus, è se saŋle naa se naj bek feteete fe ǝb nè yionmen kum wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Herod saw Jesus, he was greatly pleased, because for a long time he had been wanting to see him. From what he had heard about him,

Oku_Text: Əb nè lo se ki shii se ǝbtəm se yene wen è me se kwaate ge ab neey əbnəə əbchio Luk ǝbyűméné é yi yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: he hoped to see him perform some sign.

Oku_Text: ǝb me se bifte əbnəə se Jesus naa se eydio, ab jia ben.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He plied him with many questions, but Jesus gave him no answer.

Oku_Text: Ghele mbiy se ghé ntaŋlee ghene ghel ghe é nè dinten tesak ə Moses nè lô se tiy fo suuye ketaan ge Jesus lu ne ǝb jas.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The chief priests and the teachers of the law were standing there, vehemently accusing him.

Oku_Text: Helod ghene ghel eyghon na wene se foo ngek se wen e jii cybee, buu wen, è lafse wen në ndǝy sejuje, è kase tinse wen se Bailet.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Herod and his soldiers ridiculed and mocked him. Dressing him in an elegant robe, they sent him back to Pilate.

Oku_Text: Se kèètè kechii fo, Helod ghene Bailet me kase se lu sunse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: That day Herod and Pilate became friends—

Oku_Text: Ghene nè báà se fǝye fo se mbiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: before this they had been enemies.

Oku_Text: Bailet me jante ghele mbiy se ghé ntaŋlee, nè cheese, gese nè ghel ejime,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers and the people,

Oku_Text: è suuy se ghene ge: Ghen kè gwiy nè wel vin se me è suuy ge ǝb nezye ne ghele taa ketuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and said to them, 'You brought this man to me as one who was inciting the people to rebellion.

Oku_Text: Me liimen e wen a kenəə e ghen eyshie è jia yen kejas ǝ wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have examined him in your presence and have found no basis for your charges against him.

Oku_Text: Helod kè báà yen sey kejas a wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Neither has Herod, for he sent him back to us;'

Oku_Text: Ghan kenǝǝ ke ke kè nɛɛyen ǝb kase fise wen se ghesen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'as you can see, he has done nothing to deserve death.'

Oku_Text: Əb báà ngɛy kenǝǝ ke lòó é yúy wen se ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Therefore, I will punish him and then release him.'

Oku_Text: Ghel ghi me chintèn ejim è waame ge: Meyse wen meysen, è fo Balabbas se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the whole crowd shouted, 'Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us!'

Oku_Text: Balabbas lu wel ne ǝb nè ndu nchak bek ge ǝb nè lu e mǝme kenon ke ke nè losen ǝbchio ge é bak eysak, gese në ka ab nè yuyen wel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (Barabbas had been thrown into prison for an insurrection in the city, and for murder.)

Oku_Text: Bailet me gese sunnen se kenone ghel kii bek ge əb nè ki shii se vase Jesus,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Wanting to release Jesus, Pilate appealed to them again.

Oku_Text: Ka ǝb sunnen dî ghene sey waame ge: Bante wen se kentam, bante wen se kentam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they kept shouting, 'Crucify him! Crucify him!'

Oku_Text: An etal bife ge: Kejas a wel vin lu ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the third time he spoke to them: 'Why? What crime has this man committed?

Oku_Text: Mẹ báà yen kenəə kə əb jasen ke lòó é yúy wen se ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have found in him no grounds for the death penalty.

Oku_Text: Me suuy é nəkse wen è vase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore I will have him punished and then release him.'

Oku_Text: Ghel ghii waame ken ketaan ketaan se Bailet ge: É lu se bante Jesus se kentam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But with loud shouts they insistently demanded that he be crucified,

Oku_Text: Ke ghene me fel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and their shouts prevailed.

Oku_Text: Bailet me chiase eysak ge é yúy Jesus ka ghene nè kerjen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So Pilate decided to grant their demand.

Oku_Text: Əb nè fǝyse wel we ǝb nè nduun nchak ne ab nè ndu bek ka ǝb nè luun & mǝme kenon ke ke nè losen abchio ɛ ntek ge é bak eysak, gese ka ǝb nè yuyen wel, è me fo Jesus ge é yúy ka ghene nè kerjen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He released the man who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, the one they asked for, and surrendered Jesus to their will.

Oku_Text: Ghel eyghone me lie Jesus ǝbliy nê fo, è se nduu se bante, è loo ndu, ghene ghe wele wê Silen ǝblee konen ne é torte ge Simon, ne əb kfəəy əbkwak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As the soldiers led him away, they seized Simon from Cyrene, who was on his way in from the country,

Oku_Text: Ghene me koo wen è kan ǝb bɛɛy kentam a Jesus è se jumte nduu nè wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.

Oku_Text: Ghele nè lô se jumte nduu nè Jesus naa se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A large number of people followed him,

Oku_Text: Əbkiy elee nè lu & kenon fo tornnene gese koosen cysen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: including women who mourned and wailed for him.

Oku_Text: Jesus benle è suuy se ghene ge: Əbkiy ghe Jolesaleme, ghàà ghen yɛ diy me, ghen yɛ diy ngen a sen gese diye ghon a ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus turned and said to them, 'Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me; weep for yourselves and for your children.

Oku_Text: Bek ge ǝbliye gwiye we ghele nêy ye suuy ge: Jofe ke ǝbkiy ghe luun ǝbkan, nè ghe é nè báà bii wán gese nề ghe é nè báà yi nyonse wán.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the time will come when you will say, "Blessed are the childless women, the wombs that never bore and the breasts that never nursed!"

Oku_Text: nêy loo lu əbliy fo é ghele ye suuy se tetane ge: Ghen feyke e ghes ǝbtuu, suuye se kfǝnse ge: Ghen chite ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Then "they will say to the mountains, 'Fall on us!' and to the hills, 'Cover us!'"'

Oku_Text: Ghele le mome əbnəə ka vine ne fekake bery fezǝǝ, é ke nɛy ye lu leey ǝbliy we fe yumenmen?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'For if people do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?'

Oku_Text: É se nduu nè Jesus di, ne é lie ghel elee ebaa, ne lu ghel ne é nè fey eysak, ge é ndu è yuy ghene ghe Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Two other men, both criminals, were also led out with him to be executed.

Oku_Text: Ghene ney ndu è chin e kelik ne é tonte ge: Kevəfə ketuu, è me bante Jesus se kentam, gese nè ghel ghĩi ghe é nè feyen eysak, ǝblee & Jesus ə eybee ǝbkoy ǝbtaa, nè ǝblee cybee ǝbkoy ǝbkiye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they came to the place called the Skull, they crucified him there, along with the criminals—one on his right, the other on his left.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney loo è suuy ge: Ba a wom, leyse kejas a ghen bek ge ghene báà se ke kenǝǝ ke ghene nɛɛyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said, 'Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.'

Oku_Text: Ghel ghii mak kan se keele jii ye ghene giate ndəyse Jesus e ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they divided up his clothes by casting lots.

Oku_Text: Kenone ghele se tiy fo kiye, ne cheese buu wen suuye ge: Əb nè ghane boyse ghel, ab boyse yi ngerŋ ə wen e lefe ǝb luun Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen, ka ab nè choken!
English_NIV_Equivalent: The people stood watching, and the rulers even sneered at him. They said, 'He saved others; let him save himself if he is God’s Messiah, the Chosen One.'

Oku_Text: Ghel eyghone se buu sey wen, è kokte è fo amnduk ne əm ngweymen se wen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: The soldiers also came up and mocked him. They offered him wine vinegar

Oku_Text: suuye ge: Boyse ngen yie e lefe wè luun abfon a ghele Juus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and said, 'If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself.'

Oku_Text: me nyak waale è base se Jesus a ketuu kentam ne zy suuy ge: Vin lu ǝbfon ə ghele Juus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There was a written notice above him, which read: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee se mǝme ghel ghe é nè feyen eysak, é bante ghene ghe Jesus, séy me se teyle wen suuye ge: Wề le báà se lu Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: One of the criminals who hung there hurled insults at him: 'Aren't you the Messiah?'

Oku_Text: Boyse ngeŋ ə yie, è boyse sey ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Save yourself and us!'

Oku_Text: Wele vidi key wen è bif ge: Wè báà se fane Feyin-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the other criminal rebuked him. 'Don't you fear God,' he said,

Oku_Text: Ghesen nè ghe wel vin kuo eykuo gymok.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'since you are under the same sentence?'

Oku_Text: Ke lu & titiy ge ghesoo kuo, bek ge ghesoo kuo keghoo kesoo, ne wel vin kuo ke wene se lólò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'We are punished justly, for we are getting what our deeds deserve. But this man has done nothing wrong.'

Oku_Text: Əb suuy di è me suuy ge: Jesus, nây kuume me ǝbliy we wè se saken abfon a vie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he said, 'Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Με suuye kenǝǝ ke ǝbluuy se wè ge ghesoo nêy ye lu e kelik eyzəəte lan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered him, 'Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.'

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ka chuy tiysen, mbi sasen è fin naa eyjim, è ney kase se laane ne chuy se make mbàŋse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour,

Oku_Text: Chuy liime di, ndǝy se é nè kerjen cybuu so e ndaa-eykokse Feyin ney loo è basen ǝbwaŋ əbbua.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for the sun stopped shining. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney lòò è ton e gie eytaa ge: Ba ǝ wom, me vii keyioy kom ne lu e wè keghoo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus called out with a loud voice, 'Father, into your hands I commit my spirit.'

Oku_Text: Əb suuy di è me mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he had said this, he breathed his last.

Oku_Text: Ketuu eyghon ke ke nè tiyen fo yene əbnəə we ǝb ganen, nè lô fo eykokse se Feyine suuye ge: Ke lu se ǝbluuy ge wel vin nè lu wele titiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The centurion, seeing what had happened, praised God and said, 'Surely this was a righteous man.'

Oku_Text: Ngose wel ye ey nè luun fo kiye, yɛn əbnəə we ǝb ganen, è vii amwem è se koosen eysen kfǝǝye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When all the people who had gathered to witness this sight saw what took place, they beat their breasts and went away.

Oku_Text: Əbnəə vin nè gane di, ne ghel ejim ghe é nè looten keen Jesus gese nè abkiy ghe é nè lòòn & wen cybam e Galilee nè lu fo ne é teem ke ghene ǝbtəm è se kiye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But all those who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee nè lu ne é toņte Lak ge Josef, ne lu wel e wê nteke ghele Juus ye Alimatea.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there was a man named Joseph, a member of the Council, a good and upright man, from the Judean town of Arimathea.

Oku_Text: Əb nè lu wel abjun, neeye ken ka Feyine kerjen, ne ǝb nè gese chiyte əbliy we Feyine nêy ye sak ǝbfon ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He had not consented to their decision and action. He was waiting for the kingdom of God.

Oku_Text: Chèmè lu ka ǝb nè luun wel ǝblee e ndaa nsak nə ghele Juuse, ab nè báà vii keghoo ə wen se əbnəə we é nè diom è lem, nè we é nè neeyen se yuye Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Going to Pilate, he asked for Jesus’ body.

Oku_Text: Əb ndu è yen Bailet è lon ge ǝb fo rygvən ə Jesus se yi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he took it down, wrapped it in linen cloth

Oku_Text: əb lon di, é beeme, ab me ndu è səkse eygvən ə Jesus yi, è bate e ndəy sefee, è ndu nyinse se eysey eylee ne é chuf & eyghok, nè é nè beɛy ghàà gvəəme wel e ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and placed it in a tomb cut in the rock, one in which no one had yet been laid.

Oku_Text: Kechii nê ki nè foo se kechii cybame, ne ke se meye, nè ǝbliy cybame se shi se keete.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.

Oku_Text: Əbkiy ghii ghe ghene ghe Jesus nè lòòn & Galilee, ndu e Josef eybam è yen eysey, è gese yen ka é gvǝǝmen Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and saw the tomb and how his body was laid.

Oku_Text: Ghene kfǝǝy, è siyse ǝbyiósén eygvən, ne ǝb lu e tenkii tenkii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they went home and prepared spices and perfumes.

Oku_Text: Kechii eybame ney kwen, ghene yese se zəèlè ka cysake nè dinen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they rested on the Sabbath in obedience to the commandment.

Oku_Text: Se lu e kechii ke mbiye se ǝbwey, ǝbkiy ghii ndu nè ǝbyióséne ngvǝǝme we ghene nè siysen vi se eysey naa e bvase bvase.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb.

Oku_Text: Ghene chin fo è yen ne é binsemen rytiy, ey lòò se ǝbchio cysry fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,

Oku_Text: Ghene lii se rysey fey è séy jia yen rygvən a Báàba Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

Oku_Text: Kenǝǝ tene loo foo ngek se ghene, e leghe ghel ebaa teem è gwiy e ghene eybee në ndǝyse ghene njante ka ryghuu zyliakene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them.

Oku_Text: Əbkiy ghii fan è ngvəkte è tam tekek a ghen se ntie, ghel ghii suuy se ghene ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them,

Oku_Text: Lu bek ghe we ghene kiyen wel ne ab lu abtaa e mame ghel ɛkuone.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Why do you look for the living among the dead?

Oku_Text: ǝb báà se lu fen, ab loolemen se zykuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He is not here; he has risen!

Oku_Text: Ghen kuume kenǝǝ ke ǝb në suuyen se ghen ne ghene ghene bery & Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Remember how he told you, while he was still with you in Galilee:'

Oku_Text: Əb nè suuy ge: É lu se naa se vii Wanwel se eghoo na ghele mbeese, é bante wen se kentam, ǝbchii lun abtal, əb kase loole se zykuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: '"The Son of Man must be delivered over to the hands of sinners, be crucified and on the third day be raised again."

Oku_Text: Ghene me kuume kenǝǝ ke Jesus nè suuyenmen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they remembered his words.

Oku_Text: è nay lòò se bene rysey fo, è fiile è ndu è fiel əbnəə ǝbjim we ǝb kè ganen se ghon-eyfel na Jesus ghe njio zymok eyvəm gese në se ghel elee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they came back from the tomb, they told all these things to the Eleven and to all the rest.

Oku_Text: Əbkiy nê ghin nè lu Mrele Magdalene, nè Joanna gese nè Merle we no a Jems, gese nè ǝbkiy elee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the others with them who told this to the apostles.

Oku_Text: Ghene nè gwiy è suuy əbnəə në vin se ghé ntume.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they did not believe the women, because their words seemed to them like nonsense.

Oku_Text: Bita me tum è loole, è nyin è ndu se ǝbchio rysey, è ngvǝkte è yen ne lu ken nday se é nè limten rygvən ə Jesus so ka se nyinen, ê lòò, è se kfǝǝye kante ge se kè gan ghe-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. Bending over, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.

Oku_Text: Ko se lu ken e kechii fo, ghon-cyfel na Jesus elee ebaa lòὸ ε Jolesalem è se nduu e ntek ne é tonte ge Emmaus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Əbtame ntek yin se Jolesalem nè lu ka tetan saamba dì.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.

Oku_Text: Ghene loo ndu è se fiele əbnəə në vin əbjim we ǝb ganen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: As they talked and discussed these things with each other,

Oku_Text: Ghene se fiele kante di e leghe, Jesus se wen a ngen baksen è gwiy nè ghene, ghene ghene se nduu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus himself came up and walked along with them;

Oku_Text: Dî ne kefaa bukse eshie ghen, ghene jia keele ge kè lu wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but they were kept from recognizing him.

Oku_Text: ǝb bif se ghene ge: Lu ghe ke ghene jélen, fielen, gheynen le? Ghene me teem fo rysen eysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He asked them, 'What are you discussing together as you walk along?' They stood still, their faces downcast.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee e ghene mam ne é tonte ge Kleobas fise se wen ge: Lu ken wè wel eyghan & Jolesalem we ǝb báà se ke əbnəə we ǝb ganen e abchii fen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: One of them, named Cleopas, asked him, 'Are you the only one visiting Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in these days?'

Oku_Text: əb bif ge: Əbnəə əbke? Ghene me se suuye se wen ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'What things?' he asked. 'About Jesus of Nazareth,' they replied.

Oku_Text: Lu əbnəə we kum Jesus wê Nazalet, we nè luun ntum Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'He was a prophet, powerful in word and deed before God and all the people.

Oku_Text: Əb nè nɛɛye əbnəə ǝbchio ǝbyúméné, fekte ǝbfektene, ne Feyine bente wen, ne ghele bente sêy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The chief priests and our rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him;'

Oku_Text: Ghele mbiy se ghé ntanlee gese nè ghel ghe é saken ghes nè lô fo wen ge é chiase eysak ge é yúy, é me bante se kentam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'but we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel.

Oku_Text: Ghesen bo shii wiy wel Eyshir. Ghesen yiomen kenǝǝ ke ke fǝyen & wen ǝbchio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And what is more, it is the third day since all this took place.'

Oku_Text: Se baksen se keghene, ǝbkiy elee & ghes a ngoo kè suuy kenǝǝ əbchio ǝbyúméné se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'In addition, some of our women amazed us. They went to the tomb early this morning'

Oku_Text: Ghene kè ndu ǝbchio eysey e bvase bvəse lan, è jia yen eygvən a wen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'but didn't find his body. They came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.'

Oku_Text: è kase fiile è gwiy è se suuye ge ghene kè lô yen nchiyse-ndaa nə Feyine ey suuy ge ǝb bo bezy ǝbtaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but they did not see Jesus.'

Oku_Text: Ghele ngoo nə ghes elee ndu e bene cysty fo, è yen ne zy lu ken ka ǝbkiy ghii kè suuyen dì, è jia yen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, 'How foolish you are, and how slow to believe all that the prophets have spoken!'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Ghene ngoole ghe le.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Did not the Messiah have to suffer these things and then enter his glory?'

Oku_Text: Ke taa se ghen se beeme əbnəə əbjim we ghele ntum nə Feyine nè suuyen bek ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.

Oku_Text: Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen le nè báà se lu se yene ngɛk shin se gee se ye yio ǝbjun abghaake na wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: As they approached the village to which they were going, Jesus continued on as if he were going farther.

Oku_Text: Jesus me jemte əbnəə əbjim we é nè nyaken kum ngerŋ ə wen & ŋwaale-Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But they urged him strongly, 'Stay with us, for it is nearly evening; the day is almost over.' So he went in to stay with them.

Oku_Text: Əb nè kɛɛte nè rŋwaale se eynyake Moses è me diarŋten è ndu nè sé ghele ntum nə Feyine naa ɛjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, gave thanks, broke it and began to give it to them.

Oku_Text: Ghene ghene ndu è loo beste e ntɛk ye ghene nè nduun e ten Jesus se nɛɛye ka ǝb chia chian.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, and he disappeared from their sight.

Oku_Text: Ghene bente wen ge: Kiy wè, ghesen ko ndu è chii, bek ka shiin e likfǝǝy, ne əbtəke se jiɛ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They asked each other, 'Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?'

Oku_Text: Əb me ndu ge ghene ghene chii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They got up and returned at once to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, assembled together

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ka ǝb nè diom se ye yíé ghene ghene, ǝb liɛ keban, è bèèlè Feyin è bɛkte, è fo se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and saying, 'It is true! The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon.'

Oku_Text: Eshiɛ a ghene me laane, ghene se keele ge kè lu Jesus, & leghe ǝb me liime è ley,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the two told what had happened on the way, and how Jesus was recognized by them when he broke the bread.

Oku_Text: Ghene se kante ge: Tetem a teso le kè báà se fuo ka é kwaay ǝbvəs e ten e ǝbliy we ǝb kè sunnen se ghesoo e jii-a, gese dinte kenǝǝ ke waale-Feyine suuyen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, 'Peace be with you.'

Oku_Text: Ghene kante dì, è me loole ken se ǝbliy è kase fiile se Jolesalem, è yen ghon-eyfel ghe njio zymok eyvəm bene fe ghene ɛlee nè kaamen kelik kemok,
English_NIV_Equivalent: They were startled and frightened, thinking they saw a ghost.

Oku_Text: è se suuye ge: Ke lu se ǝbluuy nɛ Báàba loolemen se zykuo. Simon kè lô yen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, 'Why are you troubled, and why do doubts rise in your minds?

Oku_Text: Ghon-eyfel ghe ebaa ghii me fiel se ghene əbnəə we ǝb kè ganen se ghene & jii, gese nè ka ghene kè keelen Jesus ɛ ǝbliy we ǝb kè bekten keban.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Look at my hands and my feet. It is I myself! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have.'

Oku_Text: Ghene se fiele di e leghe, Jesus me sasen, è fay e ghene məm è suuy se ghene ge: Nyian ye lu se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and feet.

Oku_Text: Ghene se fane naa se narŋ, chine gese kwaate ge ghene yen ne lu nkfəsay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And while they still did not believe it because of joy and amazement, he asked them, 'Do you have anything here to eat?'

Oku_Text: Əb bif se ghene ge: Foo ghe ngɛk se ghen? Ghene maanen mɛ bek ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,

Oku_Text: Ghen kiy eghoo ǝ ghom gese nè evəə ə ghome, é ghen yen ge lu me nè ngeŋ ə yom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and he took it and ate it in their presence.

Oku_Text: Ghen momte ǝbghun ə wom, é ghen keele bek ge nkfəsay see jia ki njia ǝbghun nè ǝbvəfe ka ghen yenen ne me ki le.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said to them, 'This is what I told you while I was still with you:

Oku_Text: Əb suuy di, è din eghoo wen gese nè evǝǝ wene se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms.'

Oku_Text: Eysaŋlee nè lô lun se ghene tetem, ǝbchio yum ghene e jii ye ghene nè báà se ke jii yɛ lòó é beeme.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.

Oku_Text: Jesus bif se ghene ge: Ghen le ki kefaa keyíéne fen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: He told them, 'This is what is written: The Messiah will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,'

Oku_Text: lie kentime sǝǝ eynyoken è fo se wen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'and repentance for the forgiveness of sins will be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.'

Oku_Text: Əb fii è kfəl, ghene yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You are witnesses of these things.'

Oku_Text: Əb me suuy se ghene ge: Əbnəə vin we ǝb kooyen lu we me nè suuyen se ghen e ǝbliy we me nè bɛɛyen ghesen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I am going to send you what my Father has promised;'

Oku_Text: Me nè suuye ge: əbnəə əbjim we é nè nyaken kum me e rŋwaalese na Moses, nè we nè nyaken ghele ntum nə Feyine gese nè we é nè nyaken e ŋwaale Salm kum me lu se kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'but stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.'

Oku_Text: ǝb surjnen di, è me yem eytof a ghen ka ghene keele ke ke luun e ŋwaale-Feyin,
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he had led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, he lifted up his hands and blessed them.

Oku_Text: è me suuy se ghene ge: É nyak e nwaale-Feyin ge: Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen lu se naa se yene ngek se kuo, é ǝbchii chin əbtal əb loole se eykuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up into heaven.

Oku_Text: Ke gese se lu ge, é lu se ney se kèètè & Jolesalem se ye fekte ǝbtum ǝbjim e me eyghel ge, ghele kubsen tetem a ghen é Feyine løyse mbeese ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.

Oku_Text: Ghen lu ghel ghe é yenen əbnəə vin we ǝb kooyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And they stayed continually at the temple, praising God.

Oku_Text: Nè lu & kenkɛɛte ne Gie lu, ey nè lu ghene Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God,

Oku_Text: Gie në yin nè lu Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and the Word was God.

Oku_Text: Əb nè lu ghene Feyine se kenkette.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He was with God in the beginning.

Oku_Text: Feyine nè bom ǝbfua ǝbjim ne lu nè Gie nê yin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Through him all things were made;

Oku_Text: Kefaa wiy ke Feyine nè bomen ne wiy nè Gie yin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: without him nothing was made that has been made.

Oku_Text: Eychii nè lòò se wen, ne eychii nê yine lu kekaaken se wele-meson.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In him was life, and that life was the light of all mankind.

Oku_Text: Kekaaken kine bane e mame nfiine, ne lòó nfiine jia limse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee nè lo se lu we Feyine nè tumen ne é tonte ge Joon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There was a man sent from God whose name was John.

Oku_Text: A b nè gwiy se fekte kum cyban në yi ka ghele keele, è fo eytem se cyghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He came as a witness to testify concerning that light, so that through him all might believe.
