Oku_Text: Joon se wene ngeŋ nè báà se lu cybane yé tene, di è gwiy se fekte se ghele kum eyghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He himself was not the light; he came only as a witness to the light.

Oku_Text: Eyban nê yine lu nkante kekaaken ke ke gwiyen e mbi è se foo cyban se wel ǝbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The true light that gives light to everyone was coming into the world.

Oku_Text: A b nè lu e mbi, ne Feyine nè bom ǝbfua əbjim nè wen, dî mbi jia keele wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He was in the world, and though the world was made through him, the world did not recognize him.

Oku_Text: Əb nè gwiy ɛ wene kelik, dî ghele wene jia fiisen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He came to that which was his own, but his own did not receive him.

Oku_Text: Ghel clee fiisen wen, ab me fo abtaawe se ghene ge ghene ye lu ghon a Feyin, dii ghel ghe é nè foon rytem se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet to all who did receive him, to those who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God—

Oku_Text: Ghene nè báà se lu ghon a Feyin ge é bii ǝblam na wele-meson, jia semte wel se bii ghene, jia kwaate wel, nè lu zykwaate a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: children born not of natural descent, nor of human decision or a husband’s will, but born of God.

Oku_Text: Giɛ nê yin nè gwiy è see se wele-meson, è se chii & ghesene məm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us.

Oku_Text: Ghesen se yene ǝbfon ə wen, ne lu abfon we ǝb nè kiin ka wán ə Feyin we ǝbmok.
English_NIV_Equivalent: We have seen his glory, the glory of the one and only Son, who came from the Father,

Oku_Text: Əb nè chame eysene ghel naa se nan gese lu wel ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: full of grace and truth.

Oku_Text: Joon nè lô sunnen se ghele kum wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (John testified concerning him. He cried out, saying,

Oku_Text: Əb nè suuy ge: Lu wen në vi we me në suuyen ge: Wel ǝblee gwiye & me eybam, di ne ǝb ngaŋte è chia me, bek ge é nè bii me ne ab nè shi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'This is the one I spoke about when I said, "He who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me."')"

Oku_Text: Se lu bek ka ǝb chammen Eysene yésen naa se nan, we ab foon rybose se ghesen, toke në cybosee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Out of his fullness we have all received grace in place of grace already given.

Oku_Text: Moses nè gwiy nè nchil se ghele ka Feyine nè tumen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the law was given through Moses;

Oku_Text: Jesus Christ gwiy Ké wene ne lu nè ɛycham eysen na Feyine gese nè ǝbluuye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

Oku_Text: Wel nè bezy ghàà yen Feyin, buu ken wán ə wen we ǝbmok we ab luun e Bay cybee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No one has ever seen God, but the one and only Son, who is himself God and is in closest relationship with the Father,

Oku_Text: Lu wen we ǝb nééyen në ghele keele Bay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: has made him known.

Oku_Text: Ghé mbiy na ghele Juuse tum ghé ntanlee ɛlee ghene ghel elee ghe é nè félen e ndaa-eykokse e Jolesalem ge ghene ndu è bif se Joon ge se lu wen nde-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now this was John’s testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him who he was.

Oku_Text: Ghene ndu, Joon jia ten se sunnen, dî è sunnen kelaanen ge: Mɛ báà se lu Vi we Feyine në Yiosen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He did not fail to confess, but confessed freely, 'I am not the Messiah.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me bif ge: Wè lu nde? Wè lu Elija-a? Joon fise ge: Engaŋ! Mẹ báà se lu wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They asked him, 'Then who are you? Are you Elijah?' He said, 'I am not.'

Oku_Text: Ghene gese bif ge: Wè lu wele ntum na Feyine we é chiyten-a? Əb tûn!
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Are you the Prophet?' He answered, 'No.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me suuy se wen ge: Fekte wel we wè luun ǝbghen se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Finally they said, 'Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us.

Oku_Text: Ghes lu se ndu nè eyfisee, se ghel ghe é kè tumen ghes. We suuy gee ghe kum ngeŋ ə yiz?
English_NIV_Equivalent: What do you say about yourself?'

Oku_Text: Joon fise ge: Me lu wel we wele ntum na Feyine we Ezaya nè suuyen ge: Gie wene jane ǝbkwak ge: Ghen neyte kevəə jii se Báaba é ke ye nduu e titiy!
English_NIV_Equivalent: John replied in the words of Isaiah the prophet, 'I am the voice of one calling in the wilderness, "Make straight the way for the Lord."'"

Oku_Text: Ghel ghii nè lòò ntum ghele kenone Falisee,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now the Pharisees who had been sent

Oku_Text: Joon fise ge: Me foo ne lu moo, dî ne wel ǝblee lu e ghene mam e leenen, ne ghene bezy ghàà keele wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I baptize with water,' John replied, 'but among you stands one you do not know.

Oku_Text: Əb bo gwiye eybam, ne chèmè lu ken nkfəəse guose evəə nə wen fene me báà se lu kekwenten ka lòó me yayte.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He is the one who comes after me, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.'

Oku_Text: Əbnǝǝ vin nè gan e Betani & njine jio Joldan ghàà Joon nè fóon moo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This all happened at Bethany on the other side of the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

Oku_Text: Əbtəke yio, Joon yen Jesus ka ǝb gwiyen, è suuy ge: Dii wan-njie na Feyine, we ǝb leyse mbeese mbi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, 'Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

Oku_Text: Lu wen në vi we me nè sunnen kum wen ge: Wele gwiye rybam chia me, bek ge é nè bii me ne ǝb nè se shi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the one I meant when I said, "A man who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me."

Oku_Text: Mẹ nè báà se ke wel we luun wen, di è gwiy è se foo moo se ghele se nery ge ghele Islaele keele wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I myself did not know him, but the reason I came baptizing with water was that he might be revealed to Israel.'

Oku_Text: Joon suuy dì, è me din ge: Me nè yen Keyioy ne ke sǝǝke eyyio ka kenlal è gok e wen ǝbghun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then John gave this testimony: 'I saw the Spirit come down from heaven as a dove and remain on him.

Oku_Text: Με se me ngen nè báà se ke wen, di ne Feyin Fe Fe nè tumen me ge me ye foo moo nè lô suuy se me ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: And I myself did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water told me,

Oku_Text: We nêy yen në Keyioy sǝǝke è guo ε wel abghun, fene lu wen we ǝb nêy ye foo ne lu Keyioy Kelanene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: '"The man on whom you see the Spirit come down and remain is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit."

Oku_Text: Me nè yenmen, è me se fekte se ghen ge wel në vi lu wán a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have seen and I testify that this is the Chosen One of God.'

Oku_Text: Əbtake gese yio, Joon se tiy ghene ghon-ryfel na wen elee ebaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day John was there again with two of his disciples.

Oku_Text: Jesus gese se chia, Joon gese yen wen è suuy ge: Dii wan-njie na Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, 'Look, the Lamb of God!'

Oku_Text: Ghon-cyfel nə Joon ghii yio di è me se nduu e Jesus rybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.

Oku_Text: Jesus benle è yen ne ghene gwiye e wen eybam è bif ge: Ghene kene ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Turning around, Jesus saw them following and asked, 'What do you want?'

Oku_Text: Ghene fise ge: Labbi (dii ge ndinte) wè chii ghááy?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They said, 'Rabbi' (which means 'Teacher'), 'where are you staying?'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Ghen gwiy è yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Come,' he replied, 'and you will see.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me ndu ghene ghe Jesus è yen ghaa ǝb nè chiin, è me gok fo, ǝbtəke meyse jie ghene bek ka në shiin & keleje nfam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they went and saw where he was staying, and they spent that day with him. It was about four in the afternoon.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝblee se ghel ghe ebaa ghe é nè yion ka Joon surjnen kum Jesus è se jumte wen nè lu Andole we wán-no a Simon Bita.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard what John had said and who had followed Jesus.

Oku_Text: Ka Andole yrnen Jesus e leghe è me ken wán-no a wene Simon è suuy se wen ge: Ghes yenmen Mesaya (dii ge Christ) ne lu ge, Vi wr Feyin nè yiosen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother Simon and tell him, 'We have found the Messiah' (that is, the Christ).

Oku_Text: Andole me lir Simon è ndu so se Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he brought him to Jesus.

Oku_Text: Jesus tun eyshie e Simon abghun è suuy se wen ge: Kéné lu wè Simon, we wán a Joon? É nêy ye tonte wê ge Sefas. (Eyghel ye Sefas lu ge Bita, ne lu ge cyghok.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus looked at him and said, 'You are Simon son of John. You will be called Cephas' (which, when translated, is Peter).

Oku_Text: Əbtake yio, Jesus lie ge yi ndu e Galilee, è loo ndu è yen Filib, è suuy se wen ge: Gwiy é loo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. Finding Philip, he said to him, 'Follow me.'

Oku_Text: Filib nè lu wele Betsaida, ka Andole ghene Bita.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Philip, like Andrew and Peter, was from the town of Bethsaida.

Oku_Text: Filib ndu è yen Natanael, è suuy se wen ge: Ghes yenmen wel we Moses nè nyaken e gwaale tesak, ghele ntum na Feyine gese nyak ge ǝb nãà gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Philip found Nathanael and told him, 'We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and about whom the prophets also wrote—

Oku_Text: Əb lu Jesus wê Nazalet, ne lu wán a Josef.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.'

Oku_Text: Natanael bif se wen ge: Le lòó njun faye Nazalet-a? Filib suuy se wen ge: Gwiy è yen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Nazareth! Can anything good come from there?' Nathanael asked. 'Come and see,' said Philip.

Oku_Text: Jesus yen ka Natanael gwiyen se yene wen è suuy ge: Lee naa nkante wele Islael le! Kenlife wiy e wene məm!
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, he said of him, 'Here truly is an Israelite in whom there is no deceit.'

Oku_Text: Natanael bif se Jesus ge: Wè ghan leey se geese keele me?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'How do you know me?' Nathanael asked.

Oku_Text: Jesus fise wen ge: Me kè lô se yɛne wè cyshaa fekake fig ne Filib geese jane wè.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'I saw you while you were still under the fig tree before Philip called you.'

Oku_Text: Natanael me suuy ge: Ndinte, wè lu wán a Feyin! We lu ǝbfone Islael!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Nathanael declared, 'Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the king of Israel.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Wè fo cyteme yie san e leenen bek ka me suuyen ge me kè yen wè eyshaa fekake fig-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said, 'You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree.

Oku_Text: Wề nây yɛn əbnəə ǝbchio ǝbyúméné we ǝb chian kine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You will see greater things than that.'

Oku_Text: Jesus gese suuy se wen ge: Me suuy ǝbluuy se wè ge: Wề náà yen eyyio ne zy dise, è yen ne nchiyse-ndaa nə Feyine koke gese chio e ketuu na Wanwel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He then added, 'Very truly I tell you, you will see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.'

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ɛybam ǝbchii əbbua é se yie ǝbjen e nteke Kana e Galilee, ne no a Jesus lu fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,

Oku_Text: é nè lô jaŋ sey Jesus ghene ghon-eyfel na wene se ǝbjen fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ka amndukme nè laayenmen, no a Jesus suuy se wen ge: Ghene bo ki wiy əmnduk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When the wine was gone, Jesus’ mother said to him, 'They have no more wine.'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: No ǝ wom, wè suuye ge me jel leey? Əbliy a wome beey ghàà kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Woman, why do you involve me?' Jesus replied. 'My hour has not yet come.'

Oku_Text: No a Jesus me suuy se ghel gheé nè félen e ndaa fo ge: Ghen neey naa ghe ke ǝb suuy ge ghen neey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: His mother said to the servants, 'Do whatever he tells you.'

Oku_Text: Ntonse moo nè tiy fo ntuufe, ne lu se ghel eylake Juuse see vii moo e ten se ye suute ǝblik so e ndee eylak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Nearby stood six stone water jars, the kind used by the Jews for ceremonial washing,

Oku_Text: Eymok nè lòó ey fo moo ka amtem əmbaa kene ammtal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: each holding from twenty to thirty gallons.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghel ghe é nè félen fo ge: Ghen lunse nton shin nè moome. Ghene me vii moo se ten se lunke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to the servants, 'Fill the jars with water'; so they filled them to the brim.

Oku_Text: sǝb me suuy se ghene ge: Ghen sef amlee è ndu è fo se wel zychia é ab mom. Ghene sefte è ndu so ǝb mom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he told them, 'Now draw some out and take it to the master of the banquet.' They did so,

Oku_Text: 9ǝb mom ne moo mi shi amnduk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine.

Oku_Text: Əb jia se ke ghaa əmnduk në mi lòòn (nè ke ken ghel ghe é nè tuun moome ten).
English_NIV_Equivalent: He did not realize where it had come from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew.

Oku_Text: Wel zychia vi me jan lum jen, è suuy se wen ge: Wele see loo ǝb chia mbiy è fo əmnduk əbjun, ghele nəə è kul, ne ǝb geese fo me ncheke nchek.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then he called the bridegroom aside and said, 'Everyone brings out the choice wine first and then the cheaper wine after the guests have had too much to drink;

Oku_Text: Wè sey lem əmnduk əbjun è fo ne lu a leenen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: but you have saved the best till now.'

Oku_Text: Kii në kine nè lu kenkette ǝbchǝyte ǝbchio ǝbyúméné we Jesus nè neɛyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: What Jesus did here in Cana of Galilee was the first of the signs through which he revealed his glory;

Oku_Text: Əb nè nery & Kana ɛ Galilee è din ǝbtaawe wen. Ghon-eyfel nə wene fő gytem se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and his disciples believed in him.

Oku_Text: Se cybame ten Jesus ghene no ǝ wen, nè ghone-no wene, nè ghon-eyfel na wene me chio e ntek ye Kabanaum è chii gho se ǝbchii se eylaay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother and brothers and his disciples. There they stayed for a few days.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ne eyyie Kechii Eyboyse nə ghele Juuse se baksene, Jesus me kok se Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When it was almost time for the Jewish Passover, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Əb kok è lii e ndaa-eykokse è yen ne ghele feene nase, nè bvǝyse njie gese nè ǝbnlale, gese nè ghel ghe në kubsen ǝbkaa ka é diom e ghene ǝbtatan,
English_NIV_Equivalent: In the temple courts he found people selling cattle, sheep and doves, and others sitting at tables exchanging money.

Oku_Text: ǝb me lie kfǝa è báà ngwan e ten è weefe ghel ghi è jum nase në bvǝyse ghene, è fayse se ndaa-cykokse è takse ǝbkaa a ghế eykubsen ǝbkaa, è bvəņse abtatan a ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he made a whip out of cords, and drove all from the temple courts, both sheep and cattle; he scattered the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.

Oku_Text: Əb nery di è suuy se ghel ghe é nè feenen abnlal ge: Ghen lie è fay so se fen! Ghàà ghen bense ndaa a ba a wom ge kelik abwey!
English_NIV_Equivalent: To those who sold doves he said, 'Get these out of here! Stop turning my Father’s house into a market!'

Oku_Text: Ghon-eyfel na wene kuume ge é nè nyak & waale-Feyin ge: "Feyine, shii we me kiin se ndaa na wè nerye ne rytem a yome lume se naŋ."
English_NIV_Equivalent: His disciples remembered that it is written: 'Zeal for your house will consume me.'

Oku_Text: Ghế mbiy na ghele Juuse bif se Jesus ge: Əbchayte we wê din se ghes lu ghe se din ge wè ki ǝbtaawe se yr neey əbnəǝ në vin?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Jews then responded to him, 'What sign can you show us to prove your authority to do all this?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghen sakte ndaa eykokse yin, é me kase baklé se abchii abtal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered them, 'Destroy this temple, and I will raise it again in three days.'

Oku_Text: Ghé mbiy na ghele Juus ghii me suuy se wen ge: É nè lie nguk njiose-ntuufe əmmkerk se bàklè ndaa-cykokse yin, é wè ye lu se kase se bàklè se ǝbchii ǝbtal-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They replied, 'It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and you are going to raise it in three days?'

Oku_Text: Di ne ndaa-eykokse ye Jesus nè suuyen nè lu abghun ə wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But the temple he had spoken of was his body.

Oku_Text: Naa se lu ka Jesus nè loolen se zykuo, ghon-eyfel na wene me kuume kenǝǝ në kin ke ǝb nè suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples recalled what he had said.

Oku_Text: Ghene me fo zytem se kenǝǝ ke nwaale-Feyine suuyen gese nè se gie se Jesus nè sunnen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they believed the Scripture and the words that Jesus had spoken.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney se lu z Jolesalem əbliye eyyie Kechii Eyboyse, ghele yrn abchǝyte ǝbchio ǝbyúméné we ab nè nrryen, ghel se eydio me fo cytem se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now while he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Festival, many people saw the signs he was performing and believed in his name.

Oku_Text: Di Jesus jia samse ghene bek ge ǝb në ke ghene naa cjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But Jesus would not entrust himself to them, for he knew all people.

Oku_Text: ǝb nè báà se shii ge wel fekte kum wel ǝblee se yi bek ge ab në ke teteme ghel ejim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He did not need any testimony about mankind, for he knew what was in each person.

Oku_Text: Wel ablee nè lô se lu ne é tonte ge Nikodemus, ne lu chee wele Juus, gese ne lu wele kenone Falisee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there was a Pharisee, a man named Nicodemus who was a member of the Jewish ruling council.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu nè ntǝke kechii kelee, ǝb ndu se Jesus è suuy ge: Ndinte, ghes ke ge wè lu ndinte ne tum Feyin bek ge ghes ke ge lòó wel jia ye nerye əbnəə əbchio ǝbyúméné we wè nzeyen vin buu ge Feyine lu ghene wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He came to Jesus at night and said, 'Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God. For no one could perform the signs you are doing if God were not with him.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Me suuy abluuy se wè ge, lòó wel jia yen cysake Feyin buu ge é kase bii wen kefrr.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus replied, 'Very truly I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again.'

Oku_Text: Nikodemus bif ge: Lòó é kase bii lezy wel ne ǝb kuymen?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'How can someone be born when they are old?' Nicodemus asked.

Oku_Text: Le lòó wel kase lii e no a wen ǝblee é ab bii wen kefer-α?.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Surely they cannot enter a second time into their mother’s womb to be born!'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Me suuy ǝbluuy se wè ge lòó wel jia lii se eysak nə Feyine buu ge é bii wen kefee në moome, gese nè Keyioy a Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'Very truly I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless they are born of water and the Spirit.

Oku_Text: Kii dii ke biin wel lu kechak, ne ke biin Keyioy ǝ Feyin lu Keyioy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the Spirit gives birth to spirit.

Oku_Text: Ghàà ye lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyúméné bek ka me ke suuyen ge é lu se kase se bii ghen & eybii eyfer.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You should not be surprised at my saying, "You must be born again."

Oku_Text: Əbfiefe lóó ǝb ye ndu ghaa əb konen, πε wel yio ka ǝb chian, di ke wiy ghaa ǝb lòòn, kene ghaa əb nduun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you cannot tell where it comes from or where it is going.

Oku_Text: Lu ghee-ghee ka ke luun se wel ǝbjim we Keyioy a Feyine biin kefee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.'

Oku_Text: Nikodemus me bif ge: Lòó kii nê kine gan & jii eγκε?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'How can this be?' Nicodemus asked.

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Wè lu ndinte cytaa Islael, è ghan lecy se jia se ye ke əbnəə në vin?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You are Israel’s teacher,' said Jesus, 'and do you not understand these things?

Oku_Text: Me suuy ǝbluuy se wè ge: Ghese suuy əbnəə se ghen we ghes keen gese fekte we ghes yenenmen, dî ne ghen beeme wiy əbnəə we ghese suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you the truth, we speak of what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, but still you people do not accept our testimony.

Oku_Text: Me le suuy əbnəə se ghen we luun wê ketuu ntie ne ghen beeme wiy, é ghen nêy beeme leey we luun wê eyyio?
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have spoken to you of earthly things and you do not believe; how then will you believe if I speak of heavenly things?

Oku_Text: Wel nè bɛɛy ghàà kok se gyyio chia ken Wanwel we ǝb nè lòòn e eyyio è sǝǝke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No one has ever gone into heaven except the one who came from heaven—the Son of Man.

Oku_Text: Ka Moses nè losen yio & fekak & ǝbliy we ghene nè luun e ǝbkwak, lu ghee-ghee ka é náà lose Wanwel,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,

Oku_Text: ka ghel ghe é foon sytem se wene kiile sychii yɛ ɛy jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: that everyone who believes may have eternal life in him.'

Oku_Text: Feyine nè lô kon mbi yin e ŋan è me fo wán ǝ wen we ǝbmok ge fo naa nde eytem se wen, é ab jia yi ley, dî è ye ki sychii ye ey jia yî may.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Oku_Text: Feyine nè báà tinse wán ə wen e mbi ge ab gwiy è sak ghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world,

Oku_Text: Əb nè tinse ge ǝb gwiy è boyse ghel boysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but to save the world through him.

Oku_Text: Feyine jia náà sak wel we ǝb foon eytem se wan ə wen në vin, dî ne naa nde we ǝb báà fo zytem se wen lu ne Feyine sakmen wen bek ge ǝb báà fo ɛytem se mey-mey wan a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe stands condemned already because they have not believed in the name of God’s one and only Son.

Oku_Text: Feyine sake mbi yin e jii ghan ge: Eybane gwiymen se mbi, dî ghele se korje ne lu nfiine chia cyban bek ge ghene nɛɛy mbee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the verdict: Light has come into the world, but people loved darkness instead of light because their deeds were evil.

Oku_Text: Wel we ǝb nɛɛyen mbee bane eyban, nè lòó ǝb jia fǝy è gwiy e eyban bek ge ǝb báà se koje ge ghele ye yene əbnəə əbbee we ab nɛɛyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who does evil hates the light, and will not come into the light for fear that their deeds will be exposed.

Oku_Text: Dî ne wel we ab neeyen əbnəə wê ǝbluuye, fǝy è gwiy ε eyban ka cybane ye din se ghele ge ǝb neey əbnəə ne lu we wê Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But whoever lives by the truth comes into the light, so that it may be seen plainly that what they have done has been done in the sight of God.

Oku_Text: Jesus ghene ghon-eyfel nə wene ney me ndu se eybee cylake Judea eylee, ghene ghene se chiite fo ne ǝb foo moo se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this, Jesus and his disciples went out into the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them and baptized.

Oku_Text: Joon se foo sey moo e nteke Aenon ne ey baksene se ntɛke Salim, bek ge jiose nè lu fo se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water,

Oku_Text: Ghele se gwiye se wen nè ǝb foo moo se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and people were coming and being baptized.

Oku_Text: Nè lu əbliy fo ne é beey ghàà vii Joon se nchak.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (This was before John was put in prison.)

Oku_Text: Ghon-eyfel nə Joon se beke mbek ghene ghe wele Juus ǝblee kum kensuu.
English_NIV_Equivalent: An argument developed between some of John’s disciples and a certain Jew over the matter of ceremonial washing.

Oku_Text: Ghon-eyfel nə Joone ndu se wen è suuy ge: Ndinte, wè le bo kuume wel we ghene wen nè luun e njine jio Joldan, wè se sunnene kum wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They came to John and said to him, 'Rabbi, that man who was with you on the other side of the Jordan—the one you testified about—'

Oku_Text: Əb vii di ka əb se foon moo, ne wel ǝbjim se nduu ken ne lu se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'look, he is baptizing, and everyone is going to him.'

Oku_Text: Joon fise ge: Lòó wel jia yɛε ki kefaa buu ge fo Feyin se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: To this John replied, 'A person can receive only what is given them from heaven.

Oku_Text: Ghen se ghene ngense lu ghel eyshie ge me nè lô suuy ge me báà se lu Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen, di ne Feyine nè tum me ge me gwiy se mbiy ne ǝb geese ye gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You yourselves can testify that I said, "I am not the Messiah but am sent ahead of him."

Oku_Text: Wel we ǝb kiilen jen lu lum jen ten, dî ne sun lum jen we ǝb teem e wen ɛybee lòó ǝb ye sarŋle ǝbliy we ab yion gie a wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who attends the bridegroom waits and listens for him, and is full of joy when he hears the bridegroom’s voice.

Oku_Text: Me loote sarple bek kii në kine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: That joy is mine, and it is now complete.

Oku_Text: Kenǝǝ ə wel në vi lu se ye nduu ken e mbiy, ne kome fiile rybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He must become greater; I must become less.'

Oku_Text: Vi di we ǝb lòòn e eyyio lu e ketuu se əbjime, ne vi di we ǝb lòòn e ketuu ntie lu wele ntie, gese sunnen ne lu əbnəə wê ketuu ntie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks as one from the earth.

Oku_Text: Vi di we ǝb lòòn ɛ eyyio lu ketuu se abjime.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The one who comes from heaven is above all.

Oku_Text: Əb fekte kenǝǝ ne lu ke ab yenen è gese yio, dî ne wel beeme wiy kenǝǝ ke ǝb fekten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.

Oku_Text: Dî ne naa nde fo eytem se kenǝǝ ke ǝb fekten fene ǝb bééme nè ǝbluuye ge kii dii ke Feyine neeyen lu ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever has accepted it has certified that God is truthful.

Oku_Text: Ke lu dî bek ge vi di we Feyine nè tumen fekte ne lu giese Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the one whom God has sent speaks the words of God,

Oku_Text: Feyine foo Keyioy a wen se wen, beese wiy kefaa.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for God gives the Spirit without limit.

Oku_Text: Baye koje wán ǝ wen, è me vii əbnəə əbjim e wene keghoo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ab foon eytem se wan vin yî ye ki eychii ye ey jia yî mɛy, dĩ ne naa nde we ǝb tunsen wan vine jia yî ye ki ɛychii, è jia yî boy se eyyafse eytem nə Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life, for God’s wrath remains on them.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney me keele ge ghele kenone Falisee yiomen ge yi kiile ghon-eyfel gese foo moo se ghene chia Joon;
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was gaining and baptizing more disciples than John—

Oku_Text: (Jesus se wene ngen nè báà se foo moo se ghele, nè foo ghon-cyfel nə wen.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: although in fact it was not Jesus who baptized, but his disciples.

Oku_Text: ǝb keele dì è me lòò se sybee eylak ye é tonten ge Judea, è fiile se ye é tornten ge Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he left Judea and went back once more to Galilee.

Oku_Text: ǝb loo ndu, è se lu se tosen e eylake Samalia.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now he had to go through Samaria.

Oku_Text: səb ndu è chin enteke Samalia cylee ne é tonte ge Sika.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he came to a town in Samaria called Sychar,

Oku_Text: Ey nè baksen se eylem ye Jakob we ba a ghone Islael nè foon se wan a wene Josef.
English_NIV_Equivalent: near the plot of ground Jacob had given to his son Joseph.

Oku_Text: Eybee moo na Jakob nè lu fo; se lu ka Jesus nè jelenmen è bol, ǝb nè lô diom & ǝbghun cybee fo ne chuy se tiysee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, tired as he was from the journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.

Oku_Text: Əbvii wê Samalia ǝblee gwiy fo se tuu moo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When a Samaritan woman came to draw water,

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Fó mẹ nè moo ǝmnəən.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to her, 'Will you give me a drink?'

Oku_Text: (Nè lu di ne ghon-eyfel na wene liimen è ndu entek se yun əbfua-ǝbyíéne.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: (His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.)

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi fise ge: Wè lu wele Juus, ne me lu wele Samalia, ke ghan leey ne wè yɛ lone moo əmnəən se me?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Samaritan woman said to him, 'You are a Jew and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink?'

Oku_Text: (Əb nè suuy dì bek ge ghele Juuse nè ghele Samalia nè see jia neey əbnəə konene)
English_NIV_Equivalent: (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.)

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Wè kè boy se ye ke kefaa ke Feyine foon, gese ke wel we ǝb lonen moo se wè, fene wê kè lô lon moo se wen, é ǝb fó ne lu moo me ǝm lernen se we ǝbliy ǝbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered her, 'If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.'

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi me suuy ge: Babey, wê báà se ki kefaa moo, ne eybee moo gese sooye, lòó wè lie ghááy moo me am lejen lernen?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Sir,' the woman said, 'you have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where can you get this living water?

Oku_Text: Wề chia ba a wese Jakob-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Are you greater than our father Jacob,

Oku_Text: Nè Fo wen zybee moo yin se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: who gave us the well

Oku_Text: Əb nè lô se nǝǝ moo me ǝm fǝyen e ten, ne ghon a wene ghene nyamse wene nǝǝ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and drank from it himself, as did also his sons and his livestock?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Wel nəə moo min é kendoje moo ko ye yume wen,
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again,

Oku_Text: dî ne wel nəə moo me foon me, é kendoje moo jia bo yî yum wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.

Oku_Text: Mi nê min me foon me nêy ye lu ntone jio e wen ǝbghun ye ey foon moo me luun eychii ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.'

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi me suuy ge: Babey, fó me nè mòò nê min ka kendoje moo jia bo nêy yum me, é me jia bo nêy ye gwiy fen se tuu moo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The woman said to him, 'Sir, give me this water so that I won't get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Ndu, è jan lum a vie, é ghene wen gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He told her, 'Go, call your husband and come back.'

Oku_Text: ǝb fise ge: Me báà se ki lum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I have no husband,' she replied.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: Ke lu e titiy ka wè suuyen ge wề bảà se ki lum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to her, 'You are right when you say you have no husband.

Oku_Text: Wè nè ghalmen elum etan, ne wel we ghene wen chiin e leenen chèmè wiy lum a vie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The fact is, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband.

Oku_Text: Kenǝǝ ke wè suuyen lu ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: What you have just said is quite true.'

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi me suuy ge: Babey, me yene ge we lu wele ntum nə Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Sir,' the woman said, 'I can see that you are a prophet.

Oku_Text: Ebaa a ghese në kokse Feyin e kfən fen, se lu ne ghen ghe Juuse suuye ge kelik ke é luun se ye kokse Feyin e ten lu kɛn e Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews claim that the place where we must worship is in Jerusalem.'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: No a wom, me suuy ǝbluuy se wè ge: Əbliye gwiye we ghele jia bo nêy ye kokse Bay & kfən fen kene & Jolesalem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Woman,' Jesus replied, 'believe me, a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.

Oku_Text: Ghen ghe ghele Samalia báà se loote ke wel we ghene koksen, dî ne ghes ke wel we ghese koksen bek ge eyboye lòò nɛ lu se ghele Juus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You Samaritans worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews.

Oku_Text: Di ne əbliye gwiye, ne ǝb kwenmen, we ghel ghe é loote koksen Baye nêy ye kokse wen se Keyioy gese kokse nè əbluuye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in the Spirit and in truth,

Oku_Text: Lee eynkii ghel ghe Baye kerjen ge é ye kokse wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for they are the kind of worshipers the Father seeks.

Oku_Text: Feyine lu Keyioy, ne ghel ghe é koksen wen lu se ye kokse se Keyioy, gese kokse wen nè ǝbluuye.
English_NIV_Equivalent: God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in the Spirit and in truth.'

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi me suuy ge: Me ke ge Vi we Feyine nè Yiosene nêy gwiy ne lu wel we é tonten ge Christ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The woman said, 'I know that Messiah' (called Christ) 'is coming.

Oku_Text: Əb nêy gwiy è fekte əbnəə əbjim se ghes.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he comes, he will explain everything to us.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se wen ge: Με σε me sunnen se wè lu Christ nê vi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus declared, 'I, the one speaking to you—I am he.'

Oku_Text: Se lu ken e ǝbliy fo ghon-eyfel nə Jesuse me se gwiye, è yen ne ǝb sunnene ghene ǝbvii, əbchio yúm ghene, dî wel jia bif se abvii ten ge ab se kerne ghe-a, kene se Jesus ge əb se sunnen ghene wen bek ghe-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just then his disciples returned and were surprised to find him talking with a woman. But no one asked, 'What do you want?' or 'Why are you talking with her?'

Oku_Text: Əbvii vi me vase feteme moo na wen se fo, è fiile se ntek è suuy se ghele ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then, leaving her water jar, the woman went back to the town and said to the people,

Oku_Text: Ghen gwiy è yen wel we ǝb suuyen əbnəə əbjim we me nè yimen neey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did.

Oku_Text: Le lòó ye lu wen Vi we Feyine nè Yiosen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Could this be the Messiah?'

Oku_Text: Ghene me lòò se ntek, è ndu se yene Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They came out of the town and made their way toward him.

Oku_Text: Nè lu dî ne ghon-eyfel na Jesuse kè se lone wen ge: Ndinte, kô yie kefaa!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Meanwhile his disciples urged him, 'Rabbi, eat something.'

Oku_Text: Əb séy suuy se ghene ge: Me ki abfua-abyíéne we ghene báà se ke.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he said to them, 'I have food to eat that you know nothing about.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me se kante ge: Le lòó ye lu ge wel kè lô fo abfua-ǝbyíéne se wen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then his disciples said to each other, 'Could someone have brought him food?'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Əbfua-ǝbyíéne nə me lu se ye neɛy kenǝǝ ke wel we ǝb nè tumen me kernen, se gese se meyse eyfel ye ǝb nè foon se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'My food,' said Jesus, 'is to do the will of him who sent me and to finish his work.

Oku_Text: Ghene lóó é ye suuy ge: Bersemen njon sekeck é abliy eydan esaje kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Don't you have a saying, "It’s still four months until harvest"?

Oku_Text: Dî ne me suuy se ghen ge ghen loote kiy è yen ka teleme keken.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I tell you, open your eyes and look at the fields!

Oku_Text: Esaņe biymen è kwen eydən.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They are ripe for harvest.

Oku_Text: Wel we ǝb kaan əbfua kiile ǝblaake wen, ne ǝb baate ǝbfua we ǝb kiilen ne lu eychii ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Even now the one who reaps draws a wage and harvests a crop for eternal life,

Oku_Text: Ke me se lu ge wel we ab lemen ghene wel we ǝb kaan nêy ye sarŋle ghel ɛjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: so that the sower and the reaper may be glad together.

Oku_Text: Ngan ye ge, wel ǝblee vii, ne wel ǝblee kaa, me se lu se ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Thus the saying "One sows and another reaps" is true.

Oku_Text: Με tume ghen ge ghen kaa ǝbfua ghaa ghen nè báà lem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I sent you to reap what you have not worked for.

Oku_Text: Ghel echine nè lem fô, ghene me se yio ǝbjun eyfel nə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Others have done the hard work, and you have reaped the benefits of their labor.'

Oku_Text: Ghele Samalia se eydio e ntek fő, fő eytem se Jesus bek ka ǝbvii vi nè suuyen ge: Əb kè fekte əbnəə əbjim we me nè yimen neey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed in him because of the woman’s testimony, 'He told me everything I ever did.'

Oku_Text: Ghene gwiy se Jesus, è lon wen ge ab ko moote ghene ghene. Jesus me chii fo se ǝbchii ǝbbua.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So when the Samaritans came to him, they urged him to stay with them, and he stayed two days.

Oku_Text: Ghel elee se eydio fó eytem se Jesus bek sakghɛ we ǝb në fekten;
English_NIV_Equivalent: And because of his words many more became believers.

Oku_Text: è me suuy se ǝbvii vi ge: Ghes lu ne é fó cytem e leenen, ne wiy bek kenǝǝ ke wè kè suuyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: They said to the woman, 'We no longer believe just because of what you said;

Oku_Text: Ghes fo bek ka ghes se ghese ngeŋse yion wen, nè ghɛs ke ge loote lu wen mboyse mbi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: now we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man really is the Savior of the world.'

Oku_Text: Jesus chii fo se ǝbchii əbbua, è me ndu se Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After the two days he left for Galilee.

Oku_Text: Nè lu dì ne Jesus suuymen ge: É see jia vəme wele ntum nə Feyine eylak ye ǝb lòòn ɛ ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (Now Jesus himself had pointed out that a prophet has no honor in his own country.)

Oku_Text: Əb nɛy chin e Galilee, ghele ghe fóò fiisen wen bek ge ghene nè lô ndu & Jolesalem əbliy eyyie Kechii Eyboyse, è yɛn we ǝb nè nɛɛyen fo e keleŋ əbliy fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him. They had seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the Passover Festival, for they also had been there.

Oku_Text: Jesus ney fiile se Kana e Galilee ghaa ǝb nè bensen moo se əmnduk.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once more he visited Cana in Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine.

Oku_Text: Nè lu dî ne cher eytaa eylee ye ɛy nè chiin e Kabanaum ki wán a wen nɛ əb yafe.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And there was a certain royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum.

Oku_Text: ǝb yio ge Jesus lòòmen se Judea, è gwiy Galilee, è me ndu è yen wen è se bernte ge ab ko gwiy è chuk wán a vi bek ka ǝb nè se kuon.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When this man heard that Jesus had arrived in Galilee from Judea, he went to him and begged him to come and heal his son, who was close to death.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Buu ge ghen yɛn əbchǝyte ǝbchio ǝbyúméné ghene əbnəə əbkanene ka me nɛɛyen, é ghen jia fo eytem se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Unless you people see signs and wonders,' Jesus told him, 'you will never believe.'

Oku_Text: Wel abghaken vi fise ken ge: Babey, gwiy é lóó buu dî, é wan ə wom kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The royal official said, 'Sir, come down before my child dies.'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: Yɛɛ nduu, wan a vie bonenmen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Go,' Jesus replied, 'your son will live.'

Oku_Text: Əb fö eytem se kenǝǝ ke Jesus suuyen è lòò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The man took Jesus at his word and departed.

Oku_Text: Əb loo ndu ghel eyfel na wene báksén wen, è suuy se wen ge: Wan ə vie di bonenmen!
English_NIV_Equivalent: While he was still on the way, his servants met him with the news that his boy was living.

Oku_Text: Əb bif se ghene ge: Əb kè kɛɛte se yio ǝbfief e leghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When he inquired as to the time when his son got better,

Oku_Text: Ghene fise ge: Chỉ se lu eyyionén ka chuy kasen | we kenchɛme ne vǝsen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: they said to him, 'Yesterday, at one in the afternoon, the fever left him.'

Oku_Text: Əb me kuume ge lu ken e ǝbliy në fo we Jesus ne suuyen se yi ge: Wan a vie bonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the father realized that this was the exact time at which Jesus had said to him, 'Your son will live.'

Oku_Text: Ghene ɛyshaa ndaa na wene me fo eytem se Jesus ghel ejim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So he and his whole household believed.

Oku_Text: Kine nè lu kenkumtene kenǝǝ nɛɛyen se ka ǝb nè lòòn se Judea è ndu & Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This was the second sign Jesus performed after coming from Judea to Galilee.

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine chia, Jesus we ghele Juuse nè yien kechii kase kok & Jolesalem ɛ əbliy keghakene nə ghen kelee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some time later, Jesus went up to Jerusalem for one of the Jewish festivals.

Oku_Text: Kechəm kelee nè lu & Jolesalem ne é tonte əblame Heblu ge Betzata, ne ke lu ǝbchio kengey we é tonten ge we bvǝy njie, ne ǝbnchərŋ əbghuye lu ǝbghune kengey fo ǝbtan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool, which in Aramaic is called Bethesda and which is surrounded by five covered colonnades.

Oku_Text: Felic ghel eyafkene nè lóó fe ye lu əmtaa fo ne lu əbnfef, nè ghel eləmmene gese nè ɛkeblene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Here a great number of disabled people used to lie—the blind, the lame, the paralyzed.

Oku_Text: [Ghene nè lóó é ye chiyte ge jio shin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and they waited for the moving of the waters.

Oku_Text: Nè loo lu ǝbliy nè ǝbliye, nchiy-ndaa Feyin sǝǝke & kechəm fo è shik jio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: From time to time an angel of the Lord would come down and stir the waters.

Oku_Text: Ey nè shike dì naa wel ǝbyafen ǝbke we ǝb kesten lii se kechəm fo, me bonen naa se eyyaf cyke ye ey kè yafen wen.]
English_NIV_Equivalent: The first one into the pool after each such disturbance would be cured of whatever disease they had.]

Oku_Text: Nè lu e məm nə ghel eyafkene ghe tene ne wel ǝblee lu ne ǝb yafmen se nguk njiose-cyfaame əmmtal.
English_NIV_Equivalent: One who was there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.

Oku_Text: Jesus yen ka ab nyinen fo & leghe, è keele ge ǝb yafmen se əbtəm əbdia, è me bif se wen ge: Le lòó wê ye kon se bonen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, 'Do you want to get well?'

Oku_Text: ǝb fise ge: Babey, jio shine, ghàà me ye ki wel we vii me se ten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Sir,' the invalid replied, 'I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred.

Oku_Text: Me lòò mom se lii se jio e leghe, wel abchin ko lii se mbiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: While I am trying to get in, someone else goes down ahead of me.'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se wen ge: Loole se eyyio, è lie fegham a fie è ye jel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus said to him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.'

Oku_Text: Wele ten me bonen kan se ǝbliy, è kaale fegham ǝ wen è se jele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At once the man was cured; he picked up his mat and walked.

Oku_Text: Kenəə kin nè gan e kechii eybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The day on which this took place was a Sabbath,

Oku_Text: Ghé mbiy nə ghele Juuse me suuy se wel we é kè chuken vi ge: Lan dì lu kechii eybam ne nchile báà beeme ge wel ye jel në feghame.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and so the Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, 'It is the Sabbath; the law forbids you to carry your mat.'

Oku_Text: Əb fise ge: Di kè suuy wel we ǝb kè chuken me ge me kaale fegham a fom è ye jel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he replied, 'The man who made me well said to me, "Pick up your mat and walk."

Oku_Text: Ghene bif se wen ge: Wel nê vi we ǝb kè suuyen ge we kaale fegham a fie, è ye jel lu nde?
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they asked him, 'Who is this fellow who told you to pick it up and walk?'

Oku_Text: Wel we é kè chuken cheme jia se ke wel we ǝb kè chuken wen bek ka Jesus kè leyenmen e mame ngose wel ye ey nè luun fo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The man who was healed had no idea who it was, for Jesus had slipped away into the crowd that was there.

Oku_Text: Se cybame ten Jesus yen wen e ndaa-eykokse è suuy se wen ge: Yen-a, kiy ka wè bonenmen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Later Jesus found him at the temple and said to him, 'See, you are well again.

Oku_Text: Ghàà gese ye neeye mbee fe kenǝǝ nây gan se wề chia kine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Stop sinning or something worse may happen to you.'

Oku_Text: Wel vi nɛy lòò, è ndu è suuy se ghé mbiy nə ghele Juus ghii ge nè chuk Jesus yi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The man went away and told the Jewish leaders that it was Jesus who had made him well.

Oku_Text: Ghé mbiy nə ghele Juuse me se nakse Jesus bek ka ab kè chuken wel e kechii eybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So, because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath, the Jewish leaders began to persecute him.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Ba a wom fele ǝbliy ǝbjim chǝǝke wiy ne me ko ye fel sey əbliy abjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: In his defense Jesus said to them, 'My Father is always at his work to this very day, and I too am working.'

Oku_Text: Kenəǝ ke ǝb nè suuyen nê kin nɛɛy ghé mbiye na ghele Juuse me vii viin ge ghene yuy wen ne wiy ken bek ka ǝb kè kolen nchile kechii eybam, ne lu sey bek ka ǝb nè tonte Feyin ge Ba a vi se din ge ghene Feyine lu eyghel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For this reason they tried all the more to kill him; not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Me suuy ǝbluuy se ghen ge, lòó wan jia nɛɛy kenǝǝ se wene ngen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus gave them this answer: 'Very truly I tell you, the Son can do nothing by himself;

Oku_Text: Əb lóó ǝb ye neɛy ne lu ken kenǝǝ ke əb yenen ne ba ǝ wen neeye, bek ge naa ghe ke Baye nesyen lu keghene ke wane nɛɛyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: he can do only what he sees his Father doing, because whatever the Father does the Son also does.

Oku_Text: Baye kone wan, dine əbnəə əbjim we ǝb neeyen se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all he does.

Oku_Text: Əb nêy din é wan ye neey əbnəə əbtaa we ǝb chian vine, é ghen yɛ lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyúméné.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yes, and he will show him even greater works than these, so that you will be amazed.

Oku_Text: Ken ka Baye losen ghel se eykuo, nerye ne ghene kase chii, lu sey ka wan yenen ge yi nɛɛy se ghel ghe ab kerjen se nery se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son gives life to whom he pleases to give it.

Oku_Text: Bay se wene ngen báà se tenen nsake wel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Moreover, the Father judges no one,

Oku_Text: Əb fomen ǝbtaawe ne lu se wan ge ǝb ye tenen;
English_NIV_Equivalent: but has entrusted all judgment to the Son,

Oku_Text: ka wel ǝbjime ye ngvamle wán ghee-ghee ka é ngvamlen Bay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ǝb báà se ngvəmle wan, lu nɛ ǝb báà se ngvamle Bay we ǝb nè tumen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father, who sent him.

Oku_Text: Me suuye ǝbluuy se ghen ge naa nde we ǝb yion gie a som, è fo cytem se vi we ab nè tumen me, se ki eychii ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Very truly I tell you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life

Oku_Text: É jia bo yî sak wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and will not be judged

Oku_Text: Əb boymen se eykuo è se ki eychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but has crossed over from death to life.

Oku_Text: Me suuye ǝbluuy se ghen ge: Əbliye gwiye, ne ǝb shi ken e keghoo, ne lu ǝbliy we ghel ghe é kuonmen yio gie wan ə Feyine, ne naa nde we ǝb yio kiile sychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Very truly I tell you, a time is coming and has now come when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and those who hear will live.

Oku_Text: Ken ka sychii se lòòn se Bay, lu ghee-ghee ka Əb neeymen ge ey yɛ lòò sɛy se wan.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son also to have life in himself.

Oku_Text: Bay lu ne ab fomen sey ǝbtaawe se wan ge ab ye tenen nsakse, bek ge ab lu Wanwel.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And he has given him authority to judge because he is the Son of Man.

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghen ye lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyúméné bek kii nê kine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Do not be amazed at this,

Oku_Text: Əbliye gwiye we ghel ɛjim ghe é nè kuomen nêy yio gie ǝ wen;
English_NIV_Equivalent: for a time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice

Oku_Text: è loole se eykuo, é ghel ghe é nè neeyen njun loole è yɛ chii, é ghel ghe é nè neeyen mbee loole è lii se ngek ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and come out—those who have done what is good will rise to live, and those who have done what is evil will rise to be condemned.

Oku_Text: Lòó me jia neey kenəə əbtaawe nə me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: By myself I can do nothing;

Oku_Text: Me sake ken nsak a wel ka Feyine dinen se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I judge only as I hear, and my judgment is just,

Oku_Text: Eysak a yome me se lu & titiy bek ge me báà se neeye ne lu kenǝǝ ke me kerjen, me neeye ken ne lu kenǝǝ ke wel we ǝb nè tumen me kerjen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: for I seek not to please myself but him who sent me.

Oku_Text: Loo lu ge suuy me ge lɛɛ wel we me luun abghen, fene lòó wel jia beeme ge kenəə ke me suuyen lu ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.

Oku_Text: Dî ne wel ǝblee lu we ǝb sunnen kum me, ne me ke ge kenǝǝ kɛ əb suuyen lu ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There is another who testifies in my favor, and I know that his testimony about me is true.

Oku_Text: Ghen nè lô tum ghel se Joon se keele ge me se lu nde-a, kenəə ke ǝb nè suuyen se lu ǝbluuy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You have sent to John and he has testified to the truth.

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine báà se lu ge me yekte ne lu əbghune əbnəə we ghele suuyen kum me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Not that I accept human testimony;

Oku_Text: Me suuy le ken ka ghene kiile eyboy,
English_NIV_Equivalent: I mention these things so that you may be saved.

Oku_Text: Joon nè lu ka kekaaken ke ke baalen, bane, ne ghen nè lô se koje se yio əbjurj eyban nə wen se kelerŋ əbliy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: John was a lamp that burned and gave light, and you chose for a time to enjoy his light.

Oku_Text: Dî ne me ki kenǝǝ kum ngen a yom ne ke taa chia ke Joon nè fekten.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I have testimony weightier than that of John.

Oku_Text: Eyfel ye Ba a wom tumen me ge me ye neey lu eyghene ye zy dinen ge nè tum Bay me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the works that the Father has given me to finish—the very works that I am doing—testify that the Father has sent me.

Oku_Text: Bay we ǝb nè tumen me dine sey wel we me luun abghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And the Father who sent me has himself testified concerning me.

Oku_Text: Ghen në bezy ghàà yio gie ǝ wen, ne ghen nè beey ghàà yi yen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You have never heard his voice nor seen his form,

Oku_Text: Ghene báà ghal giese wen bek ka ghɛne báà fo eytem se vi di we ǝb nè tumen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: nor does his word dwell in you, for you do not believe the one he sent.

Oku_Text: Ghene yieke waale-Feyin naa ketaan kwaate ge lu cyghen yɛ lòó ey nɛɛy é ghen kiile sychii ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You study the Scriptures diligently because you think that in them you have eternal life.

Oku_Text: Dwaale nê yin dì surgnene kum me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: These are the very Scriptures that testify about me,

Oku_Text: Ghene cheme jia se lu se gwiy se me se kiile sychii ye ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: yet you refuse to come to me to have life.

Oku_Text: Shii a wom báà se lu ge ghele ye bente me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'I do not accept glory from human beings,

Oku_Text: Dî se kene lu ne me ke ge ghene báà se korje Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but I know you. I know that you do not have the love of God in your hearts.

Oku_Text: Me gwiy e ǝbtaawe na Ba ǝ wom, ghene jia fiisen, di ne wel gwiy se wene ngen, é ghen me fiisen wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I have come in my Father’s name, and you do not accept me; but if someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.

Oku_Text: Lòó ghen fő leey eytem se me ne ghen koje ge ghele ye bente ghen, dî koje wiy se kiile eybente se vi di we luun ken wen Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: How can you believe since you accept glory from one another but do not seek the glory that comes from the only God?

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghen ye kwaate ge me yî gham ghen & eyshiɛ nə Bay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'But do not think I will accuse you before the Father.

Oku_Text: Wel we ǝb yî gham ghen lu Moses we ghen nè foon eytem se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your accuser is Moses, on whom your hopes are set.

Oku_Text: Lòò è ye lu ge ghen nè lô loote beeme kenǝǝ ke Moses nè nyaken fene ghen beeme ne lu me bek ge ab nè nyak kum me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, for he wrote about me.

Oku_Text: Lòò leɛy é ghen beeme kenǝǝ ke me suuyen ne ghen nè báà beeme kenǝǝ ke Moses nè nyaken?
English_NIV_Equivalent: But since you do not believe what he wrote, how are you going to believe what I say?'

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine chia, Jesus dian 6 ikiye chalee dian tonten sey ge Tibelias è ndu e njine vii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Some time after this, Jesus crossed to the far shore of the Sea of Galilee (that is, the Sea of Tiberias),

Oku_Text: Felie wele lòò e wen cybam bek əbnəə əbchio ǝbyúméné we ǝb nè neeyen se ghel eyafkene ne ghene yene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and a great crowd of people followed him because they saw the signs he had performed by healing the sick.

Oku_Text: Jesus ghene ghon-eyfel nə wene me lòò è kok e kfən è diom.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus went up on a mountainside and sat down with his disciples.

Oku_Text: Nè lu di ne ǝbliy we ghele Juuse see yie Kechii Eyboyse se baksene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Jewish Passover Festival was near.

Oku_Text: Jesus loo kiy è yen ne felis wele gwiye, è bif se Filib ge: Lòó ghesen yun ghááy ǝbfua-əbyíéne əbkwenen we yiese ghel ghin so?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When Jesus looked up and saw a great crowd coming toward him, he said to Philip, 'Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?'

Oku_Text: (Əb nè bife kine se mome Filib bek ge ǝb nè se ke kenǝǝ ke ǝb neey.)
English_NIV_Equivalent: He asked this only to test him, for he already had in mind what he was going to do.

Oku_Text: Filib fise ge: Lòò è yɛ lu ge wel ǝbjime kiile kefaa se yie fene lòó yun keban se ǝbkaa ne ǝb chia denalius eyvie eybaa".
English_NIV_Equivalent: Philip answered him, 'It would take more than half a year’s wages to buy enough bread for each one to have a bite!'

Oku_Text: Wan-eyfel nə wen ǝblee we Andole, we nè luun wán-no a Simon Bita suuy ge:
English_NIV_Equivalent: Another of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, spoke up,

Oku_Text: "Wan əbluumen əblee lu fen ki ntukse keban setan nè səə sebaa, dĩ nɛ áb báà chỉn ka lòó ghel ghine yić.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Here is a boy with five small barley loaves and two small fish, but how far will they go among so many?'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy ge: Ghen lam é ghene diom e ntie.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said, 'Have the people sit down.'

Oku_Text: Nè lu dî ne abgii dio fo ge.
English_NIV_Equivalent: There was plenty of grass in that place,

Oku_Text: Ghel ejime me diom, ne eluumen lu ka nkam setan di.
English_NIV_Equivalent: and they sat down (about five thousand men were there).

Oku_Text: Jesus lie ntukse keban shi, è bèèlè Feyin, è fo é giate se ghel ghe é nè diom, ǝb beele Feyin se saǝse fey, è fo é giate se ghele, wel əbjim yić ka ǝb në kɛŋen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus then took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted. He did the same with the fish.

Oku_Text: Wel ǝbjim yíé è kul, Jesus suuy se ghon-eyfel na wene ge: Ghen binte we ǝb bersen fe kefaa ley se lólò.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they had all had enough to eat, he said to his disciples, 'Gather the pieces that are left over. Let nothing be wasted.'

Oku_Text: Ghene chinten ǝbmbɛkele abfua-ǝbyíéne we nè yien ntukse keban se setan shi ǝb beese, əb lun nkaase njio-sebaa eyvəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.

Oku_Text: Ghel ghii yen kenǝǝ əbchio əbyúméné ke Jesus neeyen è suuy ge: Vin lu ken naa wele ntum nə Feyine we ŋwaale-Feyine dinen ge ǝb náà gwiy e mbi fen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After the people saw the sign Jesus performed, they began to say, 'Surely this is the Prophet who is to come into the world.'

Oku_Text: Jesus keele ge ghel ghii shi se koo yi se kokse ge abfon, è me sosen è kase kok se kfan wen nyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus, knowing that they intended to come and make him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by himself.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu e likfǝǝy, ghon-eyfel nə Jesus chio è ndu ǝbchio jio eyghuy,
English_NIV_Equivalent: When evening came, his disciples went down to the lake,

Oku_Text: è lii se kenǝk è kase dian se Kabanaum.
English_NIV_Equivalent: where they got into a boat and set off across the lake for Capernaum.

Oku_Text: Kebeye fin, ne Jesus beey ghàà baksen ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: By now it was dark, and Jesus had not yet joined them.

Oku_Text: Əbrief ǝbtaa me se chia, shikse jio ne moome loole.
English_NIV_Equivalent: A strong wind was blowing and the waters grew rough.

Oku_Text: Ney se lu ne ghon-eyfel nə Jesuse jelmen e məme jio ka tetarŋ tetal kene tekzek dì, ghene yen Jesus ne ǝb jele e ketuu moo baksen gwiye se kenǝk, è me se fane.
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the water; and they were terrified.

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Ghàà ghen ye fan, lu me Jesus.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But he said to them, 'It is I; don't be afraid.'

Oku_Text: Ghene se koje se lie wen, è loo chin në kenǝke chinmen ɛ shif ghàà ghene nè nduun se fay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they were willing to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the shore where they were heading.

Oku_Text: Əbtake nɛy yio felie wel fe fe nè goken e njine jio yen ge lu ken kenək fo kemok, è me keele ge Jesus nè báà lii se kenǝk ghene ghon-eyfel nə wene, dii ge ghene ne lòò è vase wen e eybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the opposite shore of the lake realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had gone away alone.

Oku_Text: Əbnək əblee ko nè lòò e Tibelias, è gwiy ɛ ǝbchio jio bene fe Báàba nè lien ntukse keban, è bèèlè Feyin, è yiese felie wel so.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

Oku_Text: Ngose wel yi keele ge Jesus ghene ghon-eyfel na wene báà se lu fo, è lii əbnək è dian se Kabanaum, keje nduu nè wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Once the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum in search of Jesus.

Oku_Text: Ghene yen Jesus e njine jio fey, è bif se wen ge: Ndinte, wè kè gwiy fen e leghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, 'Rabbi, when did you get here?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Mɛ suuy ǝbluuy se ghen ge, ghene kerne me bek ka ghen ne yien keban è kul ne báà se lu ge ǝbchǝyte əbnəə əbchio ǝbyúméné we me neeyen se dine ghe-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'Very truly I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw the signs I performed but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghen ye fel bek əbfua-əbyíéne we ǝb see bef.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Do not work for food that spoils,

Oku_Text: Ghen sey ye fel ne lu se we ab see lu ken luun, gwiye nè cychii ye ey jia yî mey, abfua-ǝbyíéne në vine lu ne nêy fo Wanwel se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you.

Oku_Text: Feyin we Bay dinmen ge yi ke se cyfel ye ǝb félen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For on him God the Father has placed his seal of approval.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me bif se wen ge: Lòó ghes jel lɛɛy se geese ye fel eyfel a Feyin?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then they asked him, 'What must we do to do the works God requires?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Eyfel ye Feyine kenen ge ghen yɛ fel lu ge: Ghen fó cytem se wel vi we yi nè tumen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus answered, 'The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent.'

Oku_Text: Ghene bif ge: Kenəə əbchio ǝbyúméné ke wè nɛɛy ka ǝbchǝyte ge ghes yen è fo eytem se wè lu ghe? Wé neey ghe?
English_NIV_Equivalent: So they asked him, 'What sign then will you give that we may see it and believe you? What will you do?

Oku_Text: Ebaa ǝ ghesene nè yie abfua-ǝbyléne we é nè tonten ge manna e ǝbkwak, ghee-ghee ka nwaale-Feyine suuyen ge: Əb nè fo keban ke ke nè lòòn e eyyio se ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written: "He gave them bread from heaven to eat."

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Me suuy ǝbluuy se ghen ge nè báà fo Moses ǝbfua-ǝbyićne se ghen we ǝb lòò eyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to them, 'Very truly I tell you, it is not Moses who has given you the bread from heaven,

Oku_Text: nè fo Ba ǝ wom nkante ǝbfua-əbyíéne we ǝb lòòn e eyyio se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.

Oku_Text: Əbfua-ǝbyićne we Feyine fóon lu foo ɛychii se mbi.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For the bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.'

Oku_Text: Ghene yio è me suuy ge: Babey, yɛɛ foo ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne nê vin se ghes əbliy ǝbjim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Sir,' they said, 'always give us this bread.'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Me lu ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne we ǝb fóon eychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus declared, 'I am the bread of life.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ǝb gwiyen se me jia bo ye yio jin, nè kendore moo jia bo ye yume wel vi we ǝb foon sytem se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.

Oku_Text: Dî ne me nè lô suuy se ghen ge ghen lu ne é yen me, è jia fo eytem se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But as I told you, you have seen me and still you do not believe.

Oku_Text: Wel abjim we Ba a wom foon se me nêy gwiy se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: All those the Father gives me will come to me,

Oku_Text: Me jia yî jum ghii dii ghe é gwiyen se me;
English_NIV_Equivalent: and whoever comes to me I will never drive away.

Oku_Text: bek ge me lòò eyyio è gwiy e ntie fen se ye nɛɛy kenǝǝ ke wel we ǝb nè tumen me kernen, ne wiy ke korjen me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For I have come down from heaven not to do my will but to do the will of him who sent me.

Oku_Text: Kenǝǝ ke wel we ǝb nè tumen me kenen lu ge ghàà mẹ neɛy ge wel se ghii dii ghe ǝb nè foon se me ley, ne me yî lose ghene se eykuo e kechii kenləysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I shall lose none of all those he has given me, but raise them up at the last day.

Oku_Text: Kenǝǝ ke Ba a wom kerjen lu ge wel ǝbjim we ǝb yenen wan è fo cytem se wene náà kiile sychii yɛ ey jia yî mey, ne me náà lose ghene se eykuo e kechii kenləysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For my Father’s will is that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in him shall have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.'

Oku_Text: Ghele Juus me keete è se vǝke bek ka Jesus nè suuyen ge yi lu abfua-abyíéne we ǝb səǝken gwiy ε εyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At this the Jews there began to grumble about him because he said, 'I am the bread that came down from heaven.'

Oku_Text: Ghene me se suuye ge: Wel vin le báà se lu Jesus we wán ə Josef-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: They said, 'Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph,

Oku_Text: Ghesen ke ba a wen ghene no a wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: whose father and mother we know?

Oku_Text: Lòó ǝb ye suuy leey ɛ leenen ge yi səǝke gwiy ne lu eyyio?
English_NIV_Equivalent: How can he now say, "I came down from heaven"?'

Oku_Text: Jesus fise ge: Ghàà ghen se vək se ghene ngeŋse.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'Stop grumbling among yourselves,' Jesus answered.

Oku_Text: Lòó wel jia gwiy se me buu ge gufte Bay we ǝb nè tumen me ge ǝb gwiy san; ne me náà lose wen se eykuo ɛ kechii kenləysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws them, and I will raise them up at the last day.

Oku_Text: Ghele ntum nə Feyine nè nyak ge: "Feyine náà dinte əbnəə se ghel ghin əbjim."
English_NIV_Equivalent: It is written in the Prophets: "They will all be taught by God."

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ǝb yionmen è gese yieke kenǝǝ ke Baye suuyen, gwiye se me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Everyone who has heard the Father and learned from him comes to me.

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine báà me se lu ge wel nè yenmen Bay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No one has seen the Father

Oku_Text: Lu ken vi di we ǝb nè lòòn se Feyine we ǝb nè yenenmen Bay.
English_NIV_Equivalent: except the one who is from God; only he has seen the Father.

Oku_Text: Me suuy ǝbluuy se ghen ge naa nde we ǝb foon zytem se me se ki sychii yɛ ey jia yî mey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Very truly I tell you, the one who believes has eternal life.

Oku_Text: Me lu ǝbfua-ǝbyféne we ǝb foon eychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I am the bread of life.

Oku_Text: Ebaa a ghene nè lô yie manna əbkwak è ko naa kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, yet they died.

Oku_Text: Di ne ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne we ǝb lòòn e eyyio lu eynkii abfua-ǝbyíéne we wel yien, é ǝb jia yî kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But here is the bread that comes down from heaven, which anyone may eat and not die.

Oku_Text: Me lu abfua-ǝbyíéne we ǝb lòòn e zyyio ne lu we ǝb foon cychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: I am the living bread that came down from heaven.

Oku_Text: Yie naa nde əbfua-ǝbyíéne në vin é ǝb yî ye chii ndu ken nduun.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever eats this bread will live forever.

Oku_Text: Əbfua-əbyíéne nê vine lu ǝbghun a wom we me nêy fo se boyse ghele ghe mbi fene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This bread is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.'

Oku_Text: Ghele Juus yio kine è me kɛɛte se ye beke mbek se ghene ngeŋse ge: Lòó wel vin fó lɛɛy ǝbghun ə wen ge ghesen yie?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then the Jews began to argue sharply among themselves, 'How can this man give us his flesh to eat?'

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Me suuy ǝbluuy se ghen ge: Loo lu ghene jia yie ǝbghun ə Wanwel, è gese nəə əmndun mə wen, é ghen jia ye ki eychii e məm nə ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to them, 'Very truly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ǝb yien ǝbghun ə wom, è nəə əmndun mə mom se ki eychii ye ey jia yî mey, é me náà lose wen se eykuo & kechii kenlǝysen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.

Oku_Text: Ke lu dî bek ka ǝbghun a wome lu nkante abfua-ǝbyíéne, nɛ əmndun mə mom lu nkante eynǝǝ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For my flesh is real food and my blood is real drink.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we ǝb yien abghun ə wom, è nəə əmndurŋ mə mom lu ne ǝb gokmen e me məm, me gok & wene məm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in them.

Oku_Text: Bay we ǝb foon ɛychii nè tum me, se lu bek wen we me kiin sychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father,

Oku_Text: Lu ghee-ghee ka yien naa nde ǝbghun a wom é əb ye ki eychii bek me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: so the one who feeds on me will live because of me.

Oku_Text: Kii nê kine lu əbfua-əbyféne we əb səəken gwiy e eyyio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: This is the bread that came down from heaven.

Oku_Text: ǝb báà se lu ka əbfua-ǝbyíéne we ebaa ə ghene nè yien è ko naa kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Your ancestors ate manna and died,

Oku_Text: Wel yie ǝbfua-ǝbyíéne nê vin é ǝb jia bo yî kuo.
English_NIV_Equivalent: but whoever feeds on this bread will live forever.'

Oku_Text: Jesus nè suuy əbnəə nê vin e ngay eyjeme ye Kabanaum e ǝbliy we ǝb nè dinten əbnəə se ghele.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He said this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.

Oku_Text: Ghon-cyfel nə Jesuse yio kenǝǝ kin ke ǝb nè suuyen, ndio wel e ghene mam me se suuy ge: Eyyíéke yine taa naa se naŋ.
English_NIV_Equivalent: On hearing it, many of his disciples said, 'This is a hard teaching.

Oku_Text: Lòó bééme nde kenǝǝ ten?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Who can accept it?'

Oku_Text: Jesus ko me keele ge ghene vəke kesunnene ki è me suuy se ghene ge: Kii në kine taa se ghen se beeme-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Aware that his disciples were grumbling about this, Jesus said to them, 'Does this offend you?

Oku_Text: Le loo lu ghene yen Wanwel ne ǝb kase koke se ghaa ǝb nè luun se mbiy é ghen neey lɛɛy?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then what if you see the Son of Man ascend to where he was before!

Oku_Text: Kefaa ke ke fóon sychii lu Keyioy a Feyin.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Spirit gives life;

Oku_Text: Wel se wene ngen lòó ǝb jia nɛɛy kenǝǝ se kiile.
English_NIV_Equivalent: the flesh counts for nothing.

Oku_Text: Gie se me suijnen se ghen lòò se Keyioy nê kine ne lu se se fóon cychii.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The words I have spoken to you—they are full of the Spirit and life.

Oku_Text: Dî ne ghen clee báà fo cytem.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet there are some of you who do not believe.'

Oku_Text: Jesus nè suuy dì ka ǝb në se keen ghel ghe é báà fo cytem se wen, gese ke wel we ǝb nêy feene wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For Jesus had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray him.

Oku_Text: Əb me gese suuy ge: Ghey kenǝǝ ke ke nè neeyen me suuy se ghen ge lòó wel jia gwiy se me buu ge nery Bay ge ǝb gwiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He went on to say, 'This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless the Father has enabled them.'

Oku_Text: Se kèètè se ǝbliy fo ndionen ghon-eyfel nə Jesus me fiile eybam, è jia gese se jele ghene ghene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: From this time many of his disciples turned back and no longer followed him.

Oku_Text: Əb me bif se ghé ntume ghe njio-sebaa eyvəm ge: Leey se ghen! Ghene koje se vase me sey-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: 'You do not want to leave too, do you?' Jesus asked the Twelve.

Oku_Text: Simon Bita fise ge: Báàba, lòó ghes ndu se nde?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Simon Peter answered him, 'Lord, to whom shall we go?

Oku_Text: We ki gie se se foon eychii ye ey jia yi mry.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You have the words of eternal life.

Oku_Text: Ghes lu ne é fó cytem se wè è se ke ge wè lu wel ǝblanen vi we ǝb lòòn se Feyine.
English_NIV_Equivalent: We have come to believe and to know that you are the Holy One of God.'

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy ge: Me le nè báà chok ghen njio-sebaa syvəm, wel ablee se lu kevarj-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus replied, 'Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!'

Oku_Text: Əb nè sunnen kum Judas we wán ə Simon Iskaliot Judas nè lu wel ǝblee & məme ghon-eyfel ghe njio-sebaa eyvəm.
English_NIV_Equivalent: (He meant Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, who, though one of the Twelve, was later to betray him.)

Oku_Text: Se lu eybame kii në kine Jesus me se kale ken & Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After this Jesus went around in Galilee.

Oku_Text: Əb nè báà se koje se ndu e Judea bek ge ghé mbiye na ghele Juuse nè baate se yuye wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: He did not want to go about in Judea because the Jewish leaders there were looking for a way to kill him.

Oku_Text: ǝbliy we ghele Juuse sez yie kechii keghakene ke é tonten ge ké amtaa nè se baksene.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But when the Jewish Festival of Tabernacles was near

Oku_Text: Ghone-no a Jesuse me suuy se wen ge: Loo se fen è ndu e Judea ka ghon-cyfel nə wè yen əbnəə ǝbchio ǝbyúméné we wè nezyen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus’ brothers said to him, "Leave Galilee and go to Judea, so that your disciples there may see the works you do.

Oku_Text: Wele kene ge ghele keele kenǝǝ ke ǝb neeyen, é ab jia ya ghoone so ghoonen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: No one who wants to become a public figure acts in secret. Since you are doing these things, show yourself to the world.”

Oku_Text: Dine ngej a yie se mbi ka we nezyen əbnəə əbchio ǝbyúméné vine le.
English_NIV_Equivalent: For even his own brothers did not believe in him.

Oku_Text: Ghene nè suuy di bek ge ghene ghe ghone-no a wene chèmè nè báà fo zytem se wen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Therefore Jesus told them, "My time is not yet here; for you any time will do.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Əbliy we lòó me ndu beey ghàn kwen, di ne əbliy abjime lu ǝbkwenen se ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify that its works are evil.

Oku_Text: Ghele ghe mbi báà se bane ghen di bane me bek ka me suuyen se ghene əbliy ǝbjim ge jiise ghene báà jof.
English_NIV_Equivalent: You go to the festival. I am not going up to this festival, because my time has not yet fully come.”

Oku_Text: Ghen ndu, è yiz kechii keghakene, me jia ndu bek ka ǝbliy we lòó me ndu e ten bery ghaa kwen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: After he had said this he stayed in Galilee.

Oku_Text: Əb suuy di è me gok e Galilee.
English_NIV_Equivalent: However, after his brothers had left for the festival, he went also, not publicly, but in secret.

Oku_Text: Ghone-no a wene me ndu eyyie yi, əb yese è ndu sey, dî è jia se lu e ghele mam, è se lele lelen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now at the festival the Jewish leaders were watching for Jesus and asking, "Where is he?”

Oku_Text: Ghé mbiye na ghele Juuse me se keje wen fo bifte ge ab se waay-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Among the crowds there was widespread whispering about him. Some said, "He is a good man.” Others replied, "No, he deceives the people.”

Oku_Text: Nè lu di ne ghele cham cham kum wen fo naa se eydio.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But no one would say anything publicly about him for fear of the leaders.

Oku_Text: Ghel elee nè suuy ge ǝb lu wel ǝbjun, ne elee suuy ge: Engary, ǝb lofte ghel e loften.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Not until halfway through the festival did Jesus go up to the temple courts and begin to teach.

Oku_Text: Ghene loo fane ghé mbiye na ghele Juuse di wel jia se surŋnen kum Jesus e ngenge.
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Jews there were amazed and asked, "How did this man get such learning without having been taught?”

Oku_Text: Eyyie yi ney loo gianen
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus kok è ndu e ndaa-eykokse è se dinte əbnəə se ghele.

Oku_Text: Ghe mbiye nə ghele Juuse se lu nè ǝbchíò ǝbyúméné kante ge: Ke ghan leey ne wel vin ye ke əbnəə se jaŋ le ne ab nè beey ghàà yi yieke ka wánde nwaale?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Anyone who chooses to do the will of God will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own.

Oku_Text: Jesus me suuy se ghene ge: Əbnəə we me dinten se ghen báà se lu wome, ǝb lóò se Feyine we ǝb nè tumen me.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Whoever speaks on their own does so to gain personal glory, but he who seeks the glory of the one who sent him is a man of truth; there is nothing false about him.

Oku_Text: Naa nde we shii ǝ wen luun se ye nɛɛy kenǝǝ ke Feyine kɛŋen lòó ǝb keele ge əbriǝǝ we me dinten se lòò se Feyine moo, me dinte e me ǝbtaawe-a.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?”

Oku_Text: Wel we ǝb suuyen kenǝǝ e wen ǝbtaawe karne ge ghele ye bente yi
English_NIV_Equivalent: dî ne wel we ǝb kerjen ge ghele ye bente ne lu vi we ab tumen yi lu wel ǝbluuy

Oku_Text: Le nè báà fo Moses nchil se ghen-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Jesus said to them, "I did one miracle, and you are all amazed.

Oku_Text: Dî ne wel se ghen leme wiy.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Yet, because Moses gave you circumcision (though actually it did not come from Moses, but from the patriarchs), you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath.

Oku_Text: Lu bek ghe we ghene kenen se yuye me?
English_NIV_Equivalent: Now if a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses may not be broken, why are you angry with me for healing a man’s whole body on the Sabbath?

Oku_Text: Ghel ghi fise ge: Wê ki eyfim e we ǝbler!
English_NIV_Equivalent: Stop judging by mere appearances, but instead judge correctly.”

Oku_Text: Kene nde se yuye we?
English_NIV_Equivalent: At that point some of the people of Jerusalem began to ask, "Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?

Oku_Text: Jesus suuy se ghene ge: Me nè nɛɛy kenəə kemok ǝbchio yum ghen.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Here he is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying a word to him. Have the authorities really concluded that he is the Messiah?

Oku_Text: Moses nè lô fo eysak ge ghen ye neyte ghon eluumene di ne nè báà kɛɛte Moses, nè keste ebaa a ghen se ye neyte.
English_NIV_Equivalent: But we know where this man is from; when the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from.”

Oku_Text: Ghen ko lóó é neyte ghon e kechii eybam.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Then Jesus, still teaching in the temple courts, cried out, "Yes, you know me, and you know where I am from. I am not here on my own authority, but he who sent me is true. You do not know him,

Oku_Text: Ghene lóó é ko neyte wel e kechii eybam ka é jia kol cysake Moses
English_NIV_Equivalent: se lu bek ghe we ghene yafsen se me bek ka me nè chuken wel & kechii eybam əb bonen ǝbghun əbjim?

Oku_Text: Ghàà ghen ye loo sake nsak ghen ye kiynen ken kenəə ɛbey ebey.
English_NIV_Equivalent: At this they tried to seize him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.

Oku_Text: Ghen ye sake e titiy kiynen kenǝǝ ten kejim.
English_NIV_Equivalent: Still, many in the crowd believed in him. They said, "When the Messiah comes, will he perform more signs than this man?”

Oku_Text: Ghele ghe Jolesalem elee me se kante ge: Vin le báà se lu wel we é kerjen se yuy-a?
English_NIV_Equivalent: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about him. Then the chief priests and the Pharisees sent temple guards to arrest him.

Oku_Text: Əb vine ka əb sunnen e ngenge ne wel suuy wiy kenǝǝ kum wen.
